[...spec.baseArgs, modeFlag] : [...spec.baseArgs, String(timeoutSeconds)] 304: const child = spawn(spec.command, args, { 305: cwd: jobDir, 306: stdio: ['pipe', 'pipe', 'pipe'], 307: env: { 308: ...process.env, 309: ...spec.env, 310: EXECUTOR_TIMEOUT_SECONDS: String(timeoutSeconds), 311: SOLVER_MAX_SECONDS: String(Math.max(5, timeoutSeconds - 5)), 312: SOLVER_WORKERS: String(workerCount), 313: }, 314: }) 315: 316: let stdout = '' 317: let stderr = '' 318: let settled = false 319: const finish = (value) => { 320: if (settled) return 321: settled = true 322: resolve(value) 323: } 324: 325: const timer = setTimeout(() => { 326: child.kill('SIGKILL') 327: finish({ ok: false, status: 'timeout', durationMs: timeoutMs, 328: errorDigest: '[MAIN] Timeout from Electron main process' }) 329: }, timeoutMs) 330: 331: child.stdout.on('data', (d) => (stdout += d.toString())) 332: child.stderr.on('data', (d) => (stderr += d.toString())) 333: child.on('error', (err) => { 334: clearTimeout(timer) 335: finish({ ok: false, status: 'crashed', durationMs: 0, 336: errorDigest: `[MAIN] Failed to spawn executor: ${err.message}` }) 337: }) 338: 339: child.on('close', () => { 340: clearTimeout(timer) 341: try { 342: const lastLine = stdout.trim().split('

15: ipcRenderer.on('solver-runtime:notice', listener); 16: return () => ipcRenderer.removeListener('solver-runtime:notice', listener); 17: }, 18: }, 19: secureStore: { 20: saveProvider: (config) => ipcRenderer.invoke('secure-store:save-provider', config), 21: loadProvider: () => ipcRenderer.invoke('secure-store:load-provider'), 22: clearProvider: () => ipcRenderer.invoke('secure-store:clear-provider'), 23: isAvailable: () => ipcRenderer.invoke('secure-store:available'), 24: }, 25: }); 1: // Single source of truth for resolving how the Python solver is launched. 2: // Imported by electron/main.mjs and unit-tested via `node --test`. 3: // 4: // Three modes (outer launcher + inner sandbox together): 5: // - bundled: PyInstaller `code_executor` binary; inner sandbox auto-detected. 6: // - docker: launcher with TT_SANDBOX_MODE=docker (after a docker availability check). 7: // - system: system python3 running python/code_executor.py (dev only). 8: 9: import path from 'node:path' 10: 11: export const RUNTIME_MODES = ['bundled', 'docker', 'system'] 12: export const DEFAULT_RUNTIME_MODE = 'bundled' 13: 14: export function normalizeRuntimeMode(mode) { 15: return RUNTIME_MODES.includes(mode) ? mode : DEFAULT_RUNTIME_MODE 16: } 17: 18: export function bundledBinaryName(platform) { 19: return platform === 'win32' ? 'code_executor.exe' : 'code_executor' 20: } 21: 22: // Directory that holds the bundled binary, depending on dev vs packaged. 23: export function bundledBinaryDir({ isDev, appDir, resourcesPath }) { 24: return isDev ? path.join(appDir, '..', 'python-dist') : path.join(resourcesPath, 'python') 25: } 26: 27: export function bundledBinaryPath({ isDev, appDir, resourcesPath, platform }) { 28: return path.join( 29: bundledBinaryDir({ isDev, appDir, resourcesPath }), 30: bundledBinaryName(platform) 31: ) 32: } 33: 34: // Path to the python source entrypoint for system mode. 35: export function systemEntrypoint({ isDev, appDir, resourcesPath }) { 36: return isDev 37: ? path.join(appDir, '..', 'python', 'code_executor.py') 38: : path.join(resourcesPath, 'python-src', 'code_executor.py') 39: } 40: 41: // Base env applied to every runtime. `isPackaged` drives the production guard 42: // (#8): packaged builds must never allow the unsafe no-sandbox bypass. 43: export function baseRuntimeEnv({ isPackaged }) { 44: const env = { 45: PYTHONUNBUFFERED: '1', 46: PYTHONHASHSEED: '0', 47: } 48: if (isPackaged) { 49: env.TT_PRODUCTION = '1' 50: } 51: return env 52: } 53: 54: // Resolve a concrete spawn spec for the given mode. 55: // `dockerAvailable` is the result of the async probe (see docker-check.mjs). 56: // When docker is requested but unavailable, we fall back to bundled and record 57: // the reason so the UI can surface a notice (#7). 58: export function resolveSpawnSpec({ 59: mode, 60: isDev, 61: isPackaged, 62: appDir, 63: resourcesPath, 64: platform, 65: pythonExecutable = 'python3', 66: dockerAvailable = false, 67: dockerImage = 'tack-timetable-solver:latest', 68: }) { 69: const requested = normalizeRuntimeMode(mode) 70: let effective = requested 71: let fallbackReason = null 72: 73: if (requested === 'docker' && !dockerAvailable) { 74: effective = 'bundled' 75: fallbackReason = 'docker_unavailable' 76: } 77: 78: const env = baseRuntimeEnv({ isPackaged }) 79: 80: if (effective === 'system') { 81: return { 82: requestedMode: requested, 83: mode: 'system', 84: command: pythonExecutable, 85: baseArgs: [systemEntrypoint({ isDev, appDir, resourcesPath })], 86: env, 87: fallbackReason, 88: } 89: } 90: 91: const binary = bundledBinaryPath({ isDev, appDir, resourcesPath, platform }) 92: 93: if (effective === 'docker') { 94: return { 95: requestedMode: requested, 96: mode: 'docker', 97: command: binary, 98: baseArgs: [], 99: env: { ...env, TT_SANDBOX_MODE: 'docker', TT_DOCKER_IMAGE: dockerImage }, 100: fallbackReason, 101: } 102: } 103: 104: return { 105: requestedMode: requested, 106: mode: 'bundled', 107: command: binary, 108: baseArgs: [], 109: env, 110: fallbackReason, 111: } 112: } 113: 114: // Clamp solver worker count into the supported [1, 8] band, defaulting from 115: // cpu count when not provided (#9). 116: export function resolveWorkerCount({ requested, cpuCount, envWorkers }) { 117: const fromEnv = Number(envWorkers) 118: const base = Number(requested) || (Number.isFinite(fromEnv) && fromEnv > 0 ? fromEnv : 0) 119: const cpus = Math.max(1, Number(cpuCount) || 1) 120: const fallback = Math.max(1, cpus - 1) 121: const chosen = base > 0 ? base : fallback 122: return Math.min(8, Math.max(1, Math.floor(chosen))) 123: } 124: 125: // Convert a timeout in ms into whole seconds, never below 1 (#9). 126: export function resolveTimeoutSeconds(timeoutMs) { 127: return Math.max(1, Math.ceil(Number(timeoutMs || 0) / 1000)) 128: } 1: #!/usr/bin/env python3 2: 3: import ast 4: import contextlib 5: import json 6: import py_compile 7: import runpy 8: import shutil 9: import subprocess 10: import sys 11: import tempfile 12: import time 13: import traceback 14: from pathlib import Path 15: from typing import Any 16: 17: # When PyInstaller freezes us into a single binary, sys.frozen=True and 18: # sys.executable points at the binary itself, not a Python interpreter. The 19: # bundled CPython is only reachable by re-exec'ing this binary with a 20: # dedicated mode flag (--run-solver). Sandbox launchers must use this same 21: # binary as the interpreter — never bare `python`, which is unavailable on 22: # user machines that didn't install Python. 23: IS_FROZEN = bool(getattr(sys, "frozen", False)) 24: SELF_EXECUTABLE = sys.executable 25: 26: REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent 27: if str(REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path: 28: sys.path.insert(0, str(REPO_ROOT)) 29: 30: # TIMEOUT_SECONDS mặc định; có thể override qua đối số dòng lệnh argv[1] 31: # hoặc env EXECUTOR_TIMEOUT_SECONDS để đồng bộ với timeoutMs phía Node 32: # (fix bug #6). 33: import os as _os_mod 34: 35: 36: def _default_timeout() -> int: 37: env_value = _os_mod.environ.get("EXECUTOR_TIMEOUT_SECONDS") 38: if env_value: 39: try: 40: v = int(float(env_value)) 41: if v > 0: 42: return v 43: except ValueError: 44: pass 45: if len(sys.argv) >= 2: 46: try: 47: v = int(float(sys.argv[1])) 48: if v > 0: 49: return v 50: except ValueError: 51: pass 52: return 360 53: 54: 55: TIMEOUT_SECONDS = _default_timeout() 56: MAX_STDOUT_LINES = 100 57: FORBIDDEN_AST_NAMES = {"open", "exec", "eval", "__import__", "compile", "input", "breakpoint", "globals", "locals", "vars", "print"} 58: FORBIDDEN_AST_ATTRS = {"__import__", "__builtins__", "__class__", "__bases__", "__subclasses__", "__mro__"} 59: ALLOWED_AST_LOAD_NAMES = {"model", "slots", "data", "assignments", "days", "periods", "periods_by_day", "constraints", "custom_specs", "schedule", "len", "range", "int", "str", "set", "list", "dict", "tuple", "frozenset", "sum", "min", "max", "sorted", "reversed", "round", "divmod", "zip", "map", "filter", "enumerate", "isinstance", "bool", "float", "abs", "all", "any", "ValueError", "NotImplementedError"} 60: 61: 62: def _truncate_output(text: str, max_lines: int = MAX_STDOUT_LINES) -> str: 63: lines = text.splitlines() 64: if len(lines) <= max_lines: 65: return text 66: head = "
".join(lines[:max_lines]) 67: return f"{head}
...[truncated {len(lines) - max_lines} lines]" 68: 69: 70: def _digest_error(stderr: str, max_length: int = 800) -> str: 71: lines = [line.strip() for line in stderr.splitlines() if line.strip()] 72: focused = "
".join(lines[-12:]) 73: return focused[:max_length] 74: 75: 76: def _map_status(raw: str) -> str: 77: status = raw.upper() 78: if status == "OPTIMAL": 79: return "optimal" 80: if status == "FEASIBLE": 81: return "feasible" 82: if status in {"INFEASIBLE", "MODEL_INVALID"}: 83: return "infeasible" 84: if status in {"UNKNOWN"}: 85: return "unknown" 86: return "unknown" 87: 88: 89: def check_syntax_only(code: str) -> dict[str, Any]: 90: with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="tt_syntax_check_") as temp_dir: 91: solver_path = Path(temp_dir) / "solver_syntax_check.py" 92: solver_path.write_text(code, encoding="utf-8") 93: try: 94: py_compile.compile(str(solver_path), doraise=True) 95: return {"ok": True} 96: except (py_compile.PyCompileError, SyntaxError) as exc: 97: return {"ok": False, "error": _digest_error(str(exc))} 98: 99: 100: def check_ast_safety(code: str) -> dict[str, Any]: 101: try: 102: tree = ast.parse(code) 103: except SyntaxError as exc: 104: return {"ok": False, "error": f"SyntaxError: {exc}"} 105: 106: errors: list[str] = [] 107: local_names: set[str] = set() 108: for node in ast.walk(tree): 109: if isinstance(node, ast.Name) and isinstance(node.ctx, (ast.Store, ast.Param)): 110: local_names.add(node.id) 111: elif isinstance(node, (ast.For, ast.AsyncFor)): 112: targets = [node.target] 113: while targets: 114: target = targets.pop() 115: if isinstance(target, ast.Name): 116: local_names.add(target.id) 117: elif isinstance(target, (ast.Tuple, ast.List)): 118: targets.extend(target.elts) 119: 120: for node in ast.walk(tree): 121: if isinstance(node, (ast.Import, ast.ImportFrom)): 122: errors.append(f"Forbidden import at line {node.lineno}") 123: elif isinstance(node, ast.Call): 124: fn = node.func 125: name = getattr(fn, "id", None) or getattr(fn, "attr", None) 126: if name in FORBIDDEN_AST_NAMES: 127: errors.append(f"Forbidden call '{name}' at line {node.lineno}") 128: elif isinstance(node, ast.Attribute): 129: if node.attr in FORBIDDEN_AST_ATTRS: 130: errors.append(f"Forbidden attribute '{node.attr}' at line {node.lineno}") 131: elif isinstance(node, ast.Name) and isinstance(node.ctx, ast.Load): 132: if node.id not in ALLOWED_AST_LOAD_NAMES and node.id not in local_names and not node.id.startswith("_"): 133: errors.append(f"Unknown name '{node.id}' at line {node.lineno}") 134: 135: return {"ok": not errors, "error": "; ".join(errors) if errors else None} 136: 137: 138: def run_user_code(code: str, timeout: int, job_dir: str | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]: 139: started = time.time() 140: with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="timetable_exec_") as temp_dir: 141: workspace = Path(temp_dir) 142: input_src = Path(job_dir) / "input.json" if job_dir else Path.cwd() / was not generated by solver.", 236: "stdout": _truncate_output(stdout), 237: "stderr": _truncate_output(stderr), 238: } 239: 240: try: 241: result_json = result_json.get("schedule", []) if isinstance(result_json, dict) else [] 254: status_raw = result_json.get("status", "unknown") if isinstance(result_json, dict) else "unknown" 255: status = "infeasible" 260: 261: unscheduled: list[str] = [] 262: if isinstance(result_json, dict) and isinstance(result_json.get("assignments"), list): 263: scheduled_ids = { 264: str(entry.get("assignmentId")) 265: for entry in schedule 266: if isinstance(entry, dict) and entry.get("assignmentId") is not None 267: } 268: unscheduled = [ 269: str(item.get("id")) 270: for item in result_json["assignments"] 271: if isinstance(item, dict) and str(item.get("id")) not in scheduled_ids 272: ] 273: 274: ok = return_code == 0 and status in {"optimal", "feasible"} 275: digest_source = stderr if stderr.strip() else stdout 276: 277: artifact_dir = Path.cwd() / ".ai_results" 278: artifact_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) 279: 280: # Cleanup: giữ tối đa 50 file 309: code = sys.stdin.read() 310: if not code.strip(): 311: print( 312: json.dumps( 313: { 314: "phase": "parse", 315: "ok": False, 316: "status": "crashed", 317: "durationMs": 0, 318: "errorDigest": "No code received on stdin.", 319: "stdout": "", 320: "stderr": "", 321: }, 322: ensure_ascii=False, 323: ) 324: ) 325: return 326: 327: try: 328: result = run_user_code(code, TIMEOUT_SECONDS) 329: except Exception: 330: result = { 331: "phase": "run", 332: "ok": False, 333: "status": "crashed", 334: "durationMs": 0, 335: "errorDigest": _digest_error(traceback.format_exc()), 336: "stdout": "", 337: "stderr": "", 338: } 339: print(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False)) 340: 341: 342: def daemon() -> None: 343: """Persistent mode: read newline-delimited JSON jobs from stdin, write results to stdout.""" 344: for raw_line in sys.stdin: 345: raw_line = raw_line.strip() 346: if not raw_line: 347: continue 348: try: 349: job = json.loads(raw_line) 350: except json.JSONDecodeError as exc: 351: print(json.dumps({"phase": "parse", "ok": False, "status": "crashed", 352: "durationMs": 0, "errorDigest": f"JSON decode error: {exc}", 353: "stdout": "", "stderr": ""}, ensure_ascii=False), flush=True) 354: continue 355: 356: code = job.get("code", "") 357: job_type = job.get("type", "execute") 358: timeout = int(job.get("timeoutSeconds", TIMEOUT_SECONDS)) 359: job_dir = job.get("jobDir") or None 360: solver_workers = job.get("solverWorkers") 361: if job_type == "syntax-check": 362: print(json.dumps(check_syntax_only(code), ensure_ascii=False), flush=True) 363: continue 364: if job_type == "ast-check": 365: print(json.dumps(check_ast_safety(code), ensure_ascii=False), flush=True) 366: continue 367: if not code.strip(): 368: print(json.dumps({"phase": "parse", "ok": False, "status": "crashed", 369: "durationMs": 0, "errorDigest": "No code in job.", 370: "stdout": "", "stderr": ""}, ensure_ascii=False), flush=True) 371: continue 372: 373: prev_solver_workers = _os_mod.environ.get("SOLVER_WORKERS") 374: if solver_workers is not None: 375: _os_mod.environ["SOLVER_WORKERS"] = str(int(solver_workers)) 376: try: 377: with contextlib.redirect_stdout(sys.stderr): 378: result = run_user_code(code, timeout, job_dir) 379: except Exception: 380: result = {"phase": "run", "ok": False, "status": "crashed", "durationMs": 0, 381: "errorDigest": _digest_error(traceback.format_exc()), "stdout": "", "stderr": ""} 382: finally: 383: if solver_workers is not None: 384: if prev_solver_workers is None: 385: _os_mod.environ.pop("SOLVER_WORKERS", None) 386: else: 387: _os_mod.environ["SOLVER_WORKERS"] = prev_solver_workers 388: try: 389: output_line = json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False) 390: except (TypeError, ValueError) as exc: 391: result = { 392: "phase": "serialize", 393: "ok": False, 394: "status": "crashed", 395: "durationMs": 0, 396: "errorDigest": f"Result not JSON-serializable: {exc}", 397: "stdout": "", 398: "stderr": "", 399: } 400: output_line = json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False) 401: sys.stdout.write(output_line + "
") 402: sys.stdout.flush() 403: 404: 405: def run_solver_self_exec() -> int: 406: """Self-interpreter mode: ``code_executor --run-solver <file>``. 407: 408: Lets sandbox launchers use this binary as the Python interpreter when no 409: system Python is available (PyInstaller frozen build). 410: """ 411: args = [a for a in sys.argv[1:] if a != "--run-solver"] 412: if not args: 413: print("[code_executor] --run-solver requires a script path", file=sys.stderr) 414: return 2 415: script = Path(args[0]).resolve() 416: if not script.exists(): 417: print(f"[code_executor] script not found: {script}", file=sys.stderr) 418: return 2 419: sys.argv = [str(script), *args[1:]] 420: sys.path.insert(0, str(script.parent)) 421: try: 422: runpy.run_path(str(script), run_name="__main__") 423: return 0 424: except SystemExit as exc: 425: return int(exc.code) if isinstance(exc.code, int) else 0 426: except Exception: 427: traceback.print_exc() 428: return 1 429: 430: 431: if __name__ == "__main__": 432: if "--run-solver" in sys.argv: 433: sys.exit(run_solver_self_exec()) 434: if "--syntax-check" in sys.argv: 435: print(json.dumps(check_syntax_only(sys.stdin.read()), ensure_ascii=False)) 436: elif "--ast-check" in sys.argv: 437: print(json.dumps(check_ast_safety(sys.stdin.read()), ensure_ascii=False)) 438: elif "--daemon" in sys.argv: 439: daemon() 440: else: 441: main() 1: """IR Compiler: Constraint IR → CP-SAT model. 2: 3: This module is Backend 1 of the dual-backend IR architecture. It compiles an IR 4: constraint AST into CP-SAT variables and constraints that ENFORCE the constraint 5: inside the solver (not just verify after the fact). 6: 7: One IR, two backends: 8: - Backend 1 (this file): compile_constraint → CP-SAT model constraints 9: - Backend 2 (ir_eval.py): eval_constraint → Python boolean (verify) 10: 11: Because both backends share the same IR, the enforce and verify semantics are 12: guaranteed to match — no more "checked but not encoded" bugs. 13: 14: Standard reification recipe (copy-paste for implementers): 15: def reify_and(model, lits, name): 16: b = model.NewBoolVar(name) 17: model.AddBoolAnd(lits).OnlyEnforceIf(b) 18: model.AddBoolOr([l.Not() for l in lits]).OnlyEnforceIf(b.Not()) 19: return b 20: 21: def reify_or(model, lits, name): 22: b = model.NewBoolVar(name) 23: model.AddMaxEquality(b, lits) 24: return b 25: 26: def reify_atleast(model, lits, k, name): 27: b = model.NewBoolVar(name); s = sum(lits) 28: model.Add(s >= k).OnlyEnforceIf(b) 29: model.Add(s <= k - 1).OnlyEnforceIf(b.Not()) 30: return b 31: 32: def reify_compare(model, lhs, rhs, op, name): 33: b = model.NewBoolVar(name) 34: pos = {"<=":(lhs<=rhs),"<":(lhs<rhs),">=":(lhs>=rhs),">":(lhs>rhs),"==":(lhs==rhs),"!=":(lhs!=rhs)} 35: neg = {"<=":(lhs>rhs),"<":(lhs>=rhs),">=":(lhs<rhs),">":(lhs<=rhs),"==":(lhs!=rhs),"!=":(lhs==rhs)} 36: model.Add(pos[op]).OnlyEnforceIf(b) 37: model.Add(neg[op]).OnlyEnforceIf(b.Not()) 38: return b 39: 40: Hard/Soft semantics: 41: - severity=="hard": model.Add(compile_expr(expr) == 1) 42: - severity=="soft": viol = compile_expr(expr).Not(); penalty_terms.append((weight, viol)) 43: 44: Edge cases: 45: - Domain rỗng (∃/∀): exists=false, forall=true, count=0 46: - Chỉ số ngoài biên: range clamped at compile time 47: - Cache DerivedVars to avoid blow-up of auxiliary variables 48: """ 49: 50: from __future__ import annotations 51: 52: from typing import Any 53: 54: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 55: # Reification helpers 56: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 57: 58: 59: def reify_and(model: Any, lits: list, name: str) -> Any: 60: """b = AND(lits) with reification.""" 61: b = model.NewBoolVar(name) 62: if lits: 63: model.AddBoolAnd(lits).OnlyEnforceIf(b) 64: model.AddBoolOr([l.Not() for l in lits]).OnlyEnforceIf(b.Not()) 65: else: 66: model.Add(b == 1) # AND of empty set = true 67: return b 68: 69: 70: def reify_or(model: Any, lits: list, name: str) -> Any: 71: """b = OR(lits) with reification.""" 72: b = model.NewBoolVar(name) 73: if lits: 74: model.AddMaxEquality(b, lits) 75: else: 76: model.Add(b == 0) # OR of empty set = false 77: return b 78: 79: 80: def reify_atleast(model: Any, lits: list, k: int, name: str) -> Any: 81: """b = (count(lits) >= k) with reification.""" 82: b = model.NewBoolVar(name) 83: if not lits: 84: # empty domain: atLeast k = (0 >= k) = (k <= 0) 85: model.Add((0 >= k) == 1).OnlyEnforceIf(b) 86: model.Add((0 >= k) == 0).OnlyEnforceIf(b.Not()) 87: return b 88: s = sum(lits) 89: model.Add(s >= k).OnlyEnforceIf(b) 90: model.Add(s <= k - 1).OnlyEnforceIf(b.Not()) 91: return b 92: 93: 94: def reify_atmost(model: Any, lits: list, k: int, name: str) -> Any: 95: """b = (count(lits) <= k) with reification.""" 96: b = model.NewBoolVar(name) 97: if not lits: 98: # empty domain: atMost k = (0 <= k) = always true 99: model.Add(b == 1) 100: return b 101: s = sum(lits) 102: model.Add(s <= k).OnlyEnforceIf(b) 103: model.Add(s >= k + 1).OnlyEnforceIf(b.Not()) 104: return b 105: 106: 107: def reify_exactly(model: Any, lits: list, k: int, name: str) -> Any: 108: """b = (count(lits) == k) with reification.""" 109: b = model.NewBoolVar(name) 110: if not lits: 111: # empty domain: exactly k = (0 == k) 112: model.Add((0 == k) == 1).OnlyEnforceIf(b) 113: model.Add((0 == k) == 0).OnlyEnforceIf(b.Not()) 114: return b 115: s = sum(lits) 116: model.Add(s == k).OnlyEnforceIf(b) 117: model.Add((s <= k - 1) | (s >= k + 1)).OnlyEnforceIf(b.Not()) 118: return b 119: 120: 121: def reify_compare(model: Any, lhs: Any, rhs: Any, op: str, name: str) -> Any: 122: """b = (lhs OP rhs) with reification.""" 123: b = model.NewBoolVar(name) 124: pos = { 125: "<=": lambda l, r: l <= r, 126: "<": lambda l, r: l < r, 127: ">=": lambda l, r: l >= r, 128: ">": lambda l, r: l > r, 129: "==": lambda l, r: l == r, 130: "!=": lambda l, r: l != r, 131: } 132: neg = { 133: "<=": lambda l, r: l > r, 134: "<": lambda l, r: l >= r, 135: ">=": lambda l, r: l < r, 136: ">": lambda l, r: l <= r, 137: "==": lambda l, r: l != r, 138: "!=": lambda l, r: l == r, 139: } 140: model.Add(pos[op](lhs, rhs)).OnlyEnforceIf(b) 141: model.Add(neg[op](lhs, rhs)).OnlyEnforceIf(b.Not()) 142: return b 143: 144: 145: def reify_not(model: Any, lit: Any, name: str) -> Any: 146: """Boolean NOT with a named BoolVar.""" 147: b = model.NewBoolVar(name) 148: model.Add(b + lit == 1) # b = NOT lit 149: return b 150: 151: 152: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 153: # Domain expansion helpers 154: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 155: 156: def expand_domain(domain: Any, env: dict[str, Any]) -> list: 157: """Expand a Domain node into a concrete list of values. 158: 159: Handles: 160: - string literals: "days" → env["days"], etc. 161: - {list: [...]} → literal list 162: - {range: [from, to]} → integer range (from..to inclusive) 163: - {filter: {in: Domain, where: pred}} → filter elements 164: """ 165: if isinstance(domain, str): 166: if domain in ("days", "periods", "classes", "teachers", "subjects"): 167: return env.get(domain, []) 168: return [domain] 169: 170: if isinstance(domain, dict): 171: if "list" in domain: 172: return domain["list"] 173: 174: if "range" in domain: 175: r = domain["range"] 176: if len(r) != 2: 177: return [] 178: start, end = r[0], r[1] 179: # Handle string periods like "P-1" (P = total periods per day) 180: # The IR author uses "$p+1" syntax; we handle simple integer ranges here. 181: try: 182: start_i = int(start) 183: end_i = int(end) 184: return list(range(start_i, end_i + 1)) 185: except (TypeError, ValueError): 186: return [] 187: 188: if "in" in domain: 189: inner = expand_domain(domain["in"], env) 190: # Simple filter: only support {field: value} style for now 191: where = domain.get("where", {}) 192: if where: 193: # Apply filter predicates 194: filtered = [] 195: for item in inner: 196: match = True 197: for k, v in where.items(): 198: if isinstance(item, dict): 199: if item.get(k) != v: 200: match = False 201: break 202: elif str(item) != str(v): 203: match = False 204: break 205: if match: 206: filtered.append(item) 207: return filtered 208: return inner 209: 210: return [] 211: 212: 213: def resolve_var_ref(var_ref: str, env: dict[str, Any]) -> Any: 214: """Resolve a variable reference like "$p" or "$p+1" or "$p-1" against env. 215: 216: var_ref is the raw string (e.g. "$p+1"). env maps var names to their values. 217: Supports simple arithmetic: "$var+N" or "$var-N" where N is a small integer. 218: 219: For label variables (e.g. $d → "mon", $t → "Sơn"), we resolve to the label 220: value as-is. For numeric variables (e.g. $p → 1, $p+1 → 2), we apply the 221: offset. 222: """ 223: if not isinstance(var_ref, str) or not var_ref.startswith("$"): 224: return var_ref 225: 226: rest = var_ref[1:] 227: offset = 0 228: for op in ("+", "-"): 229: if op in rest: 230: var_name, offset_str = rest.split(op, 1) 231: try: 232: offset = int(offset_str) 233: if op == "-": 234: offset = -offset 235: except ValueError: 236: offset = 0 237: break 238: else: 239: var_name = rest 240: offset = 0 241: 242: val = env.get(var_name, var_ref) 243: if val == var_ref: 244: # env didn't have the var 245: return var_ref 246: 247: # Try to apply arithmetic (assumes val is int or int-string). 248: # If val is a label like "mon", int() raises — return val as-is. 249: try: 250: return int(val) + offset 251: except (TypeError, ValueError): 252: return val 253: 254: 255: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 256: # Expression compiler 257: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 258: 259: def compile_expr( 260: model: Any, 261: expr: dict[str, Any], 262: dv: "DerivedVars", # noqa: F821 263: env: dict[str, Any], 264: penalty_terms: list[tuple[int, Any]], 265: name_prefix: str = "", 266: ) -> Any: 267: """Compile a BoolExpr IR node to a CP-SAT BoolVar. 268: 269: Returns a BoolVar b such that b == 1 iff the expression is true. 270: For soft constraints, violations are appended to penalty_terms. 271: 272: Args: 273: model: CpModel 274: expr: BoolExpr dict from IR 275: dv: DerivedVars instance (provides atom BoolVars) 276: env: variable bindings for the current quantifier scope 277: penalty_terms: list to append (weight, violation_var) for soft constraints 278: name_prefix: prefix for new BoolVar names (for debugging) 279: 280: Returns: 281: BoolVar representing the expression 282: """ 283: if not isinstance(expr, dict): 284: # Atom shorthand: plain True/False 285: return dv.const(bool(expr)) 286: 287: # --- Boolean combinators --- 288: if "and" in expr: 289: lits = [ 290: compile_expr(model, sub, dv, env, penalty_terms, f"{name_prefix}_and{i}") 291: for i, sub in enumerate(expr["and"]) 292: ] 293: return reify_and(model, lits, f"{name_prefix}_and") 294: 295: if "or" in expr: 296: lits = [ 297: compile_expr(model, sub, dv, env, penalty_terms, f"{name_prefix}_or{i}") 298: for i, sub in enumerate(expr["or"]) 299: ] 300: return reify_or(model, lits, f"{name_prefix}_or") 301: 302: if "not" in expr: 303: sub = compile_expr(model, expr["not"], dv, env, penalty_terms, f"{name_prefix}_not") 304: return reify_not(model, sub, f"{name_prefix}_not") 305: 306: if "implies" in expr: 307: a, b = expr["implies"][0], expr["implies"][1] 308: a_var = compile_expr(model, a, dv, env, penalty_terms, f"{name_prefix}_imp_a") 309: b_var = compile_expr(model, b, dv, env, penalty_terms, f"{name_prefix}_imp_b") 310: # a → b ≡ NOT a OR b 311: return reify_or(model, [a_var.Not(), b_var], f"{name_prefix}_implies") 312: 313: if "iff" in expr: 314: a, b = expr["iff"][0], expr["iff"][1] 315: a_var = compile_expr(model, a, dv, env, penalty_terms, f"{name_prefix}_iff_a") 316: b_var = compile_expr(model, b, dv, env, penalty_terms, f"{name_prefix}_iff_b") 317: # a ↔ b ≡ (a → b) AND (b → a) 318: imp1 = reify_or(model, [a_var.Not(), b_var], f"{name_prefix}_iff_imp1") 319: imp2 = reify_or(model, [b_var.Not(), a_var], f"{name_prefix}_iff_imp2") 320: return reify_and(model, [imp1, imp2], f"{name_prefix}_iff") 321: 322: # --- Quantifiers --- 323: if "exists" in expr: 324: q = expr["exists"] 325: var = q["var"] 326: domain_vals = expand_domain(q["in"], env) 327: body = q["body"] 328: if not domain_vals: 329: # empty domain: exists = false 330: return dv.const(False) 331: 332: # exists var in D: OR over body(var=val) for each val in D 333: lits = [] 334: for val in domain_vals: 335: sub_env = {**env, var: val} 336: lit = compile_expr(model, body, dv, sub_env, penalty_terms, f"{name_prefix}_ex_{var}_{val}") 337: lits.append(lit) 338: return reify_or(model, lits, f"{name_prefix}_exists_{var}") 339: 340: if "forall" in expr: 341: q = expr["forall"] 342: var = q["var"] 343: domain_vals = expand_domain(q["in"], env) 344: body = q["body"] 345: if not domain_vals: 346: # empty domain: forall = true 347: return dv.const(True) 348: 349: # forall var in D: AND over body(var=val) for each val in D 350: lits = [] 351: for val in domain_vals: 352: sub_env = {**env, var: val} 353: lit = compile_expr(model, body, dv, sub_env, penalty_terms, f"{name_prefix}_fa_{var}_{val}") 354: lits.append(lit) 355: return reify_and(model, lits, f"{name_prefix}_forall_{var}") 356: 357: if "atLeast" in expr: 358: q = expr["atLeast"] 359: k = int(q["k"]) 360: var = q["var"] 361: domain_vals = expand_domain(q["in"], env) 362: body = q["body"] 363: if not domain_vals: 364: # empty domain: count = 0; atLeast k = (0 >= k) = (k <= 0) 365: return dv.const(k <= 0) 366: 367: lits = [] 368: for val in domain_vals: 369: sub_env = {**env, var: val} 370: lit = compile_expr(model, body, dv, sub_env, penalty_terms, f"{name_prefix}_al_{var}_{val}") 371: lits.append(lit) 372: return reify_atleast(model, lits, k, f"{name_prefix}_atLeast_{var}") 373: 374: if "atMost" in expr: 375: q = expr["atMost"] 376: k = int(q["k"]) 377: var = q["var"] 378: domain_vals = expand_domain(q["in"], env) 379: body = q["body"] 380: if not domain_vals: 381: return dv.const(True) # empty domain: atMost k = always true 382: 383: lits = [] 384: for val in domain_vals: 385: sub_env = {**env, var: val} 386: lit = compile_expr(model, body, dv, sub_env, penalty_terms, f"{name_prefix}_am_{var}_{val}") 387: lits.append(lit) 388: return reify_atmost(model, lits, k, f"{name_prefix}_atMost_{var}") 389: 390: if "exactly" in expr: 391: q = expr["exactly"] 392: k = int(q["k"]) 393: var = q["var"] 394: domain_vals = expand_domain(q["in"], env) 395: body = q["body"] 396: if not domain_vals: 397: return dv.const(k == 0) 398: 399: lits = [] 400: for val in domain_vals: 401: sub_env = {**env, var: val} 402: lit = compile_expr(model, body, dv, sub_env, penalty_terms, f"{name_prefix}_ex_{var}_{val}") 403: lits.append(lit) 404: return reify_exactly(model, lits, k, f"{name_prefix}_exactly_{var}") 405: 406: # --- Comparison --- 407: if "compare" in expr: 408: c = expr["compare"] 409: op = c["op"] 410: lhs = compile_int_expr(model, c["lhs"], dv, env, penalty_terms, f"{name_prefix}_cmp_lhs") 411: rhs = compile_int_expr(model, c["rhs"], dv, env, penalty_terms, f"{name_prefix}_cmp_rhs") 412: return reify_compare(model, lhs, rhs, op, f"{name_prefix}_compare") 413: 414: # --- Consecutive (temporal) --- 415: if "consecutive" in expr: 416: q = expr["consecutive"] 417: var = q["var"] 418: length = int(q["length"]) 419: domain_vals = expand_domain(q["in"], env) 420: body = q["body"] 421: 422: if not domain_vals or len(domain_vals) < length: 423: return dv.const(False) 424: 425: # consecutive L: there exists a start position such that 426: # body[$var], body[$var+1], ..., body[$var+L-1] are all true. 427: # We check all valid start positions. 428: window_vars = [] 429: for start_idx in range(len(domain_vals) - length + 1): 430: window_vals = domain_vals[start_idx : start_idx + length] 431: window_lits = [] 432: for i, val in enumerate(window_vals): 433: sub_env = {**env, var: val} 434: lit = compile_expr(model, body, dv, sub_env, penalty_terms, f"{name_prefix}_con_{var}_{val}") 435: window_lits.append(lit) 436: window_b = reify_and(model, window_lits, f"{name_prefix}_con_wnd_{start_idx}") 437: window_vars.append(window_b) 438: return reify_or(model, window_vars, f"{name_prefix}_consecutive") 439: 440: # --- Atoms --- 441: if "teaches" in expr: 442: a = expr["teaches"] 443: teacher = resolve_var_ref(a["teacher"], env) 444: day = resolve_var_ref(a["day"], env) 445: period = resolve_var_ref(a["period"], env) 446: return dv.teacher_busy(str(teacher), str(day), period) 447: 448: if "teachesOnDay" in expr: 449: a = expr["teachesOnDay"] 450: teacher = resolve_var_ref(a["teacher"], env) 451: day = resolve_var_ref(a["day"], env) 452: # teachesOnDay: OR over all periods for that day 453: periods = env.get("periods", []) 454: if not periods: 455: return dv.const(False) 456: lits = [dv.teacher_busy(str(teacher), str(day), p) for p in periods] 457: return reify_or(model, lits, f"{name_prefix}_tod_{teacher}_{day}") 458: 459: if "classSubjectAt" in expr: 460: a = expr["classSubjectAt"] 461: klass = resolve_var_ref(a["class"], env) 462: subject = resolve_var_ref(a["subject"], env) 463: day = resolve_var_ref(a["day"], env) 464: period = resolve_var_ref(a["period"], env) 465: return dv.class_subject_at(str(klass), str(subject), str(day), period) 466: 467: if "classBusy" in expr: 468: a = expr["classBusy"] 469: klass = resolve_var_ref(a["class"], env) 470: day = resolve_var_ref(a["day"], env) 471: period = resolve_var_ref(a["period"], env) 472: return dv.class_busy(str(klass), str(day), period) 473: 474: if "assigned" in expr: 475: a = expr["assigned"] 476: assignment = resolve_var_ref(a["assignment"], env) 477: day = resolve_var_ref(a["day"], env) 478: period = resolve_var_ref(a["period"], env) 479: return dv.assigned(str(assignment), str(day), period) 480: 481: if "const" in expr: 482: return dv.const(bool(expr["const"])) 483: 484: # Unknown node: treat as true (fail-open for soft, fail-closed for hard) 485: # This prevents the solver from crashing on unexpected IR nodes. 486: return dv.const(True) 487: 488: 489: def compile_int_expr( 490: model: Any, 491: int_expr: Any, 492: dv: "DerivedVars", # noqa: F821 493: env: dict[str, Any], 494: penalty_terms: list[tuple[int, Any]], 495: name_prefix: str = "", 496: ) -> Any: 497: """Compile an IntExpr IR node to a CP-SAT IntVar or int. 498: 499: Returns an integer expression suitable for use in compare nodes. 500: """ 501: if isinstance(int_expr, int): 502: return int_expr 503: 504: if isinstance(int_expr, dict): 505: if "count" in int_expr: 506: c = int_expr["count"] 507: var = c["var"] 508: domain_vals = expand_domain(c["in"], env) 509: body = c["body"] 510: if not domain_vals: 511: return 0 512: lits = [] 513: for val in domain_vals: 514: sub_env = {**env, var: val} 515: lit = compile_expr(model, body, dv, sub_env, penalty_terms, f"{name_prefix}_cnt_{var}_{val}") 516: lits.append(lit) 517: s = sum(lits) 518: return s 519: 520: if "sum" in int_expr: 521: terms = [ 522: compile_int_expr(model, t, dv, env, penalty_terms, f"{name_prefix}_sum{i}") 523: for i, t in enumerate(int_expr["sum"]) 524: ] 525: return sum(terms) 526: 527: if "scale" in int_expr: 528: s = int_expr["scale"] 529: factor = int(s["factor"]) 530: inner = compile_int_expr(model, s["of"], dv, env, penalty_terms, f"{name_prefix}_scale") 531: return factor * inner 532: 533: return 0 534: 535: 536: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 537: # Main entry point 538: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 539: 540: def compile_constraint( 541: model: Any, 542: ir: dict[str, Any], 543: dv: "DerivedVars", # noqa: F821 544: env: dict[str, Any], 545: penalty_terms: list[tuple[int, Any]], 546: ) -> None: 547: """Compile a full IR Constraint into CP-SAT constraints. 548: 549: Reads ir["expr"] and ir["severity"] and ir["weight"] and adds the appropriate 550: constraints to model. For soft constraints, appends (weight, violation_var) 551: to penalty_terms. 552: 553: Args: 554: model: CpModel (will be mutated) 555: ir: full IR constraint dict 556: dv: DerivedVars instance 557: env: top-level environment (days, periods, classes, teachers, subjects) 558: penalty_terms: list to append soft violation terms to 559: """ 560: severity = ir.get("severity", "hard") 561: expr = ir.get("expr", {}) 562: weight = int(ir.get("weight", 1) or 1) 563: 564: expr_var = compile_expr(model, expr, dv, env, penalty_terms, f"ir_{ir.get('id', 'x')}") 565: 566: if severity == "hard": 567: model.Add(expr_var == 1) 568: elif severity == "soft": 569: # Soft: minimize violations = maximize expr satisfied 570: # We add (weight, violation_var) where violation_var = NOT expr_var 571: viol = expr_var.Not() 572: penalty_terms.append((weight, viol)) 573: # severity == "info": no constraint added to model 1: """Derived variable helpers for the IR compiler. 2: 3: DerivedVars builds a cache of reified boolean variables (teacher_busy, class_subject_at, etc.) 4: on top of the raw slot variables. These derived booleans are the Atoms of the IR grammar. 5: 6: All derived variables are cached per (key) so that the same atom requested multiple times 7: (e.g. in different parts of a complex expression) returns the SAME BoolVar — critical for 8: correctness when reifying. 9: """ 10: 11: from __future__ import annotations 12: 13: from collections import defaultdict 14: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING 15: 16: if TYPE_CHECKING: 17: from ortools.sat.python import cp_model 18: 19: # type aliases used in docstring / comments 20: BoolVar = "cp_model.BoolVar" 21: IntVar = "cp_model.IntVar" 22: Model = "cp_model.CpModel" 23: SlotKey = tuple[str, str, int | str] # (assignment_id, day, period) 24: 25: 26: class DerivedVars: 27: """Cached reified views of the slot variables. 28: 29: Usage: 30: dv = DerivedVars(model, slots, assignments) 31: b = dv.teacher_busy("Thủy", "mon", 1) # BoolVar: OR of all slots for that teacher 32: """ 33: 34: def __init__( 35: self, 36: model: Model, 37: slots: dict[SlotKey, BoolVar], 38: assignments: list[dict], 39: ): 40: self.model = model 41: self.slots = slots 42: self.assignments = assignments 43: self._cache: dict[tuple, BoolVar] = {} 44: 45: # Indexes for fast lookup without scanning all assignments 46: self.by_teacher: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list) 47: self.by_class: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list) 48: self.by_class_subject: dict[tuple[str, str], list[str]] = defaultdict(list) 49: self.by_assignment: dict[str, dict] = {} 50: 51: for a in assignments: 52: aid = a.get("id") 53: self.by_assignment[aid] = a 54: self.by_teacher[a.get("teacher", "")].append(aid) 55: self.by_class[a.get("class", "")].append(aid) 56: self.by_class_subject[(a.get("class", ""), a.get("subject", ""))].append(aid) 57: 58: # ------------------------------------------------------------------------- 59: # Public derived-boolean builders 60: # ------------------------------------------------------------------------- 61: 62: def teacher_busy(self, teacher: str, day: str, period: int | str) -> BoolVar: 63: """BoolVar: teacher is teaching at (day, period).""" 64: key = ("tb", teacher, day, period) 65: if key in self._cache: 66: return self._cache[key] 67: 68: lits = [ 69: self.slots[(aid, day, period)] 70: for aid in self.by_teacher.get(teacher, []) 71: if (aid, day, period) in self.slots 72: ] 73: b = self.model.NewBoolVar(f"tb_{teacher}_{day}_{period}") 74: if lits: 75: self.model.AddMaxEquality(b, lits) # b = OR(lits) 76: else: 77: self.model.Add(b == 0) # teacher has no assignment at this slot 78: self._cache[key] = b 79: return b 80: 81: def teachesOnDay(self, teacher: str, day: str) -> BoolVar: 82: """BoolVar: teacher teaches at least one period on this day.""" 83: key = ("tod", teacher, day) 84: if key in self._cache: 85: return self._cache[key] 86: 87: # We need OR over all periods. Since periods aren't known here generically, 88: # we use a simple approximation: just check if any slot exists for this teacher+day. 89: # The actual period-by-period check is done in compile_expr when needed. 90: # For efficiency we expose this at the atom level. 91: b = self.model.NewBoolVar(f"tod_{teacher}_{day}") 92: # We'll handle this properly in compile_expr using exists over periods. 93: # For now, mark as always-true placeholder (will be refined by the compiler). 94: self.model.Add(b == 1) 95: self._cache[key] = b 96: return b 97: 98: def class_subject_at( 99: self, klass: str, subject: str, day: str, period: int | str 100: ) -> BoolVar: 101: """BoolVar: klass is having `subject` at (day, period).""" 102: key = ("cs", klass, subject, day, period) 103: if key in self._cache: 104: return self._cache[key] 105: 106: lits = [ 107: self.slots[(aid, day, period)] 108: for aid in self.by_class_subject.get((klass, subject), []) 109: if (aid, day, period) in self.slots 110: ] 111: b = self.model.NewBoolVar(f"cs_{klass}_{subject}_{day}_{period}") 112: if lits: 113: self.model.AddMaxEquality(b, lits) 114: else: 115: self.model.Add(b == 0) 116: self._cache[key] = b 117: return b 118: 119: def class_busy(self, klass: str, day: str, period: int | str) -> BoolVar: 120: """BoolVar: klass has any class at (day, period).""" 121: key = ("cb", klass, day, period) 122: if key in self._cache: 123: return self._cache[key] 124: 125: lits = [ 126: self.slots[(aid, day, period)] 127: for aid in self.by_class.get(klass, []) 128: if (aid, day, period) in self.slots 129: ] 130: b = self.model.NewBoolVar(f"cb_{klass}_{day}_{period}") 131: if lits: 132: self.model.AddMaxEquality(b, lits) 133: else: 134: self.model.Add(b == 0) 135: self._cache[key] = b 136: return b 137: 138: def assigned(self, assignment_id: str, day: str, period: int | str) -> BoolVar: 139: """BoolVar: assignment_id is scheduled at (day, period) — raw slot variable.""" 140: key = ("a", assignment_id, day, period) 141: if key in self._cache: 142: return self._cache[key] 143: var = self.slots.get((assignment_id, day, period)) 144: if var is None: 145: var = self.model.NewBoolVar(f"a_{assignment_id}_{day}_{period}") 146: self.model.Add(var == 0) # not in model → impossible 147: self._cache[key] = var 148: return var 149: 150: def const(self, value: bool) -> BoolVar: 151: """BoolVar: constant true/false.""" 152: key = ("const", value) 153: if key in self._cache: 154: return self._cache[key] 155: b = self.model.NewBoolVar(f"const_{value}") 156: if value: 157: self.model.Add(b == 1) 158: else: 159: self.model.Add(b == 0) 160: self._cache[key] = b 161: return b 162: 163: def clear_cache(self) -> None: 164: """Clear the BoolVar cache. Useful when rebuilding model.""" 165: self._cache.clear() 166: 167: def get_cache_size(self) -> int: 168: """Number of cached derived variables. Useful for diagnostics.""" 169: return len(self._cache) 1: """IR Evaluator: Constraint IR → Python boolean (verify). 2: 3: This module is Backend 2 of the dual-backend IR architecture. It interprets the same 4: IR AST that ir_compiler.py consumes, but evaluates it against a concrete schedule 5: rather than compiling to CP-SAT. Used by the validator to VERIFY that a solved 6: schedule satisfies the constraints. 7: 8: One IR, two backends: 9: - Backend 1 (ir_compiler.py): compile_constraint → CP-SAT model (ENFORCE) 10: - Backend 2 (this file): eval_constraint → Python bool (VERIFY) 11: 12: Because both backends share the same IR, enforce and verify are guaranteed to match. 13: 14: Semantic reference (from Plan.md §3.4): 15: 16: Node | CP-SAT (enforce) | Python (verify) 17: ------------|-------------------------------------|-------------------------------- 18: and[xs] | AddMinEquality(b, reify(xs)) | all() 19: or[xs] | AddMaxEquality(b, reify(xs)) | any() 20: not x | reify(x).Not() | not 21: implies | or[ not a, b ] | (not a) or b 22: iff | and[implies, implies] | a == b 23: exists | OR reify body over domain | any(...) 24: forall | AND reify body over domain | all(...) 25: atLeast k | Add(s>=k)<=>b | count >= k 26: atMost k | Add(s<=k)<=>b | count <= k 27: exactly k | Add(s==k)<=>b | count == k 28: compare | Add(lhs op rhs)<=>b | lhs op rhs 29: consecutive | OR(all body in window) | has consecutive window 30: count | sum(reify(body)) | count 31: """ 32: 33: from __future__ import annotations 34: 35: from typing import Any 36: 37: 38: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 39: # Schedule helpers 40: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 41: 42: def _to_period(value: Any) -> int | None: 43: """Convert a period value to int, or None if it can't be converted.""" 44: try: 45: return int(value) 46: except (TypeError, ValueError): 47: return None 48: 49: 50: def _get_entries( 51: schedule: list[dict[str, Any]], 52: *, 53: teacher: str | None = None, 54: klass: str | None = None, 55: subject: str | None = None, 56: assignment_id: str | None = None, 57: day: str | None = None, 58: period: int | None = None, 59: ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]: 60: """Filter schedule entries by the given criteria (all filters are ANDed).""" 61: result = schedule 62: if teacher is not None: 63: result = [e for e in result if e.get("teacher") == teacher] 64: if klass is not None: 65: result = [e for e in result if e.get("class") == klass] 66: if subject is not None: 67: result = [e for e in result if e.get("subject") == subject] 68: if assignment_id is not None: 69: result = [e for e in result if e.get("assignmentId") == assignment_id or e.get("assignment_id") == assignment_id] 70: if day is not None: 71: result = [e for e in result if e.get("day") == day] 72: if period is not None: 73: result = [e for e in result if _to_period(e.get("period")) == period] 74: return result 75: 76: 77: def _teacher_teaches_at(teacher: str, day: str, period: int, schedule: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> bool: 78: return any( 79: e.get("teacher") == teacher 80: and e.get("day") == day 81: and _to_period(e.get("period")) == period 82: for e in schedule 83: ) 84: 85: 86: def _teacher_teaches_on_day(teacher: str, day: str, schedule: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> bool: 87: return any(e.get("teacher") == teacher and e.get("day") == day for e in schedule) 88: 89: 90: def _class_subject_at(klass: str, subject: str, day: str, period: int, schedule: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> bool: 91: return any( 92: e.get("class") == klass 93: and e.get("subject") == subject 94: and e.get("day") == day 95: and _to_period(e.get("period")) == period 96: for e in schedule 97: ) 98: 99: 100: def _class_busy_at(klass: str, day: str, period: int, schedule: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> bool: 101: return any( 102: e.get("class") == klass 103: and e.get("day") == day 104: and _to_period(e.get("period")) == period 105: for e in schedule 106: ) 107: 108: 109: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 110: # Domain expansion 111: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 112: 113: def expand_domain(domain: Any, env: dict[str, Any]) -> list: 114: """Expand a Domain IR node to a concrete list of values.""" 115: if isinstance(domain, str): 116: if domain in ("days", "periods", "classes", "teachers", "subjects"): 117: return env.get(domain, []) 118: return [domain] 119: 120: if isinstance(domain, dict): 121: if "list" in domain: 122: return domain["list"] 123: 124: if "range" in domain: 125: r = domain["range"] 126: if len(r) != 2: 127: return [] 128: try: 129: return list(range(int(r[0]), int(r[1]) + 1)) 130: except (TypeError, ValueError): 131: return [] 132: 133: if "in" in domain: 134: inner = expand_domain(domain["in"], env) 135: where = domain.get("where", {}) 136: if where: 137: filtered = [] 138: for item in inner: 139: match = True 140: for k, v in where.items(): 141: if isinstance(item, dict): 142: if item.get(k) != v: 143: match = False 144: break 145: elif str(item) != str(v): 146: match = False 147: break 148: if match: 149: filtered.append(item) 150: return filtered 151: return inner 152: 153: return [] 154: 155: 156: def resolve_var_ref(var_ref: str, env: dict[str, Any]) -> Any: 157: """Resolve $var or $var+N / $var-N against env. 158: 159: For label variables (e.g. $d → "mon", $t → "Sơn"), we resolve to the label 160: value as-is. For numeric variables (e.g. $p → 1, $p+1 → 2), we apply the 161: offset. 162: """ 163: if not isinstance(var_ref, str) or not var_ref.startswith("$"): 164: return var_ref 165: rest = var_ref[1:] 166: offset = 0 167: for op in ("+", "-"): 168: if op in rest: 169: var_name, offset_str = rest.split(op, 1) 170: try: 171: offset = int(offset_str) 172: if op == "-": 173: offset = -offset 174: except ValueError: 175: offset = 0 176: break 177: else: 178: var_name = rest 179: offset = 0 180: val = env.get(var_name, var_ref) 181: if val == var_ref: 182: return var_ref 183: # Try to apply arithmetic (assumes val is int or int-string). 184: # If val is a label like "mon", int() raises — return val as-is. 185: try: 186: return int(val) + offset 187: except (TypeError, ValueError): 188: return val 189: 190: 191: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 192: # Boolean expression evaluator 193: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 194: 195: def eval_expr( 196: expr: dict[str, Any], 197: schedule: list[dict[str, Any]], 198: dv: "EvalDerivedVars", 199: env: dict[str, Any], 200: ) -> bool: 201: """Evaluate a BoolExpr against a concrete schedule. 202: 203: Returns True if the expression is satisfied, False otherwise. 204: 205: Args: 206: expr: BoolExpr dict from IR 207: schedule: list of schedule entries (from result["schedule"]) 208: dv: EvalDerivedVars instance (provides atom evaluation) 209: env: variable bindings for quantifier scope 210: """ 211: if not isinstance(expr, dict): 212: return bool(expr) 213: 214: # --- Boolean combinators --- 215: if "and" in expr: 216: return all(eval_expr(sub, schedule, dv, env) for sub in expr["and"]) 217: 218: if "or" in expr: 219: return any(eval_expr(sub, schedule, dv, env) for sub in expr["or"]) 220: 221: if "not" in expr: 222: return not eval_expr(expr["not"], schedule, dv, env) 223: 224: if "implies" in expr: 225: a, b = expr["implies"][0], expr["implies"][1] 226: return (not eval_expr(a, schedule, dv, env)) or eval_expr(b, schedule, dv, env) 227: 228: if "iff" in expr: 229: a, b = expr["iff"][0], expr["iff"][1] 230: return eval_expr(a, schedule, dv, env) == eval_expr(b, schedule, dv, env) 231: 232: # --- Quantifiers --- 233: if "exists" in expr: 234: q = expr["exists"] 235: var = q["var"] 236: domain_vals = expand_domain(q["in"], env) 237: if not domain_vals: 238: return False # exists over empty domain = false 239: body = q["body"] 240: return any( 241: eval_expr(body, schedule, dv, {**env, var: val}) 242: for val in domain_vals 243: ) 244: 245: if "forall" in expr: 246: q = expr["forall"] 247: var = q["var"] 248: domain_vals = expand_domain(q["in"], env) 249: if not domain_vals: 250: return True # forall over empty domain = true 251: body = q["body"] 252: return all( 253: eval_expr(body, schedule, dv, {**env, var: val}) 254: for val in domain_vals 255: ) 256: 257: if "atLeast" in expr: 258: q = expr["atLeast"] 259: k = int(q["k"]) 260: var = q["var"] 261: domain_vals = expand_domain(q["in"], env) 262: if not domain_vals: 263: return k <= 0 264: body = q["body"] 265: count = sum( 266: eval_expr(body, schedule, dv, {**env, var: val}) 267: for val in domain_vals 268: ) 269: return count >= k 270: 271: if "atMost" in expr: 272: q = expr["atMost"] 273: k = int(q["k"]) 274: var = q["var"] 275: domain_vals = expand_domain(q["in"], env) 276: if not domain_vals: 277: return True # empty domain: atMost k = always true 278: body = q["body"] 279: count = sum( 280: eval_expr(body, schedule, dv, {**env, var: val}) 281: for val in domain_vals 282: ) 283: return count <= k 284: 285: if "exactly" in expr: 286: q = expr["exactly"] 287: k = int(q["k"]) 288: var = q["var"] 289: domain_vals = expand_domain(q["in"], env) 290: if not domain_vals: 291: return k == 0 292: body = q["body"] 293: count = sum( 294: eval_expr(body, schedule, dv, {**env, var: val}) 295: for val in domain_vals 296: ) 297: return count == k 298: 299: # --- Comparison --- 300: if "compare" in expr: 301: c = expr["compare"] 302: op = c["op"] 303: lhs = eval_int_expr(c["lhs"], schedule, dv, env) 304: rhs = eval_int_expr(c["rhs"], schedule, dv, env) 305: ops = { 306: "<=": lambda l, r: l <= r, 307: "<": lambda l, r: l < r, 308: ">=": lambda l, r: l >= r, 309: ">": lambda l, r: l > r, 310: "==": lambda l, r: l == r, 311: "!=": lambda l, r: l != r, 312: } 313: return ops[op](lhs, rhs) 314: 315: # --- Consecutive (temporal) --- 316: if "consecutive" in expr: 317: q = expr["consecutive"] 318: var = q["var"] 319: length = int(q["length"]) 320: domain_vals = expand_domain(q["in"], env) 321: body = q["body"] 322: if not domain_vals or len(domain_vals) < length: 323: return False 324: # Check each window of size `length` 325: for start_idx in range(len(domain_vals) - length + 1): 326: window_vals = domain_vals[start_idx : start_idx + length] 327: if all( 328: eval_expr(body, schedule, dv, {**env, var: val}) 329: for val in window_vals 330: ): 331: return True 332: return False 333: 334: # --- Atoms --- 335: if "teaches" in expr: 336: a = expr["teaches"] 337: teacher = resolve_var_ref(a["teacher"], env) 338: day = resolve_var_ref(a["day"], env) 339: period = resolve_var_ref(a["period"], env) 340: return _teacher_teaches_at(str(teacher), str(day), int(period), schedule) 341: 342: if "teachesOnDay" in expr: 343: a = expr["teachesOnDay"] 344: teacher = resolve_var_ref(a["teacher"], env) 345: day = resolve_var_ref(a["day"], env) 346: return _teacher_teaches_on_day(str(teacher), str(day), schedule) 347: 348: if "classSubjectAt" in expr: 349: a = expr["classSubjectAt"] 350: klass = resolve_var_ref(a["class"], env) 351: subject = resolve_var_ref(a["subject"], env) 352: day = resolve_var_ref(a["day"], env) 353: period = resolve_var_ref(a["period"], env) 354: return _class_subject_at(str(klass), str(subject), str(day), int(period), schedule) 355: 356: if "classBusy" in expr: 357: a = expr["classBusy"] 358: klass = resolve_var_ref(a["class"], env) 359: day = resolve_var_ref(a["day"], env) 360: period = resolve_var_ref(a["period"], env) 361: return _class_busy_at(str(klass), str(day), int(period), schedule) 362: 363: if "assigned" in expr: 364: a = expr["assigned"] 365: assignment = resolve_var_ref(a["assignment"], env) 366: day = resolve_var_ref(a["day"], env) 367: period = resolve_var_ref(a["period"], env) 368: return any( 369: (e.get("assignmentId") == str(assignment) or e.get("assignment_id") == str(assignment)) 370: and e.get("day") == str(day) 371: and _to_period(e.get("period")) == int(period) 372: for e in schedule 373: ) 374: 375: if "const" in expr: 376: return bool(expr["const"]) 377: 378: # Unknown node: fail-open for safety (don't reject schedule for unknown nodes) 379: return True 380: 381: 382: def eval_int_expr( 383: int_expr: Any, 384: schedule: list[dict[str, Any]], 385: dv: "EvalDerivedVars", 386: env: dict[str, Any], 387: ) -> int: 388: """Evaluate an IntExpr to an integer.""" 389: if isinstance(int_expr, int): 390: return int_expr 391: 392: if isinstance(int_expr, dict): 393: if "count" in int_expr: 394: c = int_expr["count"] 395: var = c["var"] 396: domain_vals = expand_domain(c["in"], env) 397: body = c["body"] 398: if not domain_vals: 399: return 0 400: return sum( 401: eval_expr(body, schedule, dv, {**env, var: val}) 402: for val in domain_vals 403: ) 404: 405: if "sum" in int_expr: 406: return sum( 407: eval_int_expr(t, schedule, dv, env) 408: for t in int_expr["sum"] 409: ) 410: 411: if "scale" in int_expr: 412: s = int_expr["scale"] 413: return int(s["factor"]) * eval_int_expr(s["of"], schedule, dv, env) 414: 415: return 0 416: 417: 418: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 419: # Derived vars for evaluator 420: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 421: 422: class EvalDerivedVars: 423: """Lightweight atom evaluation for the verifier. 424: 425: Unlike DerivedVars (which creates CP-SAT BoolVars), EvalDerivedVars 426: evaluates atoms directly against the schedule at evaluation time. 427: """ 428: 429: def __init__(self, schedule: list[dict[str, Any]], assignments: list[dict[str, Any]]): 430: self.schedule = schedule 431: self.assignments = assignments 432: 433: def teacher_busy(self, teacher: str, day: str, period: int) -> bool: 434: return _teacher_teaches_at(teacher, day, period, self.schedule) 435: 436: def teachesOnDay(self, teacher: str, day: str) -> bool: 437: return _teacher_teaches_on_day(teacher, day, self.schedule) 438: 439: def class_subject_at(self, klass: str, subject: str, day: str, period: int) -> bool: 440: return _class_subject_at(klass, subject, day, period, self.schedule) 441: 442: def class_busy(self, klass: str, day: str, period: int) -> bool: 443: return _class_busy_at(klass, day, period, self.schedule) 444: 445: def assigned(self, assignment_id: str, day: str, period: int) -> bool: 446: return any( 447: (e.get("assignmentId") == assignment_id or e.get("assignment_id") == assignment_id) 448: and e.get("day") == day 449: and _to_period(e.get("period")) == period 450: for e in self.schedule 451: ) 452: 453: 454: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 455: # Main entry point 456: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 457: 458: def eval_constraint( 459: ir: dict[str, Any], 460: schedule: list[dict[str, Any]], 461: assignments: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None, 462: env: dict[str, Any] | None = None, 463: ) -> bool: 464: """Evaluate a full IR constraint against a concrete schedule. 465: 466: Args: 467: ir: full IR constraint dict (must have id, severity, expr) 468: schedule: list of schedule entries from solver result 469: assignments: optional assignments list (for building env) 470: env: optional top-level environment override 471: 472: Returns: 473: True if the constraint is satisfied, False otherwise. 474: """ 475: assignments = assignments or [] 476: schedule = schedule or [] 477: 478: # Build environment from schedule metadata 479: days = sorted(set(e.get("day") for e in schedule if e.get("day"))) 480: periods = sorted(set(_to_period(e.get("period")) for e in schedule if e.get("period") is not None)) 481: classes = sorted(set(e.get("class") for e in schedule if e.get("class"))) 482: teachers = sorted(set(e.get("teacher") for e in schedule if e.get("teacher"))) 483: subjects = sorted(set(e.get("subject") for e in schedule if e.get("subject"))) 484: 485: if env is None: 486: env = { 487: "days": days, 488: "periods": periods, 489: "classes": classes, 490: "teachers": teachers, 491: "subjects": subjects, 492: } 493: 494: dv = EvalDerivedVars(schedule, assignments) 495: severity = ir.get("severity", "hard") 496: 497: if severity == "info": 498: return True # info constraints are never enforced 499: 500: ok = eval_expr(ir.get("expr", {}), schedule, dv, env) 501: return ok 502: 503: 504: def eval_constraints( 505: irs: list[dict[str, Any]], 506: schedule: list[dict[str, Any]], 507: assignments: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None, 508: ) -> dict[str, bool]: 509: """Evaluate multiple IR constraints. Returns {id: ok}.""" 510: results = {} 511: for ir in irs: 512: cid = ir.get("id", "<unknown>") 513: results[cid] = eval_constraint(ir, schedule, assignments) 514: return results 1: """JSON Schema definitions and validators for the Constraint IR. 2: 3: This module defines the canonical grammar of the Constraint IR (Intermediate Representation) 4: and provides validation against that grammar. Every constraint that flows through the system 5: must conform to this schema before it is compiled or evaluated. 6: 7: Grammar (informal BNF): 8: Constraint := { id, severity:"hard"|"soft", weight?:number, 9: original:string, explain:string, expr:BoolExpr } 10: 11: BoolExpr := 12: | Atom 13: | { "and": [BoolExpr, ...] } 14: | { "or": [BoolExpr, ...] } 15: | { "not": BoolExpr } 16: | { "implies": [BoolExpr, BoolExpr] } 17: | { "iff": [BoolExpr, BoolExpr] } 18: | { "exists": { var, in:Domain, body:BoolExpr } } 19: | { "forall": { var, in:Domain, body:BoolExpr } } 20: | { "atLeast": { k:int, var, in:Domain, body:BoolExpr } } 21: | { "atMost": { k:int, var, in:Domain, body:BoolExpr } } 22: | { "exactly": { k:int, var, in:Domain, body:BoolExpr } } 23: | { "compare": { op:"<="|"<"|"=="|"!="|">="|">", lhs:IntExpr, rhs:IntExpr } } 24: | { "consecutive": { var, in:Domain, length:int, body:BoolExpr } } 25: 26: IntExpr := 27: | int 28: | { "count": { var, in:Domain, body:BoolExpr } } 29: | { "sum": [IntExpr, ...] } 30: | { "scale": { factor:int, of:IntExpr } } 31: 32: Atom := # must be reifiable to BoolVar 33: | { "teaches": { teacher, day, period } } 34: | { "teachesOnDay": { teacher, day } } 35: | { "classSubjectAt": { class, subject, day, period } } 36: | { "classBusy": { class, day, period } } 37: | { "assigned": { assignment, day, period } } 38: | { "const": true|false } 39: 40: Domain := 41: | "days" | "periods" | "classes" | "teachers" | "subjects" 42: | { "list": [ ... ] } 43: | { "range": [from, to] } 44: | { "filter": { in:Domain, where:<vị từ đơn giản> } } 45: 46: Reference variables in quantifiers with "$<var>" syntax, e.g. "$p+1". 47: """ 48: 49: from __future__ import annotations 50: 51: import json 52: import re 53: from typing import Any 54: 55: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 56: # JSON Schema (string form — validated with jsonschema library at runtime) 57: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 58: 59: IR_CONSTRAINT_SCHEMA = { 60: "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#", 61: "title": "ConstraintIR", 62: "description": "Canonical constraint representation — one IR, two backends: CP-SAT enforce + Python verify.", 63: "type": "object", 64: "required": ["id", "severity", "original", "expr"], 65: "additionalProperties": True, 66: "definitions": { 67: "Domain": { 68: "anyOf": [ 69: {"type": "string", "enum": ["days", "periods", "classes", "teachers", "subjects"]}, 70: { 71: "type": "object", 72: "required": ["list"], 73: "additionalProperties": False, 74: "properties": { 75: "list": { 76: "type": "array", 77: "items": {"type": ["string", "number"]}, 78: } 79: }, 80: }, 81: { 82: "type": "object", 83: "required": ["range"], 84: "additionalProperties": False, 85: "properties": { 86: "range": { 87: "type": "array", 88: "minItems": 2, 89: "maxItems": 2, 90: "items": {"type": ["string", "number"]}, 91: } 92: }, 93: }, 94: { 95: "type": "object", 96: "required": ["in"], 97: "additionalProperties": False, 98: "properties": { 99: "in": {"$ref": "#/definitions/Domain"}, 100: "where": {"type": "object"}, 101: }, 102: }, 103: ] 104: }, 105: "Atom": { 106: "type": "object", 107: "description": "Base propositions — must be reifiable to a BoolVar.", 108: "oneOf": [ 109: { 110: "day"], 134: "additionalProperties": False, 135: "properties": { 136: "teacher": {"type": "string"}, 137: "day": {"type": "string"}, 138: }, 139: } 140: }, 141: }, 142: { 143: "type": "object", 144: "const": {"type": "boolean"}, 200: }, 201: }, 202: ], 203: }, 204: "IntExpr": { 205: "anyOf": [ 206: {"type": "integer"}, 207: { 208: "type": "object", 209: "required": ["count"], 210: 227: "additionalProperties": False, 228: "properties": { 229: "sum": { 230: "type": "array", 231: "items": {"$ref": "#/definitions/IntExpr"}, 232: } 233: }, 234: }, 235: { 236: "type": "object", 237: "properties": { 245: "factor": {"type": "integer"}, 246: "of": {"$ref": "#/definitions/IntExpr"}, 247: }, 248: } 249: }, 250: }, 251: ] 252: }, 253: "BoolExpr": { 254: "anyOf": [ 255: {"$ref": {"$ref": "#/definitions/BoolExpr"}, 284: }, 285: }, 286: { 287: "type": "object", 288: "required": ["implies"], 289: "additionalProperties": False, 290: "properties": { 291: "implies": { 292: "type": 376: "in": {"$ref": "#/definitions/Domain"}, 377: "body": {"$ref": "#/definitions/BoolExpr"}, 378: }, 379: } 380: }, 381: }, 382: { 383: "type": "object", 384: "required": ["exactly"], 385: "additionalProperties": False, 386: "properties": { 387: "exactly": { 388: "type": "object", 389: "required": ["k", "var", "in", "body"], 390: "additionalProperties": False, 391: "properties": { 392:
