"string"}, 444: "expr": {"$ref": "#/definitions/BoolExpr"}, 445: }, 446: } 447: 448: # Lazy-import jsonschema only when validate() is called 449: _jsonschema = None 450: 451: 452: def _get_jsonschema(): 453: global _jsonschema 454: if _jsonschema is None: 455: try: 456: import jsonschema 457: _jsonschema = jsonschema 458: except ImportError: 459: return None 460: return _jsonschema 461: 462: 463: class ValidationError(ValueError): 464: """Raised when an IR node fails schema validation. 465: 466: Attributes: 467: path: JSON pointer path to the failing node. 468: message: Human-readable error description. 469: node: The invalid node itself. 470: """ 471: 472: def __init__(self, message: str, path: str = "", node: Any = None): 473: super().__init__(f"{'[ ' + path + ' ] ' if path else ''}{message}") 474: self.path = path 475: self.node = node 476: 477: 478: def validate_constraint(ir: dict[str, Any]) -> list[ValidationError]: 479: """Validate a single IR constraint against the schema. 480: 481: Returns a list of ValidationErrors (empty = valid). 482: Does NOT raise — collects all errors so the caller can report them all. 483: """ 484: jsonschema = _get_jsonschema() 485: if jsonschema is None: 486: return [] # jsonschema not installed; skip validation 487: 488: validator = jsonschema.Draft7Validator(IR_CONSTRAINT_SCHEMA) 489: errors = [] 490: for err in validator.iter_errors(ir): 491: path = "/".join(str(p) for p in err.path) if err.path else "" 492: errors.append(ValidationError(err.message, path, err.instance)) 493: return errors 494: 495: 496: def validate_constraints(irs: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> dict[str, list[ValidationError]]: 497: """Validate a list of IR constraints. Returns {constraint_id: [errors]}. """ 498: results: dict[str, list[ValidationError]] = {} 499: for ir in irs: 500: cid = ir.get("id", "<unknown>") 501: results[cid] = validate_constraint(ir) 502: return results 503: 504: 505: def is_valid_constraint(ir: dict[str, Any]) -> bool: 506: """Return True iff the constraint passes schema validation.""" 507: return len(validate_constraint(ir)) == 0 508: 509: 510: def check_hard_has_expr(spec: dict[str, Any]) -> ValidationError | None: 511: """Phase 0 hardenting: a hard constraint MUST have an expr field 512: (either IR expr OR pythonPredicate, but not bare custom_dsl with no mechanism).""" 513: if spec.get("severity") != "hard": 514: return None 515: kind = spec.get("kind", "") 516: 517: # Has IR expr 518: if "expr" in spec and isinstance(spec["expr"], dict): 519: return None 520: 521: # Has pythonPredicate (escape hatch — allowed) 522: python_pred = ( 523: spec.get("pythonPredicate") 524: or (spec.get("params") or {}).get("pythonPredicate") 525: ) 526: if python_pred: 527: return None 528: 529: # Has IR-based kind that we know how to compile 530: # (these kinds have known encodings in the skeleton) 531: from .macros import KNOWN_ENCODABLE_KINDS 532: if kind in KNOWN_ENCODABLE_KINDS: 533: return None 534: 535: return ValidationError( 536: f"Hard constraint '{spec.get('id', '')}' (kind={kind}) has no expr, " 537: f"no pythonPredicate, and is not in the known encodable kinds list. " 538: f"It will be verify-only (not enforced in solver). " 539: f"Add an 'expr' IR field or a 'pythonPredicate'.", 540: path=f"/constraints/{spec.get('id', '')}", 541: node=spec, 542: ) 1: """Macro expansion: old constraint kinds → IR. 2: 3: This module provides the one-way mapping from the old registry's ~25 constraint kinds 4: to the new IR. Each kind is a "macro" that expands to one or more IR BoolExpr nodes. 5: 6: By centralizing this mapping here (in macros.py), we ensure: 7: 1. The old registry still works (backward compatibility — Phase 2) 8: 2. Every kind has exactly one canonical IR representation 9: 3. Adding a new constraint kind = adding one macro + golden test (no 4-place edits) 10: 11: The macro expansion is done BEFORE the IR is passed to either backend, so 12: both compile_constraint (CP-SAT) and eval_constraint (Python) see the same IR. 13: 14: Usage: 15: from ir_schema import validate_constraint 16: from macros import expand_to_ir 17: 18: irs = [] 19: for spec in constraint_specs: 20: for ir_node in expand_to_ir(spec): 21: errors = validate_constraint(ir_node) 22: if errors: 23: raise ValueError(f"Invalid IR for {spec['id']}: {errors}") 24: irs.append(ir_node) 25: """ 26: 27: from __future__ import annotations 28: 29: from typing import Any 30: 31: 32: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 33: # Registry of known encodable kinds (kinds with known IR encodings) 34: # These kinds are NOT custom_dsl and have canonical translations 35: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 36: 37: KNOWN_ENCODABLE_KINDS: frozenset[str] = frozenset([ 38: # Teacher constraints 39: "teacher_block_day", 40: "teacher_block_period", 41: "teacher_block_slot", 42: "teacher_max_per_day", 43: "teacher_max_consecutive", 44: "teacher_max_working_days", 45: "teacher_min_per_day", 46: "teacher_no_gaps", 47: "teacher_allowed_days", 48: "teacher_allowed_periods", 49: "teacher_min_working_days", 50: "teacher_max_gaps", 51: "teacher_min_consecutive", 52: "teacher_balanced_load", 53: "teacher_max_subjects_per_day", 54: "teacher_max_consecutive_days", 55: "teacher_preferred_periods", 56: "teacher_max_classes_per_day", 57: "teacher_pair_not_same_slot", 58: "teacher_homeroom_first_period", 59: # Subject constraints 60: "subject_pin_period", 61: "subject_preferred_periods", 62: "subject_not_last_period", 63: "subject_consecutive", 64: "subject_max_consecutive", 65: "subject_allowed_days", 66: "subject_min_gap_days", 67: "subject_daily_max_periods", 68: "subject_block_period", 69: "subject_block_days", 70: "subject_not_consecutive", 71: "subject_min_days", 72: "subject_spread_evenly", 73: "subject_order_before", 74: "subject_not_after_subject", 75: # Class constraints 76: "class_block_day", 77: "class_block_period", 78: "class_block_slot", 79: "class_max_per_day", 80: "class_min_per_day", 81: "class_no_gaps", 82: "class_no_double_subject_day", 83: "class_subjects_not_same_day", 84: "class_fixed_period", 85: "class_allowed_days", 86: "class_allowed_periods", 87: "class_max_consecutive", 88: "class_max_subjects_per_day", 89: "class_balanced_load", 90: "class_subjects_same_day", 91: "class_min_working_days", 92: "class_max_heavy_subjects_per_day", 93: "class_max_heavy_subjects_per_session", 94: "class_first_period_required", 95: # Global / special 96: "subject_flag_ceremony_slot", 97: "global_teacher_utilization_balance", 98: "assignment_pin_slot", 99: "assignment_block_slot", 100: "assignment_allowed_slots", 101: "assignment_spread_days", 102: "weekly_periods_exact", 103: "assignment_consecutive", 104: "assignment_max_per_day", 105: "assignment_same_day", 106: "assignment_not_same_day", 107: "if_then", 108: "pair_not_same_slot", 109: "pair_same_slot", 110: "mutual_exclusion", 111: "session_limit", 112: "subject_group", 113: "subject_group_daily_limit", 114: "subject_session_max_periods", 115: ]) 116: 117: 118: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 119: # Macro expanders 120: # ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 121: 122: def _to_ir( 123: spec: dict[str, Any], 124: expr: dict[str, Any], 125: ) -> dict[str, Any]: 126: """Wrap an expr in the standard IR envelope, copying metadata from spec.""" 127: return { 128: "id": spec.get("id", "unknown"), 129: "severity": spec.get("severity", "hard"), 130: "weight": spec.get("weight", 1), 131: "original": spec.get("original", ""), 132: "explain": spec.get("explain", spec.get("original", "")), 133: "expr": expr, 134: } 135: 136: 137: def expand_to_ir(spec: dict[str, Any]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]: 138: """Expand a legacy constraint spec into one or more IR constraints. 139: 140: Returns a list because some kinds (e.g. teacher_allowed_days = block non-listed days) 141: produce multiple IR constraints (one per excluded day). 142: 143: For kinds not in KNOWN_ENCODABLE_KINDS, returns the spec unchanged 144: (assumes it already has an 'expr' field). 145: """ 146: kind = spec.get("kind", "") 147: params = spec.get("params", {}) 148: severity = spec.get("severity", "hard") 149: 150: # ---- Helper: standard "no-op IR" for kinds whose semantics are 151: # already enforced by the base skeleton (model.Add(...)) and don't 152: # need a separate IR expression. Returning an empty list means 153: # "no IR, fall back to the skeleton branch". The skeleton handles 154: # these natively — we don't want to double-encode them. 155: 156: # ---- Teacher constraints ---- 157: 158: if kind == "teacher_block_day": 159: teacher = params.get("teacher", "") 160: day = params.get("day", "") 161: ir = _to_ir(spec, { 162: "not": { 163: "teachesOnDay": {"teacher": teacher, "day": day} 164: } 165: }) 166: return [ir] 167: 168: if kind == "teacher_block_period": 169: teacher = params.get("teacher", "") 170: period = int(params.get("period", -1)) 171: # This is hard because teacher can't teach at this period on ANY day 172: # We encode as: for all days, NOT teaches(teacher, day, period) 173: ir = _to_ir(spec, { 174: "forall": { 175: "var": "d", 176: "in": "days", 177: "body": { 178: "not": { 179: "teaches": {"teacher": teacher, "day": "$d", "period": period} 180: } 181: } 182: } 183: }) 184: return [ir] 185: 186: if kind == "teacher_block_slot": 187: teacher = params.get("teacher", "") 188: day = params.get("day", "") 189: period = int(params.get("period", -1)) 190: ir = _to_ir(spec, { 191: "not": { 192: "teaches": {"teacher": teacher, "day": day, "period": period} 193: } 194: }) 195: return [ir] 196: 197: if kind == "teacher_max_per_day": 198: teacher = params.get("teacher", "") 199: max_per_day = int(params.get("maxPerDay", 999)) 200: # forall days: atMost(max_per_day) of teachesOnDay(teacher, day) is FALSE 201: # actually: count of periods teacher teaches on that day <= max 202: # We encode: for each day, count of periods where teaches(teacher, day, p) <= max_per_day 203: ir = _to_ir(spec, { 204: "forall": { 205: "var": "d", 206: "in": "days", 207: "body": { 208: "atMost": { 209: "k": max_per_day, 210: "var": "p", 211: "in": "periods", 212: "body": { 213: "teaches": {"teacher": teacher, "day": "$d", "period": "$p"} 214: } 215: } 216: } 217: } 218: }) 219: return [ir] 220: 221: if kind == "teacher_max_consecutive": 222: teacher = params.get("teacher", "") 223: max_consecutive = int(params.get("maxConsecutive", 999)) 224: if max_consecutive <= 0: 225: # No consecutive teaching allowed: encode as NOT consecutive of length 2 226: ir = _to_ir(spec, { 227: "forall": { 228: "var": "d", 229: "in": "days", 230: "body": { 231: "not": { 232: "consecutive": { 233: "var": "p", 234: "in": "periods", 235: "length": 2, 236: "body": { 237: "teaches": {"teacher": teacher, "day": "$d", "period": "$p"} 238: } 239: } 240: } 241: } 242: } 243: }) 244: return [ir] 245: # For max=N: NOT exists window of length N+1 where all are true 246: window_length = max_consecutive + 1 247: ir = _to_ir(spec, { 248: "forall": { 249: "var": "d", 250: "in": "days", 251: "body": { 252: "not": { 253: "consecutive": { 254: "var": "p", 255: "in": "periods", 256: "length": window_length, 257: "body": { 258: "teaches": {"teacher": teacher, "day": "$d", "period": "$p"} 259: } 260: } 261: } 262: } 263: } 264: }) 265: return [ir] 266: 267: if kind == "teacher_allowed_days": 268: teacher = params.get("teacher", "") 269: allowed_days = params.get("days", []) 270: # Encode as: for each non-allowed day, teacher does NOT teach 271: # For each day: if day is not in allowed_days, then NOT teachesOnDay 272: ir = _to_ir(spec, { 273: "forall": { 274: "var": "d", 275: "in": "days", 276: "body": { 277: "or": [ 278: {"const": d in allowed_days}, 279: { 280: "not": { 281: "teachesOnDay": {"teacher": teacher, "day": "$d"} 282: } 283: } 284: ] 285: } 286: } 287: }) 288: return [ir] 289: 290: if kind == "teacher_allowed_periods": 291: teacher = params.get("teacher", "") 292: allowed_periods = {int(p) for p in params.get("periods", [])} 293: # For each (day, period): if period is not allowed, NOT teaches 294: ir = _to_ir(spec, { 295: "forall": { 296: "var": "d", 297: "in": "days", 298: "body": { 299: "forall": { 300: "var": "p", 301: "in": "periods", 302: "body": { 303: "or": [ 304: {"const": int("$p") in allowed_periods}, 305: { 306: "not": { 307: "teaches": {"teacher": teacher, "day": "$d", "period": "$p"} 308: } 309: } 310: ] 311: } 312: } 313: } 314: } 315: }) 316: return [ir] 317: 318: if kind == "teacher_max_working_days": 319: teacher = params.get("teacher", "") 320: max_days = int(params.get("maxDays", 999)) 321: # For each teacher: count of days where teacher teachesOnDay <= max_days 322: if teacher: 323: ir = _to_ir(spec, { 324: "compare": { 325: "op": "<=", 326: "lhs": { 327: "count": { 328: "var": "d", 329: "in": "days", 330: "body": {"teachesOnDay": {"teacher": teacher, "day": "$d"}} 331: } 332: }, 333: "rhs": max_days, 334: } 335: }) 336: return [ir] 337: # Global (no teacher): apply to all teachers — emit forall 338: ir = _to_ir(spec, { 339: "forall": { 340: "var": "t", 341: "in": "teachers", 342: "body": { 343: "compare": { 344: "op": "<=", 345: "lhs": { 346: "count": { 347: "var": "d", 348: "in": "days", 349: "body": {"teachesOnDay": {"teacher": "$t", "day": "$d"}} 350: } 351: }, 352: "rhs": max_days, 353: } 354: } 355: } 356: }) 357: return [ir] 358: 359: if kind == "teacher_min_working_days": 360: teacher = params.get("teacher", "") 361: min_days = int(params.get("minDays", 1)) 362: if teacher: 363: ir = _to_ir(spec, { 364: "compare": { 365: "op": ">=", 366: "lhs": { 367: "count": { 368: "var": "d", 369: "in": "days", 370: "body": {"teachesOnDay": {"teacher": teacher, "day": "$d"}} 371: } 372: }, 373: "rhs": min_days, 374: } 375: }) 376: return [ir] 377: ir = _to_ir(spec, { 378: "forall": { 379: "var": "t", 380: "in": "teachers", 381: "body": { 382: "compare": { 383: "op": ">=", 384: "lhs": { 385: "count": { 386: "var": "d", 387: "in": "days", 388: "body": {"teachesOnDay": {"teacher": "$t", "day": "$d"}} 389: } 390: }, 391: "rhs": min_days, 392: } 393: } 394: } 395: }) 396: return [ir] 397: 398: if kind == "teacher_min_per_day": 399: teacher = params.get("teacher", "") 400: min_per_day = int(params.get("minPerDay", 1)) 401: # For each day, if teacher teaches ≥1 period, then count >= minPerDay 402: ir = _to_ir(spec, { 403: "forall": { 404: "var": "d", 405: "in": "days", 406: "body": { 407: "implies": [ 408: { 409: "exists": { 410: "var": "p", 411: "in": "periods", 412: "body": { 413: "teaches": {"teacher": teacher, "day": "$d", "period": "$p"} 414: } 415: } 416: }, 417: { 418: "compare": { 419: "op": ">=", 420: "lhs": { 421: "count": { 422: "var": "p", 423: "in": "periods", 424: "body": { 425: "teaches": {"teacher": teacher, "day": "$d", "period": "$p"} 426: } 427: } 428: }, 429: "rhs": min_per_day, 430: } 431: } 432: ] 433: } 434: } 435: }) 436: return [ir] 437: 438: if kind == "teacher_no_gaps": 439: teacher = params.get("teacher", "") 440: # For each day: if the teacher teaches on period p and a later period p', 441: # then the teacher must also teach on every period in (p, p'). 442: # Equivalent: NOT exists a gap — i.e. NOT exists (p1, p2) with p1 < p2, p2 - p1 > 1, 443: # teacher teaches on p1 and p2, but not on some period between. 444: # We encode: forall d, forall p1 < p2: if teaches(p1) and teaches(p2) and p2 - p1 > 1, 445: # then for all p3 in (p1, p2): teaches(p3). 446: # This is complex; the skeleton handles it natively. 447: return [] 448: 449: if kind == "teacher_max_gaps": 450: # Skeleton handles natively 451: return [] 452: 453: if kind == "teacher_min_consecutive": 454: # Skeleton handles natively 455: return [] 456: 457: if kind == "teacher_balanced_load": 458: # Soft, complex — skeleton handles natively 459: return [] 460: 461: if kind == "teacher_max_subjects_per_day": 462: teacher = params.get("teacher", "") 463: max_subjects = int(params.get("max", 999)) 464: # For each day: count of distinct subjects taught <= max_subjects. 465: # This requires per-subject atom — we encode as best-effort: 466: # atMost over per-subject 611: def _predicate_is_unsafe(src: str) -> str | None: 612: """Return an error message if src violates the AST allowlists, else None.""" 613: try: 614: tree = _ast.parse(src) 615: except SyntaxError as exc: 616: return f"SyntaxError: {exc}" 617: local_names: set[str] = set() 618: for node in _ast.walk(tree): 619: if isinstance(node, _ast.Name) and isinstance(node.ctx, (_ast.Store, _ast.Param)): 620: local_names.add(node.id) 621: elif isinstance(node, (_ast.For, _ast.AsyncFor)): 622: targets = [node.target] 623: while targets: 624: target = targets.pop() 625: if isinstance(target, _ast.Name): 626: local_names.add(target.id) 627: elif isinstance(target, (_ast.Tuple, _ast.List)): 628: targets.extend(target.elts) 629: for node in _ast.walk(tree): 630: if isinstance(node, (_ast.Import, _ast.ImportFrom)): 631: return f"Forbidden import at line {node.lineno}" 632: if isinstance(node, _ast.Call): 633: fn = node.func 634: name = getattr(fn, "id", None) or getattr(fn, "attr", None) 635: f"Forbidden name '{node.id}' at line {node.lineno}" 643: if ( 644: node.id not in _PREDICATE_ALLOWED_LOAD_NAMES 645: and node.id not in local_names 646: and not node.id.startswith("_") 647: ): 648: 64: return union === 0 ? 0 : intersection / union; 65: } 66: 67: function isQuotaExceeded(err: unknown): boolean { 68: if (!(err instanceof Error)) return false; 69: return ( 70: err.name === 'QuotaExceededError' || 71: err.name === 'NS_ERROR_DOM_QUOTA_REACHED' || 72: /quota/i.test(err.message) 73: ); 1: import type { AgentInputPayload } from './types'; 2: 3: export function buildTranslatorPeriods(input: AgentInputPayload): number[] { 4: const periodSet = new Set<number>(); 5: const periodsByDay = buildTranslatorPeriodsByDay(input); 6: 7: for (const periods of Object.values(periodsByDay)) { 8: for (const period of periods) { 9: if (Number.isFinite(period) && period > 0) periodSet.add(period); 10: } 11: } 12: 13: return [...periodSet].sort((a, b) => a - b); 14: } 15: 16: export function buildTranslatorPeriodsByDay(input: AgentInputPayload): Record<string, number[]> { 17: const periodsByDay: Record<string, number[]> = {}; 18: const allDaysHaveDayLevelCount = input.days.every((day) => { 19: const value = Number(input.periodCounts[day.id]); 20: return Number.isFinite(value) && value > 0; 21: }); 22: const hasSessionCounts = input.sessions.some((session) => { 23: const value = Number(input.periodCounts[session.id]); 24: return Number.isFinite(value) && value > 0; 25: }); 26: 27: for (const day of input.days) { 28: const activePeriods: number[] = []; 29: const dayLevelValue = Number(input.periodCounts[day.id]); 30: const dayHasOwnCount = Number.isFinite(dayLevelValue) && dayLevelValue > 0; 31: 32: if ((allDaysHaveDayLevelCount || dayHasOwnCount) && !hasSessionCounts) { 33: const deletedPeriods = new Set<number>(); 34: for (const [key, isDeleted] of Object.entries(input.deletedPeriods)) { 35: if (!isDeleted) continue; 36: const [keyDay, , keyPeriodRaw] = key.split('-'); 37: const keyPeriod = Number(keyPeriodRaw); 38: if (keyDay === day.id && Number.isFinite(keyPeriod)) deletedPeriods.add(keyPeriod); 39: } 40: for (let period = 1; period <= dayLevelValue; period += 1) { 41: if (!deletedPeriods.has(period)) activePeriods.push(period); 42: } 43: periodsByDay[day.id] = activePeriods; 44: continue; 45: } 46: 47: let offset = 0; 48: for (const session of input.sessions) { 49: const sessionMax = Number(input.periodCounts[session.id] ?? 0); 50: for (let period = 1; period <= sessionMax; period += 1) { 51: const key = `${day.id}-${session.id}-${period}`; 52: if (!input.deletedPeriods[key]) activePeriods.push(offset + period); 53: } 54: offset += sessionMax; 55: } 56: periodsByDay[day.id] = activePeriods; 57: } 58: 59: return periodsByDay; 60: } 61: 62: export function periodsForSession(input: AgentInputPayload, sessionId: string): number[] { 63: let offset = 0; 64: for (const session of input.sessions) { 65: const count = Number(input.periodCounts[session.id] ?? 0); 66: const periods = Array.from({ length: Math.max(0, count) }, (_, index) => offset + index + 1); 67: if (session.id === sessionId) return periods; 68: offset += count; 69: } 70: return []; 71: }1: import { constraintSignature, stableHash } from export function digestConfirmedConstraintSpecs(confirmed: ConfirmedConstraint[]): string { 6: const specs = confirmed.flatMap((c) => c.specs); 7: const signatures = specs.map((s) => constraintSignature(s)).sort((a, b) => a.localeCompare(b)); 8: return stableHash(signatures); 9: } 1: import type { ConfirmedConstraint, ParsedConstraintDraft } from '../ai/constraint-review-types'; 3: ## Status 4: 5: implemented 6: 7: ## Lane 8: 9: normal 10: 11: ## Product Contract 12: 13: The IR humanizer must render Phase 1.1 expressions deterministically in user-facing Vietnamese without generic placeholders for supported shapes. 14: 15: ## Relevant Product Docs 16: 17: - `docs/product/constraint-engine.md` 18: - `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` 19: - `PLAN.md` 20: 21: ## Acceptance Criteria 22: 23: - Session atoms render known session ids (`morning`, `afternoon`) as natural Vietnamese (`sáng`, `chiều`). 24: - `before` and `after` expressions render both sides of the relation instead of the generic `trước/sau` placeholder. 25: - `teaches`, `classBusy`, and `classSubjectAt` atom renderers are treated as supported canonical shapes, not unmatched fallbacks. 26: - Humanizer output remains deterministic for the same IR. 27: 28: ## Design Notes 29: 30: - Commands: 31: - `npm run test:grep -- ir-humanizer-v2` 32: - `npm test` 33: - `npm run build` 34: - `npm run lint` 35: - `npx gitnexus analyze` 36: - Queries: 37: - GitNexus impact: `humanizeIRExpr` and `humanizeIR` LOW risk; direct caller limited to `humanizeIR`. 38: - API: no public HTTP route changes. 39: - Tables: no database changes. 40: - Domain rules: supported IR V1.1 shapes should not ask the UI to add a template by returning `unmatched: true`. 41: - UI surfaces: improves future confirm preview text, no direct component change. 42: 43: ## Validation 44: 45: | Layer | Expected proof | 46: | --- | --- | 47: | Unit | `npm run test:grep -- ir-humanizer-v2`; `npm test` | 48: | Integration | `npm run build`; `npx gitnexus analyze`; `gitnexus_detect_changes(scope=all, repo=timetable)` | 49: | E2E | Not covered in this slice | 50: | Platform | Not covered in this slice | 51: | Release | Not covered in this slice | 52: 53: ## Harness Delta 54: 55: The required `scripts/bin/harness-cli query matrix` command remains unavailable because `scripts/bin/harness-cli` is missing in this checkout. Proof is recorded in markdown. 56: 57: ## Evidence 58: 59: - `npm run test:grep -- ir-humanizer-v2`: passed 12 tests. 60: - `npm test`: passed 542 tests. 61: - `npm run build`: passed. 62: - `npm run lint`: passed. 63: - `npx gitnexus analyze`: repository indexed successfully (6,158 nodes, 10,228 edges, 300 flows). 64: - `gitnexus_detect_changes(scope=all, repo=timetable)`: low risk; no affected execution processes. 1: # US-015 Reparse IR Semantic Validation 2: 3: ## Status 4: 5: implemented 6: 7: ## Lane 8: 9: normal 10: 11: ## Product Contract 12: 13: When the AI reparse loop returns a `custom_dsl` constraint with an IR `expr`, the candidate must be schema-valid and semantic-valid for the current timetable input before it can become a confirmable draft. 14: 15: ## Relevant Product Docs 16: 17: - `docs/product/constraint-engine.md` 18: - `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` 19: - `PLAN.md` 20: 21: ## Acceptance Criteria 22: 23: - Hard `custom_dsl` reparse candidates without `expr` remain unsupported. 24: - `custom_dsl.params.expr` must pass IR schema validation. 25: - `custom_dsl.params.expr` must pass semantic type checking against `AgentInputPayload`. 26: - Unknown entities in reparse IR return unsupported/hard-unchecked issues. 27: - Out-of-range periods in reparse IR return unsupported/hard-unchecked issues. 28: - Valid custom IR remains accepted by the reparse validator. 29: 30: ## Design Notes 31: 32: - Commands: 33: - `npm run test:grep -- reparse-candidate-validator` 34: - `npm test` 35: - `npm run build` 36: - `npm run lint` 37: - `npx gitnexus analyze` 38: - Queries: 39: - GitNexus impact: `validateReparseCandidateSpecs` LOW risk; no upstream impacted processes. 40: - API: indirectly affects `POST /api/ai/reparse-constraint` by rejecting invalid custom IR earlier, but no route shape changes. 41: - Tables: no database changes. 42: - Domain rules: reparse cannot use `custom_dsl.params.expr` as a bypass around IR type checking. 43: - UI surfaces: invalid reparse candidates surface as unsupported/needs-review issues instead of confirmable drafts. 44: 45: ## Validation 46: 47: | Layer | Expected proof | 48: | --- | --- | 49: | Unit | `npm run test:grep -- reparse-candidate-validator`; Hard block/slot constraints are relaxed last. 11: * 12: * This is purely a TypeScript orchestration layer: it does NOT solve 13: * itself, it just produces a relaxed copy of the constraint list and 14: * asks the caller to re-run the solver. 15: * 16: * The caller is responsible for the actual re-solve. 17: */ 18: 19: import type { ConstraintSpec, ConstraintSeverity } from './constraint-spec'; 20: 21: /** Constraints in this set are safe to relax from hard → soft first. */ 22: const FLEXIBLE_KINDS = new Set([ 23: 'teacher_max_per_day', 24: 'teacher_min_per_day', 25: 'teacher_max_consecutive', 26: 'teacher_min_consecutive', 27: 'teacher_max_working_days', 28: 'teacher_min_working_days', 29: 'teacher_max_classes_per_day', 30: 'teacher_max_subjects_per_day', 31: 'teacher_max_consecutive_days', 32: 'teacher_min_off_days', 33: 'teacher_max_gaps', 34: 'teacher_no_gaps', 35: 'teacher_balanced_load', 36: 'teacher_preferred_periods', 37: 'teacher_allowed_periods', 38: 'teacher_allowed_days', 39: 'subject_pin_period', 40: 'subject_preferred_periods', 41: 'subject_max_consecutive', 42: 'subject_min_gap_days', 43: 'subject_min_days', 44: 'subject_daily_max_periods', 45: 'subject_spread_evenly', 46: 'subject_consecutive', 47: 'class_max_per_day', 48: 'class_min_per_day', 49: 'class_max_consecutive', 50: 'class_max_subjects_per_day', 51: 'class_no_gaps', 52: 'class_balanced_load', 53: 'class_allowed_days', 54: 'class_allowed_periods', 55: 'class_subjects_not_same_day', 56: 'class_subjects_same_day', 57: 'global_teacher_utilization_balance', 58: 'subject_group_daily_limit', 59: 'subject_session_max_periods', 60: 'assignment_spread_days', 61: 'assignment_consecutive', 62: 'assignment_max_per_day', 63: 'weekly_periods_exact', 64: 'session_limit', 65: 'teacher_required_day', 66: 'teacher_required_slot', 67: 'teacher_pair_required_same_day', 68: 'teacher_pair_required_same_slot', 69: ]); 70: 71: export type RelaxationStep = { 72: /** Constraint ID that was relaxed. */ 73: constraintId: string; 74: /** Original kind. */ 75: kind: string; 76: /** Original severity. */ 77: originalSeverity: ConstraintSeverity; 78: /** Why this was a good candidate to relax. */ 79: reason: string; 80: }; 81: 82: export type RelaxationPlan = { 83: /** Steps to apply in order. */ 84: steps: RelaxationStep[]; 85: /** Constraints that remain hard after this plan. */ 86: remainingHard: ConstraintSpec[]; 87: /** Constraints that became soft. */ 88: relaxed: ConstraintSpec[]; 89: /** A new constraint list with the relaxation applied. */ 90: nextSpecs: ConstraintSpec[]; 91: }; 92: 93: export type RelaxationStrategy = 'flexible_first' | 'balanced' | 'conservative'; 94: 95: const STRATEGY_ORDER: Record<RelaxationStrategy, number> = { 96: flexible_first: 0, // relax flexible kinds first 97: balanced: 1, // mixed 98: conservative: 2, // relax block constraints last 99: }; 100: 101: /** Build a relaxation plan from the current infeasible state. */ 102: export function buildRelaxationPlan( 103: specs: ConstraintSpec[], 104: options?: { strategy?: RelaxationStrategy; maxRelaxations?: number } 105: ): RelaxationPlan { 106: const strategy = options?.strategy ?? 'flexible_first'; 107: const maxRelaxations = options?.maxRelaxations ?? 5; 108: 109: const hardSpecs = specs.filter((s) => s.severity === 'hard'); 110: 111: // Score each hard spec by how "flexible" it is. 112: const scored = hardSpecs.map((spec) => { 113: let flexibilityScore: number; 114: if (FLEXIBLE_KINDS.has(spec.kind)) { 115: flexibilityScore = strategy === 'flexible_first' ? 0 : strategy === 'balanced' ? 1 : 2; 116: } else { 117: // Hard block/slot/required — least flexible 118: flexibilityScore = strategy === 'flexible_first' ? 2 : strategy === 'balanced' ? 1 : 0; 119: } 120: return { spec, flexibilityScore }; 121: }); 122: 123: // Sort by flexibility (lowest first = most flexible to relax first) 124: scored.sort((a, b) => a.flexibilityScore - b.flexibilityScore); 125: 126: // Take up to maxRelaxations 127: const toRelax = scored.slice(0, maxRelaxations); 128: 129: const steps: RelaxationStep[] = toRelax.map(({ spec }) => ({ 130: constraintId: spec.id, 131: kind: spec.kind, 132: originalSeverity: spec.severity, 133: reason: FLEXIBLE_KINDS.has(spec.kind) 134: ? 'Loại ràng buộc "mềm" về bản chất (giới hạn, cân bằng, ưu tiên) — thường dễ thỏa hơn khi nới thành soft.' 135: : 'Ràng buộc cứng cuối cùng phải nới vì các ràng buộc khác đã chùng — đây là ứng viên an toàn nhất để nới thành soft.', 136: })); 137: 138: const relaxedIds = new Set(toRelax.map((s) => s.spec.id)); 139: const nextSpecs = specs.map((spec) => { 140: if (!relaxedIds.has(spec.id)) return spec; 141: return { 142: ...spec, 143: severity: 'soft' as ConstraintSeverity, 144: weight: spec.weight ?? 10, 145: notes: (spec.notes ?? '') + ' [auto-relaxed from hard]', 146: }; 147: }); 148: 149: return { 150: steps, 151: remainingHard: nextSpecs.filter((s) => s.severity === 'hard'), 152: relaxed: nextSpecs.filter((s) => s.severity === 'soft' && relaxedIds.has(s.id)), 153: nextSpecs, 154: }; 155: } 156: 157: /** Apply a relaxation plan to a list of specs. If multiple rows match, 235: * the caller decides precedence (usually the first match by id). 236: * 237: * If the sentence contains BOTH a positive and a negative marker from the 238: * same row, the match is flagged `contradictory: true` so the caller can 239: * surface a needs_clarification. 240: */ 241: export function findDisambiguationMatch(originalText: string): DisambiguationMatch[] { 242: const normalized = normalizeConstraintText(originalText); 243: const matches: DisambiguationMatch[] = []; 244: 245: for (const row of DISAMBIGUATION_TABLE) { 246: const pos = row.positiveAssertion 247: ? matchesAssertion(normalized, row.positiveAssertion) 248: : false; 249: const neg = row.negativeAssertion 250: ? matchesAssertion(normalized, row.negativeAssertion) 251: : false; 252: const soft = row.softPreferAssertion 253: ? matchesAssertion(normalized, row.softPreferAssertion) 254: : false; 255: 256: const contradictory = pos && neg; 257: 258: if (pos && !contradictory) { 259: matches.push({ 260: row, 261: direction: 'positive', 262: recommendedKind: row.positiveKinds[0], 263: contradictory: false, 264: }); 265: } else if (neg && !contradictory) { 266: matches.push({ 267: row, 268: direction: 'negative', 269: recommendedKind: row.negativeKinds[0], 270: contradictory: false, 271: }); 272: } else if (soft && !pos && !neg) { 273: const kind = row.softPreferKinds?.[0] ?? row.positiveKinds[0]; 274: matches.push({ 275: row, 276: direction: 'soft', 277: recommendedKind: kind, 278: contradictory: false, 279: }); 280: } 281: 282: if (contradictory) { 283: matches.push({ 284: row, 285: direction: 'positive', 286: recommendedKind: row.positiveKinds[0], 287: contradictory: true, 288: }); 289: } 290: } 291: 292: return matches; 293: } 294: 295: /** Versioned summary, used in debug bundles and CI parity tests. */ 296: export function summarizeDisambiguationTable(): { 297: total: number; 298: version: string; 299: byDirection: Record<DisambiguationDirection, number>; 300: } { 301: const byDirection: Record<DisambiguationDirection, number> = { 302: require: 0, 303: only: 0, 304: block: 0, 305: soft_prefer: 0, 306: }; 307: for (const row of DISAMBIGUATION_TABLE) { 308: byDirection[row.positiveDirection] += 1; 309: if (row.negativeDirection !== row.positiveDirection) { 310: byDirection[row.negativeDirection] += 1; 311: } 312: } 313: return { total: DISAMBIGUATION_TABLE.length, version: DISAMBIGUATION_TABLE_VERSION, byDirection }; 314: } 1: /** 2: * M6.5 — Expanded Golden Set V2 (100+ Vietnamese cases) 3: * 4: * Per Plan_v2.md M6.5, the parser flip is gated on a frozen golden set 5: * that covers ALL canonical phrasings, not just happy path. This file 6: * defines an EXPANDED V2 set with categories: 7: * - teacher block day/period/slot 8: * - teacher require period 9: * - teacher only allowed periods 10: * - teacher preferred periods 11: * - class block period 12: * - class require period 13: * - subject constraints 14: * - consecutive constraints 15: * - max/min per day 16: * - if-then constraints 17: * - ambiguous cases expected to ask clarification 18: * - unsupported cases expected to reject 19: * 20: * Each case pins BOTH the kind and the IR shape, plus a Vietnamese 21: * marker taxonomy. No new kind may be silently invented. 22: */ 23: 24: import type { ConstraintKind } from './constraint-spec'; 25: import type { BuiltInConstraintScope } from './constraint-registry'; 26: 27: // ─── Expanded case shape ─────────────────────────────────────────────── 28: export type ExpandedCase = { 29: id: string; 30: text: string; 31: expectedScope: BuiltInConstraintScope | 'global'; 32: expectedKind: ConstraintKind | 'ambiguous' | 'clarify' | 'unsupported'; 33: /** What semantic direction this case demonstrates. */ 34: direction: 'require' | 'block' | 'only' | 'prefer' | 'ambiguous' | 'contradictory'; 35: category: 36: | 'teacher_block_day' 37: | 'teacher_block_period' 38: | 'teacher_block_slot' 39: | 'teacher_require_period' 40: | 'teacher_only_allowed_periods' 41: | 'teacher_preferred_periods' 42: | 'class_block_period' 43: | 'class_require_period' 44: | 'subject_require_period' 45: | 'consecutive' 46: | 'max_min_per_day' 47: | 'if_then' 48: | 'ambiguous' 49: | 'unsupported'; 50: /** Whether this case is allowed to silently flip to a wrong kind. Must be false. */ 51: silentFlipForbiden: true; 52: notes: string; 53: }; 54: 55: export const EXPANDED_GOLDEN_SET: ExpandedCase[] = [ 56: // ─── Teacher block day/period/slot (12 cases) This is 6: * the second axis along which the parser will be flipped: 7: * 8: * - Phase 2 ships the new IR-first parser alongside the legacy 9: * built-in parser. The shadow log (shadow-mode.ts) tracks 10: * divergence. The flip criteria in Phase 4 require that the 11: * golden set V2 pass on BOTH axes (kind AND IR shape). 12: * 13: * Each row also tracks: 14: * - divergenceMarker: which DirectionMarker applies (require / only / 15: * block / soft_prefer / ambiguous). The disambiguation table is 16: * the single source of truth. 17: * - isFrozen: true means changing the test (or the IR) requires 18: * manual sign-off from a senior. The original "Thủy phải có tiết 19: * 4" case is frozen forever. 20: * 21: * The full set is the union of GOLDEN_EVAL_SET (kind-keyed) + the 22: * cases below (dual-keyed). The CI test runs BOTH sets. 23: */ 24: 25: import type { ConstraintKind } from './constraint-spec'; 26: import type { BuiltInConstraintScope } from './constraint-registry'; 27: import type { BoolExpr } from './constraint-ir'; 28: 29: export type ExprShape = 30: // Phase 0.2 require-family atLeast 31: | { shape: 'atLeastDaysTeaches'; teacher: string; period: number; minCount: number } 32: | { shape: 'atLeastDaysClassBusy'; class: string; period: number; minCount: number } 33: | { shape: 'atLeastDaysClassSubjectAt'; subject: string; period: number; minCount: number } 34: // Block 35: | { shape: 'notForallDaysTeaches'; teacher: string; period: number } 36: | { shape: 'notForallDaysClassBusy'; class: string; period: number } 37: // Allowed 38: | { shape: 'forallDaysTeachesInPeriods'; teacher: string; periods: number[] } 39: // max 40: | { shape: 'forallDaysCompareCount'; entity: string; op: '<=' | '>='; rhs: number } 41: // Ordering 42: | { shape: 'gap'; min: number } 43: | { shape: 'before' } 44: | { shape: 'after' } 45: // Session 46: | { shape: 'sessionAtom'; session: string; teacher?: string; class?: string; subject?: string } 47: // Custom / clarify 48: | { shape: 'customDSL' } 49: | { shape: 'clarify' }; 50: 51: export type GoldenCaseV2 = { 52: id: string; 53: text: string; 54: expectedScope: BuiltInConstraintScope; 55: expectedKind: ConstraintKind | 'ambiguous' | 'clarify' | 'custom_dsl'; 56: expectedParamKeys?: string[]; 57: expectedExprShape: ExprShape; 58: severity: 'hard' | 'soft'; 59: requiresConfirmation?: boolean; 60: /** Which disambiguation row applies (e.g. D001). Optional. */ 61: disambiguationRowId?: string; 62: /** True means changing this case requires senior sign-off. */ 63: isFrozen: boolean; 64: notes: string; 65: }; 66: 67: /** 68: * Helper: build an atLeastDaysTeaches shape (the canonical require IR). 69: */ 70: function atLeastTeacherPeriod(teacher: string, period: number, minCount: number): ExprShape { 71: return { shape: 'atLeastDaysTeaches', teacher, period, minCount }; 72: } 73: function atLeastClassPeriod(className: string, period: number, minCount: number): ExprShape { 74: return { shape: 'atLeastDaysClassBusy', class: className, period, minCount }; 75: } 76: function atLeastSubjectPeriod(subject: string, period: number, minCount: number): ExprShape { 77: return { shape: 'atLeastDaysClassSubjectAt', subject, period, minCount }; 78: } 79: function blockTeacherPeriod(teacher: string, period: number): ExprShape { 80: return { shape: 'notForallDaysTeaches', teacher, period }; 81: } 82: function blockClassPeriod(className: string, period: number): ExprShape { 83: return { shape: 'notForallDaysClassBusy', class: className, period }; 84: } 85: function allowedTeacherPeriods(teacher: string, periods: number[]): ExprShape { 86: return { shape: 'forallDaysTeachesInPeriods', teacher, periods }; 87: } 88: function maxPerDay(entity: string, rhs: number): ExprShape { 89: return { shape: 'forallDaysCompareCount', entity, op: '<=', rhs }; 90: } 91: function minPerDay(entity: string, rhs: number): ExprShape { 92: return { shape: 'forallDaysCompareCount', entity, op: '>=', rhs }; 93: } 94: 95: export const GOLDEN_EVAL_SET_V2: GoldenCaseV2[] = [ 96: // ─── FROZEN regression: the bug that started everything ────────────────── 97: { 98: id: 'G2-FROZEN-001', 99: text: 'Cô Thủy phải có ít nhất 1 tiết 4 trong tuần', 100: expectedScope: 'teacher', 101: expectedKind: 'teacher_required_period', 102: expectedParamKeys: ['teacher', 'period', 'minCount'], 103: expectedExprShape: atLeastTeacherPeriod('Thủy', 4, 1), 104: severity: 'hard', 105: disambiguationRowId: 'D001', 106: isFrozen: true, 107: notes: 'The original silent-flip bug. The previous attempt is in the request context. The new pipeline must integrate this with the raw text + previousAttempts.', 251: }, 252: ]; 253: 254: /** Summary stats for the V2 set. */ 255: export function summarizeGoldenSetV2(): { 256: total: number; 257: frozen: number; 258: byKind: Record<string, number>; 259: byShape: Record<string, number>; 260: } { 261: const byKind: Record<string, number> = {}; 262: const byShape: Record<string, number> = {}; 263: let frozen = 0; 264: for (const c of GOLDEN_EVAL_SET_V2) { 265: byKind[c.expectedKind] = (byKind[c.expectedKind] ?? 0) + 1; 266: byShape[c.expectedExprShape.shape] = (byShape[c.expectedExprShape.shape] ?? 0) + 1; 267: if (c.isFrozen) frozen += 1; 268: } 269: return { total: GOLDEN_EVAL_SET_V2.length, frozen, byKind, byShape }; 270: } 1: /** 2: * IIS Diagnosis (Section 14.2) 3: * 4: * When the solver returns infeasible, we want to identify the *Irreducible 5: * Inconsistent Subset* of constraints — a small set whose removal makes 6: * the problem feasible. 7: * 8: * OR-Tools CP-SAT can produce an IIS via the `model.Proto()` and a manual 9: * analysis. Since we don't have direct access to that from TypeScript, 10: * we use a TS-side heuristic: 11: * 1. Start with the full hard constraint set. 12: * 2. Try removing each hard constraint one at a time (or in small groups) 13: * and re-running the solver. If the solver becomes feasible after 14: * removing a constraint, that constraint is "in" the conflict set. 15: * 3. Bisect to find the minimum conflict set. 16: * 17: * For the simpler "fast" case (Section 14.2.1), we just do a single-step 18: * leave-one-out analysis: for each hard constraint, check whether the 19: * problem is "still infeasible" without it. If it becomes feasible, that 20: * constraint is in the IIS. 21: * 22: * This module is the orchestration layer. The actual re-solve is done by 23: * the caller (`deterministic-solver.ts`). 24: */ 25: 26: import type { ConstraintSpec } from './constraint-spec'; 27: 28: export type IISCandidate = { 29: /** Constraint ID suspected to be in the IIS. */ 30: constraintId: string; 31: /** Kind for UI display. */ 32: kind: string; 33: /** Original text. */ 34: original: string; 35: /** Why this is suspicious (e.g., conflicts with another constraint). */ 36: suspicionReason: string; 37: }; 38: 39: export type IISDiagnosis = { 40: /** Heuristic conflict set (smallest plausible). */ 41: conflictSet: IISCandidate[]; 42: /** Hard constraints that have NO conflict suspicion (safe to keep). */ 43: safeConstraintIds: string[]; 44: /** Whether the diagnosis was complete (true) or partial (false — would need re-solve). */ 45: complete: boolean; 46: }; 47: 48: /** Cheap heuristic: pairs of constraints that often conflict. */ 49: const KNOWN_CONFLICT_PAIRS: Array<{ a: string; b: string; reason: string }> = [ 50: { a: 'teacher_block_day', b: 'teacher_required_day', reason: 'Cấm dạy ngày X mâu thuẫn với bắt buộc dạy ngày X' }, 51: { a: 'teacher_block_period', b: 'teacher_required_slot', reason: 'Cấm dạy tiết X mâu thuẫn với bắt buộc dạy tiết X' }, 52: { a: 'teacher_no_gaps', b: 'teacher_allowed_periods', reason: 'Không gap mâu thuẫn với chỉ được dạy tiết rời rạc' }, 53: { a: 'teacher_max_per_day', b: 'teacher_min_per_day', reason: 'Tối đa N tiết có thể mâu thuẫn với tối thiểu N' tiết (N < N')' }, 54: { a: 'teacher_max_working_days', b: 'teacher_min_working_days', reason: 'Tối đa/tối thiểu ngày mâu thuẫn' }, 55: { a: 'class_block_day', b: 'class_fixed_period', reason: 'Cấm lớp học ngày X mâu thuẫn với bắt buộc lớp học ngày X' }, 56: { a: 'class_max_per_day', b: 'class_min_per_day', reason: 'Tối đa/tối thiểu tiết ngày mâu thuẫn' }, 57: { a: 'subject_pin_period', b: 'subject_block_period', reason: 'Pin môn vào tiết X mâu thuẫn với cấm môn ở tiết X' }, 58: { a: 'subject_max_consecutive', b: 'subject_consecutive', reason: 'Max N liên tiếp mâu thuẫn với cụm N+ liên tiếp (positive)' }, 59: ]; 60: 61: /** Detect direct conflicts between two hard specs. */ 62: function detectDirectConflict(a: ConstraintSpec, b: ConstraintSpec): string | null { 63: for (const pair of KNOWN_CONFLICT_PAIRS) { 64: if (a.kind === pair.a && b.kind === pair.b) { 65: if (a.params.teacher && b.params.teacher && a.params.teacher === b.params.teacher) return pair.reason; 66: if (a.params.class && b.params.class && a.params.class === b.params.class) return pair.reason; 67: if (a.params.subject && b.params.subject && a.params.subject === b.params.subject) return pair.reason; 68: } 69: if (a.kind === pair.b && b.kind === pair.a) { 70: if (a.params.teacher && b.params.teacher && a.params.teacher === b.params.teacher) return pair.reason; 71: if (a.params.class && b.params.class && a.params.class === b.params.class) return pair.reason; 72: if (a.params.subject && b.params.subject && a.params.subject === b.params.subject) return pair.reason; 73: } 74: } 75: return null; 76: } 77: 78: /** Detect numeric over-constraint between two specs. */ 79: function detectNumericConflict(a: ConstraintSpec, b: ConstraintSpec): string | null { 80: const sameEntity = (() => { 81: if (a.params.teacher && b.params.teacher && a.params.teacher === b.params.teacher) return true; 82: if (a.params.class && b.params.class && a.params.class === b.params.class) return true; 83: if (a.params.subject && b.params.subject && a.params.subject === b.params.subject) return true; 84: return false; 94: const min = Number(b.params.minDays); 95: if (max < min) return `Tối đa ${max} ngày < tối thiểu ${min} ngày/tuần → mâu thuẫn.`; 96: } 97: if (a.kind === 'class_max_per_day' && b.kind === 'class_min_per_day') { 98: const max = Number(a.params.max); 99: const min = Number(a.params.min); 100: if (max < min) return `Lớp: tối đa ${max} < tối thiểu ${min} tiết/ngày → mâu thuẫn.`; 101: } 102: return null; 103: } 104: 105: /** Heuristic IIS: walk through hard specs and find pairs that conflict. */ 106: export function diagnoseIIS(specs: ConstraintSpec[]): IISDiagnosis { 107: const hard = specs.filter((s) => s.severity === 'hard'); 138: } 139: 140: /** Build a user-friendly summary of the IIS for the UI. */ 141: export function summarizeIIS(diagnosis: IISDiagnosis): string { 142: if (diagnosis.conflictSet.length === 0) { 143: return 'Không phát hiện mâu thuẫn rõ ràng giữa các ràng buộc. We constrain 5: * the IR vocabulary to a known set: 6: * - Boolean ops: and, or, not, implies, iff 7: * - Quantifiers: exists, forall 8: * - Aggregates: atLeast, atMost, exactly 9: * - Predicates: compare, consecutive 10: * - Atoms: teaches, teachesOnDay, classSubjectAt, classBusy, assigned, const 11: * - Domains: days, periods, classes, teachers, subjects, list, range, in/where 12: * 13: * If the LLM produces a shape outside this vocabulary, we either: 14: * - Reject (hard fail) and ask for clarification 15: * - Convert to a known equivalent (e.g., iff → and(or(a,b),or(not a, not b))) 16: * 17: * The cap keeps the interpreter testable and the long tail safe. 18: */ 19: 20: import type { ConstraintIR, BoolExpr, Domain } from './constraint-ir'; 21: 22: export const IR_VOCABULARY = { 23: boolOps: ['and', 'or', 'not', 'implies', 'iff'] as const, 24: quantifiers: ['exists', 'forall'] as const, 25: aggregates: ['atLeast', 'atMost', 'exactly'] as const, 26: comparators: ['compare', 'consecutive'] as const, 27: atomOps: ['teaches', 'teachesOnDay', 'classSubjectAt', 'classBusy', 'assigned', 'const'] as const, 28: domainKinds: ['days', 'periods', 'classes', 'teachers', 'subjects', 'list', 'range', 'in'] as const, 29: } as const; 30: 31: export type IRValidationResult = { 32: ok: boolean; 33: unknownOps: string[]; 34: unknownDomainKinds: string[]; 35: /** Suggested fix: convert to known equivalent. */ 36: rewrite?: ConstraintIR; 37: }; 38: 39: function exprOps(expr: BoolExpr | undefined | null, out: Set<string>): void { 40: if (!expr || typeof expr !== 'object') return; 41: for (const key of Object.keys(expr)) { 42: if (key === 'and' || key === 'or') { 43: const arr = (expr as Record<string, unknown>)[key] as BoolExpr[]; 44: for (const e of arr) exprOps(e, out); 45: } else if (key === 'not') { 46: exprOps((expr as Record<string, unknown>).not as BoolExpr, out); 47: } else if (key === 'implies' || key === 'iff') { 48: const tuple = (expr as Record<string, unknown>)[key] as [BoolExpr, BoolExpr]; 49: exprOps(tuple[0], out); 50: exprOps(tuple[1], out); 51: out.add(key); 52: } else if (key === 'exists' || key === 'forall') { 53: const q = (expr as Record<string, unknown>)[key] as { body: BoolExpr }; 54: exprOps(q.body, out); 55: out.add(key); 56: } else if (key === 'atLeast' || key === 'atMost' || key === 'exactly') { 57: const q = (expr as Record<string, unknown>)[key] as { body: BoolExpr }; 58: exprOps(q.body, out); 59: out.add(key); 60: } else if (key === 'compare' || key === 'consecutive') { 61: const q = (expr as Record<string, unknown>)[key] as { body?: BoolExpr }; 62: if (q.body) exprOps(q.body, out); 63: out.add(key); 64: } else { 65: // atom ops 66: out.add(key); 67: } 68: } 69: } 70: 71: function domainKindsUsed(domain: Domain | undefined, out: Set<string>): void { 72: if (domain === undefined || domain === null) return; 73: if (typeof domain === 'string') { 74: out.add(domain); 75: return; 76: } 77: if ('list' in domain) { 78: out.add('list'); 79: return; 80: } 81: if ('range' in domain) { 82: out.add('range'); 83: return; 84: } 85: if ('in' in domain) { 86: out.add('in'); 87: domainKindsUsed(domain.in, out); 88: } 89: } 90: 91: /** Check an IR for vocabulary compliance. */ 92: export function validateIRVocabulary(ir: ConstraintIR): IRValidationResult { 93: const unknownOps = new Set<string>(); 94: const unknownDomains = new Set<string>(); 95: exprOps(ir.expr, unknownOps); 96: // Check quantifier domains 97: function scanDomains(e: BoolExpr): void { 98: if (!e || typeof e !== 'object') return; 99: if ('exists' in e || 'forall' in e || 'atLeast' in e || 'atMost' in e || 'exactly' in e) { 100: const q = (e as Record<string, unknown>)[Object.keys(e)[0]] as { in: Domain; body?: BoolExpr }; 101: if (q.in) domainKindsUsed(q.in, unknownDomains); 102: if (q.body) scanDomains(q.body); 103: } 104: if ('and' in e || 'or' in e) { 105: for (const sub of (e as Record<string, unknown>)[Object.keys(e)[0]] as BoolExpr[]) scanDomains(sub); 106: } 107: if ('not' in e) { 108: scanDomains((e as Record<string, unknown>).not as BoolExpr); 109: } 110: if ('implies' in e || 'iff' in e) { 111: const tuple = (e as Record<string, unknown>)[Object.keys(e)[0]] as [BoolExpr, BoolExpr]; 112: scanDomains(tuple[0]); 113: scanDomains(tuple[1]); 114: } 115: if ('consecutive' in e) { 116: const q = (e as Record<string, unknown>).consecutive as { in: Domain }; 117: domainKindsUsed(q.in, unknownDomains); 118: } 119: } 120: scanDomains(ir.expr); 121: 122: const knownOps = new Set<string>([ 123: ...IR_VOCABULARY.boolOps, 124: ...IR_VOCABULARY.quantifiers, 125: ...IR_VOCABULARY.aggregates, 126: ...IR_VOCABULARY.comparators, 127: ...IR_VOCABULARY.atomOps, 128: ]); 129: const knownDomains = new Set<string>(IR_VOCABULARY.domainKinds); 130: 131: const unknownOpsList = [...unknownOps].filter((op) => !knownOps.has(op)); 132: const unknownDomainList = [...unknownDomains].filter((d) => !knownDomains.has(d)); 133: return { 134: ok: unknownOpsList.length === 0 && unknownDomainList.length === 0, 135: unknownOps: unknownOpsList, 136: unknownDomainKinds: unknownDomainList, 137: }; 138: } 139: 140: /** Reject an IR with a clear error message. Use this for "fail-loud" paths. */ 141: export function rejectUnknownIR(ir: ConstraintIR): string { 142: const v = validateIRVocabulary(ir); 143: if (v.ok) return ''; 144: const parts: string[] = []; 145: if (v.unknownOps.length > 0) { 146: parts.push(`op chưa hỗ trợ: ${v.unknownOps.join(', ')}`); 147: } 148: if (v.unknownDomainKinds.length > 0) { 149: parts.push(`domain chưa hỗ trợ: ${v.unknownDomainKinds.join(', ')}`); 150: } 151: return `IR không hợp lệ (${parts.join('; ')}). Hãy diễn đạt lại bằng built-in.`; 152: } 1: /** 2: * parser-mode.ts — M8 parser mode feature flag 3: * 4: * Per Plan_v2.md M8.1, the parser authority can be one of: 5: * - 'legacy' : only legacy built-in parser runs 6: * - 'shadow' : both run, legacy is authoritative, IR-first logs divergence 7: * - 'ir_first' : IR-first is authoritative, legacy is unused (except for tests) 8: * 9: * The default is 'shadow' — this is the M6/M7 verification mode. 10: * The flip happens when M6 silentFlipRate == 0 AND golden V2 passes 11: * AND solver gate is fail-closed AND no solve-time LLM/codegen. 12: * 13: * The mode can be overridden at runtime via: 14: * - Environment variable CONSTRAINT_PARSER_MODE 15: * - This module's setter (used by tests) 16: */ 17: 18: export type ParserMode = 'legacy' | 'shadow' | 'ir_first'; 19: 20: const VALID_MODES: ReadonlySet<ParserMode> = new Set<ParserMode>([ 21: 'legacy', 22: 'shadow', 23: 'ir_first', 24: ]); 25: 26: let _mode: ParserMode = readDefault(); 27: 28: function readDefault(): ParserMode { 29: if (typeof process !== 'undefined' && process.env) { 30: const env = process.env.CONSTRAINT_PARSER_MODE; 31: if (env && VALID_MODES.has(env as ParserMode)) { 32: return env as ParserMode; 33: } 34: } 35: // Per plan: default is 'shadow' (M6/M7 verification mode) 36: return 'shadow'; 37: } 38: 39: export function getParserMode(): ParserMode { 40: return _mode; 41: } 42: 43: export function setParserMode(mode: ParserMode): void { 44: if (!VALID_MODES.has(mode)) { 45: throw new Error(`Invalid parser mode: ${mode}. 15: 16: export type MissLogEntry = { 17: /** User text. */ 18: text: string; 19: /** Top score (0 if no candidates). */ 20: topScore: number; 21: /** Top kind or null. */ 22: topKind: string | null; 23: /** Number of candidates with non-zero score. */ 24: candidateCount: number; Longer names are matched first and "consume" 254: * their position so overlapping substrings cannot re-match. */ 255: export function matchTeacherLabels(text: string, labels: string[]): string[] { 256: const lowerText = text.toLocaleLowerCase('vi'); 257: const sorted = [...labels].filter(Boolean).sort((a, b) => b.length - a.length); 258: const matched: string[] = []; 259: let remaining = lowerText; 260: for (const label of sorted) { 261: const lower = label.toLocaleLowerCase('vi'); 262: const escaped = lower.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\]/g, '\$&'); 263: // Word boundary: not preceded/followed by a Unicode letter or digit. 264: const re = new RegExp(`(^|[^\p{L}\p{M}_0-9])(${escaped})(?=$|[^\p{L}\p{M}_0-9])`, 'u'); 265: if (re.test(remaining)) { 266: matched.push(label); 267: remaining = remaining.replace(new RegExp(escaped, 'u'), ' '.repeat(lower.length)); 268: } 269: } 270: return matched; 271: } 272: 273: /** Match (teacher, subject, class) tuple from a text snippet, returning the 274: * unique `assignmentId` whose metadata appears in the text. Returns null if 275: * no assignment uniquely matches. Used by `if_then` THEN-clause to emit 276: * `assignment_pin_slot` / `assignment_spread_days` (F-8). 277: * 278: * Lenient mode: a field (teacher/subject/class) only narrows the candidate 279: * set when it is actually mentioned in the text. So "Toán 6A" still matches 280: * a unique assignment whose teacher isn't named in the text. */ 281: export function extractAssignmentMatch( 282: text: string, 283: assignments: AgentInputPayload['assignments'] 284: ): string | null { 285: if (!assignments?.length) return null; 286: const lower = text.toLocaleLowerCase('vi'); 287: 288: const allTeacherLabels = [...new Set(assignments.map((a) => a.teacher.label).filter(Boolean))]; 289: const allSubjectLabels = [...new Set(assignments.map((a) => a.subject.label).filter(Boolean))]; 290: const allClassLabels = [...new Set(assignments.map((a) => a.class.label).filter(Boolean))]; 291: 292: const teachersInText = allTeacherLabels.filter((t) => includesLabel(lower, t)); 293: const subjectsInText = allSubjectLabels.filter((s) => includesLabel(lower, s)); 294: const classesInText = allClassLabels.filter((c) => includesLabel(lower, c)); 295: 296: const candidates = assignments.filter((assignment) => { 297: if (teachersInText.length > 0 && !teachersInText.includes(assignment.teacher.label)) return false; 298: if (subjectsInText.length > 0 && !subjectsInText.includes(assignment.subject.label)) return false; 299: if (classesInText.length > 0 && !classesInText.includes(assignment.class.label)) return false; 300: return true; 301: }); 302: return candidates.length === 1 ? candidates[0].id : null; 303: } 304: 305: export function splitFallbackConstraintText(text: string): string[] { 306: if (/(nếu|neu)[\s\S]*(thì|thi)/iu.test(text)) { 307: return [text.trim()].filter(Boolean); 308: } 309: 310: const hasPredicate = (clause: string) => 311: /(không|khong|chỉ|chi|phải|phai|tối\s*đa|toi\s*da|max|đúng|dung|chính\s*xác|chinh\s*xac|liên\s*tiếp|lien\s*tiep|cùng|trùng|cung|trung)/iu.test( 312: clause, 313: ); 314: 315: // "đồng thời" is only a clause separator when NOT preceded by a negation word 316: // like "không". "không đồng thời dạy" = "not simultaneously teach" — keep together. 317: const splitDongThoi = (t: string): string[] => { 318: const pattern = /\s+(?:đồng\s+thời|dong\s+thoi)\s+/iu; 319: const parts: string[] = []; 320: let remaining = t; 321: while (remaining) { 322: const m = pattern.exec(remaining); 323: if (!m) { 324: parts.push(remaining); 325: break; 326: } 327: const before = remaining.slice(0, m.index); 328: // Check if "đồng thời" is preceded by a negation — if so, don't split here 329: if (/(không|khong|chẳng|chang|chưa|chua|đừng|dung)\s*$/iu.test(before)) { 330: parts.push(remaining); 331: break; 332: } 333: parts.push(before); 334: remaining = remaining.slice(m.index + m[0].length); 335: } 336: return parts; 337: }; 338: 339: return text 340: .split(/(?:;|
|)/u) 341: .flatMap(splitDongThoi) 342: .flatMap((segment) => { 343: const clauses: string[] = []; 344: let remainder = segment.trim(); 345: while (remainder) { 346: const match = /\s+(?:và)\s+/iu.exec(remainder); 347: if (!match) { 348: clauses.push(remainder); 349: break; 350: } 351: 352: const before = remainder.slice(0, match.index).trim(); 353: const after = remainder.slice(match.index + match[0].length).trim(); 354: if (!hasPredicate(before) || !hasPredicate(after)) { 355: clauses.push(remainder); 356: break; 357: } 358: 359: clauses.push(before); 360: remainder = after; 361: } 362: return clauses; 363: }) 364: .map((clause) => clause.trim()) 365: .filter(Boolean); 366: } 367: 368: export function applyConstraintWeight(spec: ConstraintSpec, weight: number | undefined): ConstraintSpec { 369: if (spec.severity !== 'soft') return spec; 370: const safeWeight = Number(weight); 371: if (!Number.isFinite(safeWeight) || safeWeight <= 0) return spec; 372: return { 373: ...spec, 374: weight: safeWeight, 375: params: { ...spec.params, weight: safeWeight }, 376: }; 377: } 378: 379: export function inferWeeklyAssignmentId( 380: assignments: AgentInputPayload['assignments'], 381: teacher: string | null, 382: subject: string | null, 383: klass: string | null, 384: weeklyPeriods: number 385: ): string | null { 386: const matched = assignments.filter((assignment) => { 387: if (teacher && assignment.teacher.label !== teacher) return false; 388: if (subject && assignment.subject.label !== subject) return false; 389: if (klass && assignment.class.label !== klass) return false; 390: return assignment.weeklyPeriods === weeklyPeriods; 391: }); 392: return matched.length === 1 ? matched[0].id : null; 393: } 394: 395: export function shouldMarkWeeklyAutoBase( 396: spec: ConstraintSpec, 397: assignments: AgentInputPayload['assignments'] 398: ): boolean { 399: if (spec.kind !== 'weekly_periods_exact') return false; 400: const assignmentId = typeof spec.params.assignmentId === 'string' ? spec.params.assignmentId : ''; 401: if (!assignmentId) return false; 402: const weeklyPeriods = Number(spec.params.weeklyPeriods ?? NaN); 403: if (!Number.isFinite(weeklyPeriods)) return false; 404: const assignment = assignments.find((item) => item.id === assignmentId); 405: if (!assignment) return false; 406: return assignment.weeklyPeriods === weeklyPeriods; 407: } 1: import type { 2: ConditionExpr, 3: DeterministicValidationContext, 4: ScheduleEntry, 5: Violation, 6: } from './constraint-spec'; 7: 8: /** 9: * O(1) append into a grouped map. Replaces the O(n) spread 10: * `[...(map.get(k) ?? []), value]` pattern that allocated a fresh 11: * array on every insert (turning group-by loops into O(n²)). 12: */ 13: export function appendToGroup<K, V>(map: Map<K, V[]>, key: K, value: V): void { 14: const arr = map.get(key); 15: if (arr) { 16: arr.push(value); 17: } else { 18: map.set(key, [value]); 19: } 20: } 21: 22: export function toPeriod(value: number | string): number | null { 23: if (typeof value === 'number' && Number.isFinite(value)) return value; 24: const n = Number(String(value)); 25: return Number.isFinite(n) ? n : null; 26: } 27: 28: export function slotKey(entry: ScheduleEntry): string { 29: return `${entry.day}::${entry.period}`; 30: } 31: 32: export function pushViolation( 33: list: Violation[], 34: constraintId: string, 35: kind: Violation['kind'], 36: message: string, 37: offendingEntries: ScheduleEntry[] 38: ): void { 39: list.push({ constraintId, kind, message, offendingEntries }); 40: } 41: 42: export function evaluateCondition(condition: ConditionExpr, schedule: ScheduleEntry[]): boolean { 43: switch (condition.op) { 44: case 'teacher_teaches_on_day': 45: return schedule.some( 46: (entry) => entry.teacher === condition.teacher && entry.day === condition.day 47: ); 48: case 'teacher_teaches_at_slot': 49: return schedule.some( 50: (entry) => 51: entry.teacher === condition.teacher && 52: entry.day === condition.day && 53: toPeriod(entry.period) === condition.period 54: ); 55: case 'teacher_pair_teaches_same_day': 56: return condition.teachers.every((teacher) => 57: schedule.some((entry) => entry.teacher === teacher && entry.day === condition.day) 58: ); 59: case 'teacher_pair_teaches_same_slot': 60: return condition.teachers.every((teacher) => 61: schedule.some( 62: (entry) => 63: entry.teacher === teacher && 64: entry.day === condition.day && 65: toPeriod(entry.period) === condition.period 66: ) 67: ); 68: case 'class_teacher_at_slot': 69: return schedule.some( 70: (entry) => 71: entry.class === condition.class && 72: entry.subject === condition.subject && 73: entry.day === condition.day && 74: toPeriod(entry.period) === condition.period 75: ); 76: case 'and': 77: return condition.args.every((arg) => evaluateCondition(arg, schedule)); 78: case 'or': 79: return condition.args.some((arg) => evaluateCondition(arg, schedule)); 80: case 'not': 81: return !evaluateCondition(condition.arg, schedule); 82: default: 83: return false; 84: } 85: } 86: 87: export function checkBaseConstraints( 88: schedule: ScheduleEntry[], 89: ctx: DeterministicValidationContext 90: ): Violation[] { 91: const violations: Violation[] = []; 92: const teacherSlotMap = new Map<string, ScheduleEntry[]>(); 93: const classSlotMap = new Map<string, ScheduleEntry[]>(); 94: 95: const assignmentById = new Map((ctx.assignments ?? []).map((assignment) => [assignment.id, assignment])); 96: 97: for (const entry of schedule) { 98: if (ctx.assignments?.length) { 99: const assignmentId = entry.assignmentId ? String(entry.assignmentId) : ''; 100: 101: if (!assignmentId) { 102: pushViolation( 103: violations, 104: 'base_missing_assignment_id', 105: 'base_constraint', 106: `Schedule entry thiếu assignmentId: ${entry.class}/${entry.subject}/${entry.teacher}`, 107: [entry] 108: ); 109: continue; 110: } 111: 112: const assignment = assignmentById.get(assignmentId); 113: if (!assignment) { 114: pushViolation( 115: violations, 116: 'base_unknown_assignment_id', 117: 'base_constraint', 118: `Schedule entry có assignmentId không tồn tại: ${assignmentId}`, 119: [entry] 120: ); 121: continue; 122: } 123: 124: if ( 125: entry.class !== assignment.class || 126: entry.subject !== assignment.subject || 127: entry.teacher !== assignment.teacher 128: ) { 129: pushViolation( 130: violations, 131: 'base_assignment_tuple_mismatch', 132: 'base_constraint', 133: `assignmentId ${assignmentId} không khớp class/subject/teacher.`, 134: [entry] 135: ); 136: } 137: } 138: 139: const teacherKey = `${entry.teacher}::${slotKey(entry)}`; 140: const classKey = `${entry.class}::${slotKey(entry)}`; 141: 142: appendToGroup(teacherSlotMap, teacherKey, entry); 143: appendToGroup(classSlotMap, classKey, entry); 144: } 145: 146: for (const entries of teacherSlotMap.values()) { 147: if (entries.length > 1) { 148: pushViolation( 149: violations, 150: 'base_teacher_clash', 151: 'base_constraint', 152: `Teacher clash tại ${entries[0].day}/${entries[0].period} cho ${entries[0].teacher}.`, 153: entries 154: ); 155: } 156: } 157: 158: for (const entries of classSlotMap.values()) { 159: if (entries.length > 1) { 160: pushViolation( 161: violations, 162: 'base_class_clash', 163: 'base_constraint', 164: `Class clash tại ${entries[0].day}/${entries[0].period} cho lớp ${entries[0].class}.`, 165: entries 166: ); 167: } 168: } 169: 170: if (ctx.assignments?.length) { 171: for (const assignment of ctx.assignments) { 172: const count = schedule.filter( 173: (entry) => String(entry.assignmentId ?? '') === assignment.id 174: ).length; 175: 176: if (count !== assignment.weeklyPeriods) { 177: pushViolation( 178: violations, 179: `base_weekly_${assignment.id}`, 180: 'base_constraint', 181: `Weekly periods mismatch for ${assignment.id}: expected ${assignment.weeklyPeriods}, got ${count}.`, 182: schedule.filter( 183: (entry) => String(entry.assignmentId ?? '') === assignment.id 184: ) 185: ); 186: } 187: } 188: } 189: 190: return violations; 191: } 1: import type { ConstraintKind, ConstraintSpec, ConstraintSeverity } from '../ai/constraint-spec'; 2:
