## Lane 8: 9: normal 10: 11: ## Product Contract 12: 13: Constraint IR semantic validation must understand quantifier placeholders emitted by kind-to-IR adapters while still failing closed for unknown entities, unknown days/sessions, out-of-range periods, and placeholder/domain mismatches. 14: 15: ## Relevant Product Docs 16: 17: - `docs/product/constraint-engine.md` 18: - `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` 19: - `PLAN.md` 20: 21: ## Acceptance Criteria 22: 23: - `$$D$$` or equivalent placeholders bound by an enclosing `days` quantifier are accepted in atom day fields. 24: - `$$P$$` or equivalent placeholders bound by an enclosing `periods` quantifier are accepted in atom period fields. 25: - `$$C$$` or equivalent placeholders bound by an enclosing `classes` quantifier are accepted in class fields. 26: - Placeholders used in the wrong domain are rejected with an `invalid_binding` issue. 27: - `classBusy` and `classSubjectAt` validate concrete day/period ranges, not only entity existence. 28: - Kind-to-IR adapter outputs are checked for semantic validity against representative `AgentInputPayload` fixtures. 29: - Existing Phase 0 frozen and golden constraint tests keep passing. 30: 31: ## Design Notes 32: 33: - Commands: 34: - `npm run test:grep -- ir-type-checker` 35: - `npm run test:grep -- kind-to-ir` 36: - `npm test` 37: - `npm run build` 38: - `npx gitnexus analyze` 39: - Queries: 40: - GitNexus impact: `checkBoolExpr`, `checkIntExpr`, `typeCheckIR` all LOW risk. 41: - API: no public HTTP route changes. 42: - Tables: no database changes. 43: - Domain rules: semantic IR validation is now binding-aware and fail-closed for domain mismatches. 44: - UI surfaces: no direct UI changes. 45: 46: ## Validation 47: 48: | Layer | Expected proof | 49: | --- | --- | 50: | Unit | `npm run test:grep -- ir-type-checker`; `npm run test:grep -- kind-to-ir`; `npm test` | 51: | Integration | `npm run build`; `npx gitnexus analyze`; `npx gitnexus detect-changes --repo timetable` | 52: | E2E | Not covered in this slice | 53: | Platform | Not covered in this slice | 54: | Release | Not covered in this slice | 55: 56: ## Harness Delta 57: 58: The required `scripts/bin/harness-cli query matrix` command could not run because `scripts/bin/harness-cli` is missing in this checkout. This story records the proof in markdown instead of durable CLI records. 59: 60: ## Evidence 61: 62: - `npm run test:grep -- ir-type-checker`: passed 17 tests. 63: - `npm run test:grep -- kind-to-ir`: passed 10 tests, including semantic type-checking of representative adapter outputs. 64: - `npm test`: passed 544 tests. 65: - `npm run build`: passed. 66: - `npm run lint`: passed. 67: - `npx gitnexus analyze`: repository indexed successfully (6,166 nodes, 10,241 edges, 300 flows). 68: - `gitnexus_detect_changes(scope=all, repo=timetable)`: low risk for the adapter proof slice; no affected execution processes. 1: import { NextResponse } from 'next/server'; 2: 3: import type { AgentInputPayload, AIProviderConfig } from '@/features/timetable/ai/types'; 4: import { analyzeConstraint } from '@/features/timetable/ai/analyze-constraint-service'; 5: 6: type AnalyzeConstraintRequest = { 7: rawText: string; 8: constraintType: 'required' | 'preferred'; 9: weight?: number; 10: agentInput: AgentInputPayload; 11: providerConfig: AIProviderConfig; 12: previousAttempts?: Array<{ 13: displayText: string; 14: source: 'built_in' | 'semantic'; 15: confidence: 'high' | 'medium' | 'low'; 16: }>; 17: conversationMessages?: Array<{ role: 'user' | 'assistant'; content: string }>; 18: }; 19: 20: export async function POST(request: Request) { 21: try { 22: const body = (await request.json()) as AnalyzeConstraintRequest; 23: 24: if (!body?.rawText || !body?.constraintType || !body?.agentInput || !body?.providerConfig) { 25: return NextResponse.json( 26: { error: 'Thiếu rawText, constraintType, agentInput hoặc providerConfig.' }, 27: { status: 400 } 28: ); 29: } 30: 31: if (!body.providerConfig.apiKey || !body.providerConfig.model) { 32: return NextResponse.json( 33: { error: 'providerConfig cần apiKey và model.' }, 34: { status: 400 } 35: ); 36: } 37: 38: const result = await analyzeConstraint( 39: body.rawText, 40: body.constraintType, 41: body.weight, 42: body.agentInput, 43: body.providerConfig, 44: { previousAttempts: body.previousAttempts, conversationMessages: body.conversationMessages } 45: ); 46: 47: return NextResponse.json(result); 48: } catch (error) { 49: const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Analyze constraint failed'; 50: return NextResponse.json({ error: message }, { status: 500 }); 51: } 52: } 1: /** 2: * Ambiguity Gate — Parse Accuracy Layer 2 3: * 4: * When the retriever returns top-k candidates, we look at the score gap 5: * between the top-1 and top-2 candidates. If the gap is small, the LLM 6: * is being asked to disambiguate between two near-equal options — a 7: * common source of subtle parse errors. 8: * 9: * Rule: if score[0] - score[1] < AMBIGUITY_DELTA OR top1 is below 10: * AMBIGUITY_FLOOR, we return a clarification request with the top 11: * candidates listed as options. 12: * 13: * This is pure code (no LLM) and runs before invoking the slot-fill LLM. 14: */ 15: 16: import type { BuiltInConstraintScope } from './constraint-registry'; 17: import { getConstraintMeta } from './constraint-registry'; 18: import type { ConstraintRetrieverCandidate, ConstraintResolverHints } from './constraint-retriever'; 19: import { retrieveTopK } from './constraint-retriever'; 20: 21: export const AMBIGUITY_DELTA = 1.2; // score gap threshold 22: export const AMBIGUITY_FLOOR = 3; // absolute min score for top-1 to be unambiguous 23: export const AMBIGUITY_TOPK_OPTIONS = 3; 24: 25: export type AmbiguityGateResult = 26: | { 27: status: 'unambiguous'; 28: winner: ConstraintRetrieverCandidate; 29: runnerUp: ConstraintRetrieverCandidate | null; 30: delta: number; 31: } 32: | { 33: status: 'ambiguous'; 34: options: ConstraintRetrieverCandidate[]; 35: delta: number; 36: /** Human-readable explanation of why we couldn't pick a winner. */ 37: reason: string; 38: }; 39: 40: /** Run the ambiguity gate on top-k candidates. */ 41: export function evaluateAmbiguity( 42: candidates: ConstraintRetrieverCandidate[] 43: ): AmbiguityGateResult { 44: if (candidates.length === 0) { 45: return { 46: status: 'ambiguous', 47: options: [], 48: delta: 0, 49: reason: 'No candidates retrieved — bạn có thể nói rõ hơn?', 50: }; 51: } 52: // We don't have raw scores here; rank-based gap is the safe proxy. 53: // When score is available upstream, callers should pass it via a wrapper. 54: // For ranking-only: gap of 0 between rank 0 and 1 → ambiguous. 55: const top = candidates[0]; 56: const runnerUp = candidates.length > 1 ? candidates[1] : null; 57: // If we have at least 2 candidates and they're not "obviously separated" 58: // (we use rank as proxy when no scores), flag ambiguous. 59: if (candidates.length === 1) { 60: return { status: 'unambiguous', winner: top, runnerUp: null, delta: Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY }; 61: } 62: if (runnerUp) { 63: const topScore = typeof top.score === 'number' ? top.score : null; 64: const runnerScore = typeof runnerUp.score === 'number' ? runnerUp.score : null; 65: if (topScore !== null && runnerScore !== null) { 66: const delta = topScore - runnerScore; 67: if (topScore < AMBIGUITY_FLOOR || delta < AMBIGUITY_DELTA) { 68: return { 69: status: 'ambiguous', 70: options: candidates.slice(0, AMBIGUITY_TOPK_OPTIONS), 71: delta, 72: reason: `Điểm ứng viên chưa đủ tách biệt: ${top.kind}=${topScore.toFixed(2)}, ${runnerUp.kind}=${runnerScore.toFixed(2)}.`, 73: }; 74: } 75: return { status: 'unambiguous', winner: top, runnerUp, delta }; 76: } 77: 78: const sameKindFamily = isSameKindFamily(top.kind, runnerUp.kind); 79: return sameKindFamily 80: ? { 81: status: 'ambiguous' as const, 82: options: [top, runnerUp], 83: delta: 0, 84: reason: `Hai ứng viên khả thi gần ngang nhau: "${top.kind}" và "${runnerUp.kind}". Bạn muốn ý nào?`, 85: } 86: : { 87: status: 'unambiguous' as const, 88: winner: top, 89: runnerUp, 90: delta: 1, 91: }; 92: } 93: return { status: 'unambiguous', winner: top, runnerUp: null, delta: Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY }; 94: } 95: 96: /** Two kinds are "same family" if they share scope and a meaningful prefix. */ 97: export function isSameKindFamily(a: string, b: string): boolean { 98: if (a === b) return true; 99: // block_X family, max_X family, min_X family 100: const families = [ 101: ['teacher_block_day', 'teacher_block_period', 'teacher_block_slot', 'teacher_no_gaps'], 102: ['teacher_max_per_day', 'teacher_max_consecutive', 'teacher_max_working_days', 'teacher_max_subjects_per_day', 'teacher_max_classes_per_day', 'teacher_max_consecutive_days', 'teacher_max_gaps'], 103: ['teacher_min_per_day', 'teacher_min_working_days', 'teacher_min_consecutive', 'teacher_min_off_days'], 104: ['teacher_allowed_days', 'teacher_allowed_periods', 'teacher_preferred_periods'], 105: ['subject_block_period', 'subject_block_days', 'subject_not_consecutive', 'subject_not_last_period'], 106: ['subject_max_consecutive', 'subject_daily_max_periods', 'subject_min_gap_days', 'subject_min_days'], 107: ['subject_pin_period', 'subject_preferred_periods'], 108: ['class_block_day', 'class_block_period', 'class_block_slot'], 109: ['class_max_per_day', 'class_min_per_day', 'class_no_gaps', 'class_max_consecutive', 'class_max_subjects_per_day', 'class_min_working_days'], 110: ['class_allowed_days', 'class_allowed_periods'], 111: ['teacher_pair_not_same_day', 'teacher_pair_not_same_slot', 'teacher_pair_required_same_day', 'teacher_pair_required_same_slot'], 112: ]; 113: for (const family of families) { 114: if (family.includes(a) && family.includes(b)) return true; 115: } 116: return false; 117: } 118: 119: /** Build a clarification block describing ambiguity for UI. */ 120: export function buildAmbiguityQuestion(gate: AmbiguityGateResult): string { 121: if (gate.status === 'unambiguous') return ''; 122: if (gate.options.length === 0) return gate.reason || 'Mình cần bạn làm rõ thêm.'; 123: const lines = gate.options.map((opt, i) => { 124: const meta = getConstraintMeta(opt.kind); 125: return ` ${i + 1}. ${meta?.label ?? opt.kind} (${opt.scope})`; 126: }); 127: return `Mình chưa chắc ý bạn muốn loại nào. Bạn chọn một trong các khả năng sau?
${lines.join('
')}`; 128: } 129: 130: /** Convenience: run retriever + gate in one call. */ 131: export function runAmbiguityGate( 132: hints: ConstraintResolverHints, 133: scope: BuiltInConstraintScope | null 134: ): { gate: AmbiguityGateResult; candidates: ConstraintRetrieverCandidate[] } { 135: const candidates = retrieveTopK(hints, scope, AMBIGUITY_TOPK_OPTIONS + 2); 136: const gate = evaluateAmbiguity(candidates.slice(0, AMBIGUITY_TOPK_OPTIONS)); 137: return { gate, candidates }; 138: } 1: /** 2: * Pre-solve Capacity Check (Section 14.1) 3: * 4: * Detect obviously-infeasible setups BEFORE invoking the solver, by simple 5: * arithmetic over the input. Catches: 6: * - Total required periods > total available slots 7: * - A teacher needs more periods than they have available slots 8: * (after applying their own availability/block constraints) 9: * - A class needs more periods than they have available slots 10: * 11: * Returns a list of human-readable problems; if non-empty, the caller should 12: * NOT invoke the solver (or should warn the user loudly). 13: * 14: * Pure code; no LLM, no solver. 15: */ 16: 17: import type { AgentInputPayload, ScheduleEntry } from './types'; 18: import type { ConstraintSpec } from './constraint-spec'; 19: import { resolveDayLabel } from './constraint-humanizer-labels'; 20: 21: export type CapacityProblem = { 22: severity: 'fatal' | 'warning'; 23: scope: 'school' | 'teacher' | 'class' | 'subject' | 'session'; 24: entity?: string; 25: message: string; 26: /** Numeric details for UI display. */ 27: required: number; 28: available: number; 29: /** Optional hint for the user. */ 30: hint?: string; 31: }; 32: 33: export type CapacityCheckResult = { 34: ok: boolean; 35: problems: CapacityProblem[]; 36: /** Total periods required / total slots available at the school level. */ 37: totals: { 38: requiredPeriods: number; 39: availableSlots: number; 40: utilizationPercent: number; 41: }; 42: }; 43: 44: function periodCountForSession(input: AgentInputPayload, sessionId: string): number { 45: const direct = input.periodCounts[sessionId]; 46: if (Number.isFinite(direct)) return direct; 47: const fallback = Object.values(input.periodCounts).filter((value) => Number.isFinite(value)); 48: return fallback.length > 0 ? Math.max(...fallback) : 0; 49: } 50: 51: /** Compute the total number of slots available per day/session. */ 52: function computeAvailableSlots(input: AgentInputPayload): number { 53: let total = 0; 54: for (const day of input.days) { 55: for (const session of input.sessions) { 56: total += periodCountForSession(input, session.id); 57: } 58: } 59: return total; 60: } 61: 62: /** Compute available slots for a specific teacher after applying their block constraints. */ 63: function computeTeacherAvailableSlots( 64: input: AgentInputPayload, 65: teacherLabel: string, 66: blockSpecs: ConstraintSpec[] 67: ): number { 68: const totalSlots = computeAvailableSlots(input); 69: let blockedSlots = 0; 70: for (const spec of blockSpecs) { 71: if (spec.params.teacher !== teacherLabel) continue; 72: const day = spec.params.day; 73: const period = spec.params.period; 74: if (day && period) { 75: blockedSlots += 1; // specific slot 76: } else if (day) { 77: // Block entire day 78: const daySession = input.sessions.reduce((s, sess) => s + periodCountForSession(input, sess.id), 0); 79: blockedSlots += daySession; 80: } else if (period) { 81: // Block a specific period across all days 82: blockedSlots += input.days.length; 83: } 84: } 85: return Math.max(0, totalSlots - blockedSlots); 86: } 87: 88: function computeClassAvailableSlots( 89: input: AgentInputPayload, 90: classLabel: string, 91: blockSpecs: ConstraintSpec[] 92: ): number { 93: const totalSlots = computeAvailableSlots(input); 94: let blockedSlots = 0; 95: for (const spec of blockSpecs) { 96: if (spec.params.class !== classLabel) continue; 97: const day = spec.params.day; 98: const period = spec.params.period; 99: if (day && period) { 100: blockedSlots += 1; 101: } else if (day) { 102: const daySession = input.sessions.reduce((s, sess) => s + periodCountForSession(input, sess.id), 0); 103: blockedSlots += daySession; 104: } else if (period) { 105: blockedSlots += input.days.length; 106: } 107: } 108: return Math.max(0, totalSlots - blockedSlots); 109: } 110: 111: /** Run all capacity checks. Returns problems; empty array = feasible from a capacity standpoint. */ 112: export function runPreSolveCapacityCheck( 113: input: AgentInputPayload, 114: specs: ConstraintSpec[] 115: ): CapacityCheckResult { 116: const problems: CapacityProblem[] = []; 117: const totalRequired = input.assignments.reduce((s, a) => s + a.weeklyPeriods, 0); 118: const totalAvailable = computeAvailableSlots(input); 119: const utilization = totalAvailable > 0 ? (totalRequired / totalAvailable) * 100 : 0; 120: 121: // School-level check 122: if (totalRequired > totalAvailable) { 123: problems.push({ 124: severity: 'fatal', 125: scope: 'school', 126: message: `Tổng tiết cần xếp (${totalRequired}) vượt tổng slot khả dụng (${totalAvailable}).`, 127: required: totalRequired, 128: available: totalAvailable, 129: hint: 'Giảm số tiết/tuần của một số phân công, hoặc tăng số buổi/tiết trong tuần.', 130: }); 131: } 132: 133: // Per-teacher check 134: const teacherBlockSpecs = specs.filter( 135: (s) => (s.kind === 'teacher_block_day' || s.kind === 'teacher_block_period' || s.kind === 'teacher_block_slot') && s.severity === 'hard' 136: ); 137: const teacherRequired = new Map<string, number>(); 138: for (const a of input.assignments) { 139: teacherRequired.set(a.teacher.label, (teacherRequired.get(a.teacher.label) ?? 0) + a.weeklyPeriods); 140: } 141: for (const [teacherLabel, required] of teacherRequired.entries()) { 142: const available = computeTeacherAvailableSlots(input, teacherLabel, teacherBlockSpecs); 143: if (required > available) { 144: problems.push({ 145: severity: 'fatal', 146: scope: 'teacher', 147: entity: teacherLabel, 148: message: `Giáo viên ${teacherLabel} cần ${required} tiết nhưng chỉ còn ${available} slot khả dụng sau ràng buộc cấm.`, 149: required, 150: available, 151: hint: 'Bỏ bớt ràng buộc cấm ngày/tiết, hoặc giảm phân công của giáo viên này.', 152: }); 153: } 154: } 155: 156: // Per-class check 157: const classBlockSpecs = specs.filter( 158: (s) => (s.kind === 'class_block_day' || s.kind === 'class_block_period' || s.kind === 'class_block_slot') && s.severity === 'hard' 159: ); 160: const classRequired = new Map<string, number>(); 161: for (const a of input.assignments) { 162: classRequired.set(a.class.label, (classRequired.get(a.class.label) ?? 0) + a.weeklyPeriods); 163: } 164: for (const [classLabel, required] of classRequired.entries()) { 165: const available = computeClassAvailableSlots(input, classLabel, classBlockSpecs); 166: if (required > available) { 167: problems.push({ 168: severity: 'fatal', 169: scope: 'class', 170: entity: classLabel, 171: message: `Lớp ${classLabel} cần ${required} tiết nhưng chỉ còn ${available} slot khả dụng sau ràng buộc cấm.`, 172: required, 173: available, 174: hint: 'Bỏ bớt ràng buộc cấm ngày/tiết của lớp, hoặc tăng số buổi học.', 175: }); 176: } 177: } 178: 179: // Per-day check (a teacher can't teach more slots in a day than periods) 180: // Detect: any day has more teacher_block_period entries than the daily session count 181: for (const [teacherLabel] of teacherRequired.entries()) { 182: const dayBlockCounts = new Map<string, number>(); 183: for (const spec of teacherBlockSpecs) { 184: if (spec.params.teacher !== teacherLabel) continue; 185: const day = typeof spec.params.day === 'string' ? spec.params.day : null; 186: const period = spec.params.period; 187: if (day && period) { 188: dayBlockCounts.set(day, (dayBlockCounts.get(day) ?? 0) + 1); 189: } 190: } 191: for (const [day, blocked] of dayBlockCounts.entries()) { 192: const dailySlots = input.sessions.reduce((s, sess) => s + periodCountForSession(input, sess.id), 0); 193: if (blocked > dailySlots) { 194: problems.push({ 195: severity: 'warning', 196: scope: 'session', 197: entity: `${teacherLabel} @ ${resolveDayLabel(day) || day}`, 198: message: `Giáo viên ${teacherLabel} bị cấm ${blocked} slot trong ${resolveDayLabel(day) || day} nhưng ngày đó chỉ có ${dailySlots} tiết.`, 199: required: blocked, 200: available: dailySlots, 201: }); 202: } 203: } 204: } 205: 206: return { 207: ok: problems.filter((p) => p.severity === 'fatal').length === 0, 208: problems, 209: totals: { 210: requiredPeriods: totalRequired, 211: availableSlots: totalAvailable, 212: utilizationPercent: Math.round(utilization * 10) / 10, 213: }, 214: }; 215: } 216: 217: /** Build a UI-friendly summary of capacity problems. */ 218: export function summarizeCapacityProblems(result: CapacityCheckResult): string { 219: if (result.ok) return `Khả thi về mặt sức chứa (${result.totals.utilizationPercent}% sử dụng).`; 220: const lines: string[] = []; 221: for (const p of result.problems) { 222: lines.push(`- [${p.severity}] ${p.message}${p.hint ? ` Gợi ý: ${p.hint}` : ''}`); 223: } 224: return `Phát hiện ${result.problems.length} vấn đề sức chứa:
${lines.join('
')}`; 225: } 226: 227: /** 228: * Quick utility to check feasibility of a produced schedule — distinct from 229: * per-constraint validation. Checks that all assignments have at least one 230: * entry (no orphan assignments). 231: */ 232: export function checkScheduleCompleteness(schedule: ScheduleEntry[], input: AgentInputPayload): { 233: complete: boolean; 234: missing: Array<{ assignmentId: string; teacher: string; class: string; subject: string; required: number; scheduled: number }>; 235: } { 236: const counts = new Map<string, number>(); 237: for (const entry of schedule) { 238: if (!entry.assignmentId) continue; 239: counts.set(entry.assignmentId, (counts.get(entry.assignmentId) ?? 0) + 1); 240: } 241: const missing: Array<{ assignmentId: string; teacher: string; class: string; subject: string; required: number; scheduled: number }> = []; 242: for (const a of input.assignments) { 243: const scheduled = counts.get(a.id) ?? 0; 244: if (scheduled < a.weeklyPeriods) { 245: missing.push({ 246: assignmentId: a.id, 247: teacher: a.teacher.label, 248: class: a.class.label, 249: subject: a.subject.label, 250: required: a.weeklyPeriods, 251: scheduled, 252: }); 253: } 254: } 255: return { complete: missing.length === 0, missing }; 256: } 1: /** 2: * constraint-capabilities.ts — M7 capability map 3: * 4: * Per Plan_v2.md M7, every supported hard constraint must have equivalent: 5: * 1. parse output (BUILT_IN_CONSTRAINT_DEFINITIONS) 6: * 2. human preview (humanizer) 7: * 3. IR validation (ir-type-checker) 8: * 4. solver encoding (kind-to-ir + CP-SAT compiler) 9: * 5. deterministic checker / verifier (deterministic-validator) 10: * 11: * This module is the single source of truth for "what works for what kind". 12: * The solver gate reads from this map to fail closed for any kind missing 13: * a capability. 14: * 15: * If any capability is false, the solver gate MUST block that hard 16: * constraint from reaching the solver. The same goes for soft kinds if 17: * `softSolverBlocked` is true. 18: */ 19: 20: import type { ConstraintKind } from './constraint-spec'; 21: import { BUILT_IN_CONSTRAINT_DEFINITIONS, SOLVER_ENCODABLE_KINDS, CHECKED_KINDS } from './constraint-registry'; 22: import { specToIR } from './kind-to-ir'; 23: 24: export type ConstraintCapability = { 25: kind: ConstraintKind; 26: /** Parser can emit this kind from Vietnamese text. */ 27: canParse: boolean; 28: /** Humanizer can render a Vietnamese preview of this kind. */ 29: canHumanize: boolean; 30: /** specToIR can convert a spec of this kind to an executable IR. */ 31: canConvertToIR: boolean; 32: /** The IR can pass type-checker. */ 33: canValidateIR: boolean; 34: /** CP-SAT solver has an encoder for this kind. */ 35: canEncodeSolver: boolean; 36: /** Deterministic validator has a checker for this kind. */ 37: canCheckDeterministically: boolean; 38: /** True if solver gate must block this hard kind when ANY capability is false. */ 39: solverGateRequired: boolean; 40: /** Optional notes for human readers. */ 41: notes?: string; 42: }; 43: 44: // ─── Capability map builder ──────────────────────────────────────────── 45: 46: export function getConstraintCapability(kind: ConstraintKind): ConstraintCapability { 47: const def = BUILT_IN_CONSTRAINT_DEFINITIONS.find((d) => d.kind === kind); 48: const hasChecker = CHECKED_KINDS.has(kind); 49: const hasEncoder = SOLVER_ENCODABLE_KINDS.has(kind); 50: 51: // Probe IR conversion with a minimal spec; this is the most reliable 52: // way to know if the adapter is wired. 53: let canConvertToIR = false; 54: try { 55: const probeSpec: any = { 56: id: `probe_${kind}`, 57: original: 'probe', 58: severity: 'hard', 59: kind, 60: params: { 61: teacher: 'T', 62: class: 'C', 63: subject: 'S', 64: day: 'monday', 65: period: 1, 66: days: ['monday'], 67: periods: [1], 68: minCount: 1, 69: maxPerDay: 5, 70: minPerDay: 1, 71: maxConsecutive: 3, 72: max: 3, 73: min: 1, 74: maxDays: 5, 75: minDays: 1, 76: minGap: 1, 77: maxHeavy: 2, 78: maxHeavyInSession: 1, 79: sessionIds: ['morning'], 80: tolerance: 1, 81: subjectA: 'S', 82: subjectB: 'S', 83: classId: 'C', 84: teachers: ['T1', 'T2'], 85: assignmentId: 'A1', 86: assignmentIds: ['A1'], 87: count: 1, 88: minOffDays: 1, 89: maxGaps: 0, 90: minConsecutive: 1, 91: maxClasses: 5, 92: maxSubj: 3, 93: weight: 1, 94: subjects: ['S'], 95: session: 'morning', 96: maxPeriods: 3, 97: if: { kind: 'teacher_block_day', params: { teacher: 'T', day: 'monday' } }, 98: then: { kind: 'teacher_block_period', params: { teacher: 'T', period: 1 } }, 99: }, 100: }; 101: const ir = specToIR(probeSpec); 102: canConvertToIR = Boolean(ir); 103: } catch { 104: canConvertToIR = false; 105: } 106: 107: return { 108: kind, 109: canParse: Boolean(def), 110: canHumanize: Boolean(def), // humanizer falls back to labelVi, always available 111: canConvertToIR, 112: canValidateIR: canConvertToIR, // if IR is produced, type-checker is generic 113: canEncodeSolver: hasEncoder, 114: canCheckDeterministically: hasChecker, 115: // For all built-in kinds, the solver gate MUST enforce capability 116: // coverage. custom_dsl is a special case (handled separately). 117: solverGateRequired: true, 118: notes: def ? '' : 'No BUILT_IN_CONSTRAINT_DEFINITIONS entry.', 119: }; 120: } 121: 122: // ─── Solver-gate pre-flight check ────────────────────────────────────── 123: 124: /** 125: * Check if a hard constraint spec can reach the solver. Returns null if 126: * all capabilities are satisfied, or a Vietnamese error message if any 127: * capability is missing. 128: */ 129: export function capabilityBlockReason( 130: kind: ConstraintKind, 131: severity: 'hard' | 'soft' | 'info' 132: ): string | null { 133: if (kind === 'custom_dsl') return null; // custom_dsl is handled separately 134: const cap = getConstraintCapability(kind); 135: if (severity !== 'hard' && !cap.canEncodeSolver) { 136: // Soft with no encoder is a warning, not a block 137: return null; 138: } 139: if (severity === 'hard') { 140: if (!cap.canParse) return `Ràng buộc loại «${kind}» chưa được đăng ký trong hệ thống.`; 141: if (!cap.canEncodeSolver) return `Ràng buộc bắt buộc loại «${kind}» hiện chưa được hỗ trợ khi xếp lịch.`; 142: if (!cap.canCheckDeterministically) return `Ràng buộc bắt buộc loại «${kind}» hiện chưa có bước kiểm tra xác định.`; 143: if (!cap.canConvertToIR) return `Ràng buộc bắt buộc loại «${kind}» hiện chưa chuyển được sang dạng IR máy hiểu.`; 144: } 145: return null; 146: } 147: 148: // ─── Audit: every solver-encodable kind must be fully capable ────────── 149: export type CapabilityAudit = { 150: total: number; 151: fullyCapable: number; 152: blocked: Array<{ kind: ConstraintKind; missing: string[] }>; 153: }; 154: 155: export function auditCapabilities(): CapabilityAudit { 156: const blocked: Array<{ kind: ConstraintKind; missing: string[] }> = []; 157: let fullyCapable = 0; 158: for (const def of BUILT_IN_CONSTRAINT_DEFINITIONS) { 159: const cap = getConstraintCapability(def.kind); 160: const missing: string[] = []; 161: if (!cap.canParse) missing.push('parse'); 162: if (!cap.canHumanize) missing.push('humanize'); 163: if (!cap.canConvertToIR) missing.push('IR'); 164: if (!cap.canValidateIR) missing.push('IR-validate'); 165: if (!cap.canEncodeSolver) missing.push('solver-encode'); 166: if (!cap.canCheckDeterministically) missing.push('checker'); 167: if (missing.length === 0) { 168: fullyCapable += 1; 169: } else { 170: blocked.push({ kind: def.kind, missing }); 171: } 172: } 173: return { total: BUILT_IN_CONSTRAINT_DEFINITIONS.length, fullyCapable, blocked }; 174: } 1: /** 2: * constraint-clarification-builder.ts — M5 structured clarification DTO 3: * 4: * Per Plan_v2.md M5, this builder produces DTOs that are safe to render 5: * to the user: only Vietnamese text, no backend enum names, no IR shapes. 6: * Each option carries a `specDraft` or `irDraft` that can be committed 7: * deterministically when the user picks the option. 8: * 9: * The older `buildClarificationQuestions` in constraint-clarification.ts 10: * still exists for the parse-pipeline path. This builder is used by the 11: * clarification DTO consumers (UI / wizard / reparse loop). 12: */ 13: 14: import type { ConstraintSpec } from './constraint-spec'; 15: import { 16: type ClarificationOption, 17: type ClarificationQuestion, 18: type InterpretationCardDTO, 19: clarificationOptionFromBuiltIn, 20: } from './constraint-clarification-types'; 21: import { humanizeConstraintSpec } from './constraint-humanizer'; 22: 23: export type { ClarificationOption, ClarificationQuestion }; 24: 25: function dayVi(day: unknown): string | undefined { 26: if (typeof day !== 'string' || !day) return undefined; 27: const aliases: Record<string, string> = { 28: monday: 'thứ 2', 29: tuesday: 'thứ 3', 30: wednesday: 'thứ 4', 31: thursday: 'thứ 5', 32: friday: 'thứ 6', 33: saturday: 'thứ 7', 34: sunday: 'chủ nhật', 35: thu2: 'thứ 2', 36: thu3: 'thứ 3', 37: thu4: 'thứ 4', 38: thu5: 'thứ 5', 39: thu6: 'thứ 6', 40: thu7: 'thứ 7', 41: }; 42: return aliases[day] ?? day; 43: } 44: 45: function conditionVi(condition: unknown): string | undefined { 46: if (!condition || typeof condition !== 'object') return undefined; 47: const c = condition as Record<string, unknown>; 48: if (c.op === 'teacher_teaches_at_slot') { 49: return `${c.teacher ?? 'Giáo viên'} dạy ${dayVi(c.day) ?? c.day ?? ''} tiết ${c.period ?? ''}`.trim(); 50: } 51: if (c.op === 'teacher_teaches_on_day') { 52: return `${c.teacher ?? 'Giáo viên'} dạy ${dayVi(c.day) ?? c.day ?? ''}`.trim(); 53: } 54: return undefined; 55: } 56: 57: export function buildInterpretationConfirm( 58: spec: ConstraintSpec, 59: droppedIllustrations: string[] = [] 60: ): InterpretationCardDTO { 61: const thenSpecs = spec.kind === 'if_then' && Array.isArray(spec.params.then) 62: ? spec.params.then as ConstraintSpec[] 63: : [spec]; 64: const scope = thenSpecs 65: .map((item) => item.params?.scope) 66: .find((value): value is Record<string, unknown> => Boolean(value && typeof value === 'object')); 67: const scopeDay = scope ? dayVi(scope.day) : undefined; 68: 69: return { 70: scopeVi: scopeDay ? `Áp dụng trong ${scopeDay}` : undefined, 71: ifAtomVi: spec.kind === 'if_then' ? conditionVi(spec.params.if) : undefined, 72: thenAtomsVi: thenSpecs.map((item) => humanizeConstraintSpec(item)), 73: notesVi: droppedIllustrations.map((item) => `‘${item.replace(/^(ví dụ|chẳng hạn|kiểu như|như là)\s+/iu, '')}’ được hiểu là ví dụ minh hoạ nên không được encode thành ràng buộc.`), 74: editableAtomIds: thenSpecs.map((item, index) => item.id || `atom_${index}`), 75: }; 76: } 77: 78: /** 79: * Build a clarification question for ambiguous require-vs-only direction 80: * (e.g. "Cô Thủy có tiết 4" — is it at-least, or allowed-only?). 81: */ 82: export function buildRequireVsOnlyQuestion( 83: teacher: string, 84: period: number, 85: classLabel?: string 86: ): ClarificationQuestion { 87: return { 88: id: 'require_vs_only', 89: questionVi: classLabel 90: ? `Bạn muốn nói là giáo viên «${teacher}» của lớp «${classLabel}» bắt buộc có ít nhất một tiết ${period} trong tuần, hay chỉ được dạy ở tiết ${period}?` 91: : `Bạn muốn nói là «${teacher}» bắt buộc có ít nhất một tiết ${period} trong tuần, hay chỉ được dạy ở tiết ${period}?`, 92: allowFreeText: true, 93: reasonCode: 'ambiguous_direction', 94: options: [ 95: clarificationOptionFromBuiltIn( 96: 'require_at_least', 97: 'teacher_required_period', 98: { teacher, period, minCount: 1 }, 99: `Bắt buộc có ít nhất một tiết ${period} trong tuần`, 100: `«${teacher}» phải dạy tiết ${period} ít nhất 1 lần trong tuần.` 101: ), 102: clarificationOptionFromBuiltIn( 103: 'only_allowed', 104: 'teacher_allowed_periods', 105: { teacher, periods: [period] }, 106: `Chỉ được dạy ở tiết ${period}`, 107: `«${teacher}» không được dạy ngoài tiết ${period}.` 108: ), 109: ], 110: }; 111: } 112: 113: /** 114: * Build a clarification question for missing teacher (e.g. "Không dạy 115: * tiết 4" with no teacher mentioned). The UI must be passed the list 116: * of known teachers so it can render them as options. 117: */ 118: export function buildMissingEntityQuestion( 119: entityKind: 'teacher' | 'class' | 'subject', 120: knownLabels: string[] 121: ): ClarificationQuestion { 122: const labels = knownLabels.length > 0 123: ? knownLabels 124: : ['(chưa có ' + (entityKind === 'teacher' ? 'giáo viên' : entityKind === 'class' ? 'lớp' : 'môn học') + ' nào trong dữ liệu)']; 125: 126: const labelEntityVi = entityKind === 'teacher' ? 'giáo viên' : entityKind === 'class' ? 'lớp' : 'môn học'; 127: const questionVi = `Bạn muốn áp dụng ràng buộc này cho ${labelEntityVi} nào?`; 128: 129: const options: ClarificationOption[] = labels.slice(0, 8).map((label) => ({ 130: id: `pick_${entityKind}_${label}`, 131: labelVi: label, 132: })); 133: 134: return { 135: id: `missing_${entityKind}`, 136: questionVi, 137: options, 138: allowFreeText: true, 139: reasonCode: 'missing_entity', 140: }; 141: } 142: 143: /** 144: * Build a clarification question for ambiguous teacher name (multiple 145: * "Thủy" in the dataset). 146: */ 147: export function buildAmbiguousEntityQuestion( 148: entityKind: 'teacher' | 'class' | 'subject', 149: partialName: string, 150: candidates: string[] 151: ): ClarificationQuestion { 152: const labelEntityVi = entityKind === 'teacher' ? 'giáo viên' : entityKind === 'class' ? 'lớp' : 'môn học'; 153: return { 154: id: `ambiguous_${entityKind}`, 155: questionVi: `Trong danh sách có nhiều ${labelEntityVi} tên «${partialName}». Bạn muốn chọn ai?`, 156: allowFreeText: false, 157: reasonCode: 'ambiguous_entity', 158: options: candidates.slice(0, 6).map((candidate) => ({ 159: id: `pick_${entityKind}_${candidate}`, 160: labelVi: candidate, 161: })), 162: }; 163: } 164: 165: /** 166: * Build a clarification question for unresolved subject semantics 167: * (per-class vs global vs preference). 168: */ 169: export function buildSubjectScopeQuestion( 170: subject: string, 171: period: number 172: ): ClarificationQuestion { 173: return { 174: id: 'subject_scope', 175: questionVi: `Bạn muốn áp dụng môn «${subject}» ở phạm vi nào?`, 176: allowFreeText: true, 177: reasonCode: 'missing_scope', 178: options: [ 179: { 180: id: 'per_class', 181: labelVi: 'Mỗi lớp học ' + subject + ' phải có ít nhất một tiết ' + period + ' trong tuần', 182: previewVi: 'Mỗi lớp có môn này sẽ được áp dụng riêng.', 183: specDraft: { 184: id: 'clarify_subject_per_class', 185: original: 'Mỗi lớp học ' + subject + ' phải có ít nhất một tiết ' + period, 186: severity: 'hard', 187: kind: 'subject_required_period', 188: params: { subject, period, minCount: 1, scope: 'per_class' }, 189: }, 190: }, 191: { 192: id: 'specific_class', 193: labelVi: 'Chỉ một lớp cụ thể cần có ' + subject + ' tiết ' + period, 194: previewVi: 'Bạn sẽ chọn lớp ở bước tiếp theo.', 195: }, 196: { 197: id: 'just_preference', 198: labelVi: 'Đây chỉ là ưu tiên, không bắt buộc', 199: specDraft: { 200: id: 'clarify_subject_preference', 201: original: 'Ưu tiên ' + subject + ' tiết ' + period, 202: severity: 'soft', 203: kind: 'subject_preferred_periods', 204: params: { subject, periods: [period] }, 205: }, 206: }, 207: ], 208: }; 209: } 210: 211: /** 212: * Build a confirmation preview for a confirmed constraint. 213: * Shown before the user clicks "Lưu ràng buộc" / "Sửa lại". 214: */ 215: export function buildConfirmationPreview( 216: spec: ConstraintSpec, 217: labelVi: string 218: ): { questionVi: string; options: ClarificationOption[] } { 219: return { 220: questionVi: `Mình sẽ lưu ràng buộc này: ${labelVi}`, 221: options: [ 222: { 223: id: 'confirm', 224: labelVi: 'Lưu ràng buộc', 225: specDraft: spec, 226: }, 227: { 228: id: 'edit', 229: labelVi: 'Sửa lại', 230: }, 231: ], 232: // Confirmation preview reuses the ClarificationOption shape but is 233: // never returned as a ClarificationQuestion (no reasonCode, no 234: // allowFreeText needed). Callers wrap this in a confirmation step. 235: } as unknown as { questionVi: string; options: ClarificationOption[] }; 236: } 1: /** 2: * constraint-clarification-types.ts — M5 UI Clarification Contract 3: * 4: * Per Plan_v2.md M5, the UI must show only structured Vietnamese DTOs 5: * to the user. The UI must NOT show: 6: * - backend enum names (teacher_required_period, etc.) 7: * - ConstraintIR shapes 8: * - params / DSL / internal fields 9: * 10: * This DTO is the boundary between the AI pipeline and the UI. The UI 11: * renders questionVi and option.labelVi only. The id and specDraft/irDraft 12: * travel with the option so the user click can deterministically map back 13: * to a ConstraintSpec without further LLM calls. 14: */ 15: 16: import type { ConstraintKind, ConstraintSpec } from './constraint-spec'; 17: import type { ConstraintIR } from './constraint-ir'; 18: 19: /** 20: * A single option the user can pick when clarifying an ambiguous 21: * constraint. `labelVi` is what the user sees — must be natural 22: * Vietnamese. `specDraft` and `irDraft` are the deterministic mappings 23: * the pipeline can apply if the user picks this option. 24: */ 25: export type ClarificationOption = { 26: /** Stable id, used for telemetry and to deterministically rebuild spec. */ 27: id: string; 28: /** User-facing Vietnamese label — no backend enums, no IR shapes. */ 29: labelVi: string; 30: /** Optional preview text shown beneath the label for longer context. */ 31: previewVi?: string; 32: /** If this option maps to a built-in kind, the draft spec to commit. */ 33: specDraft?: ConstraintSpec; 34: /** If this option maps to an executable IR, the IR to commit. */ 35: irDraft?: ConstraintIR; 36: }; 37: 38: /** 39: * A clarification question the UI shows to the user. The UI must show 40: * ONLY `questionVi` and each option's `labelVi` / `previewVi`. The 41: * `reasonCode` lets the UI display a small status badge (e.g. "mơ hồ 42: * hướng") and the `allowFreeText` toggles a free-text input as a 43: * fallback for users who don't see a fitting option. 44: */ 45: export type ClarificationQuestion = { 46: id: string; 47: questionVi: string; 48: options: ClarificationOption[]; 49: allowFreeText: boolean; 50: reasonCode: 51: | 'ambiguous_entity' 52: | 'ambiguous_direction' 53: | 'missing_entity' 54: | 'missing_period' 55: | 'missing_scope' 56: | 'unsupported_semantics' 57: | 'contradictory_markers' 58: | 'confirm_interpretation'; 59: }; 60: 61: export type InterpretationCardDTO = { 62: scopeVi?: string; 63: ifAtomVi?: string; 64: thenAtomsVi: string[]; 65: notesVi: string[]; 66: editableAtomIds: string[]; 67: }; 68: 69: /** 70: * WHY code tags that the UI may use to display a small label 71: * (e.g. "Mơ hồ về hướng" or "Thiếu thực thể"). 84: 85: // ─── Helpers used by the pipeline to build DTOs deterministically ───── 86: 87: /** 88: * Convert a built-in kind+params draft to a fully-formed ClarificationOption. 89: * `labelVi` is rendered from the spec via the humanizer so the UI never 90: * shows `kind` directly. `specDraft` carries the raw spec for commit. 91: */ 92: export function clarificationOptionFromBuiltIn( 93: id: string, 94: kind: ConstraintKind, 95: params: Record<string, unknown>, 96: labelVi: string, 97: previewVi?: string 98: ): ClarificationOption { 99: return { 100: id, 101: labelVi, 102: previewVi, 103: specDraft: { 104: id: `clarify_${id}_${Date.now()}`, 105: original: labelVi, 106: severity: 'hard', 107: kind, 108: params, 109: }, 110: }; 7: import type { 8: ConstraintParseIssue, 9: ConstraintParseStatus, 10: ParsedConstraintDraft, 11: } from './constraint-review-types'; 12: 13: const ROOM_KEYWORDS = 14: / (phong hoc|phong bo mon|suc chua phong|phong a|phong b|room) /u; 15: 16: export function isRoomConstraintText(text: string): boolean { 17: const normalized = normalizeConstraintText(text); 18: return ROOM_KEYWORDS.test(normalized) || / phong\s+[a-z0-9]/iu.test(text); 19: } 20: 21: function collectEntityLabels(input: AgentInputPayload): { 22: teachers: string[]; 23: subjects: string[]; 24: classes: string[]; 25: } { 26: const teachers = new Set<string>(); 27: const subjects = new Set<string>(); 28: const classes = new Set<string>(); 29: for (const a of input.assignments) { 30: teachers.add(a.teacher.label); 31: subjects.add(a.subject.label); 32: classes.add(a.class.label); 33: } 34: return { 35: teachers: [...teachers], 36: subjects: [...subjects], 37: classes: [...classes], 38: }; 39: } 40: 41: function matchEntity( 42: value: string, 43: pool: string[] 44: ): { kind: 'ok' } | { kind: 'unknown' } | { kind: 'ambiguous'; candidates: string[] } { 45: const trimmed = value.trim(); 46: if (!trimmed || trimmed === '__all__' || trimmed === 'all') return { kind: 'ok' }; 47: if (pool.includes(trimmed)) return { kind: 'ok' }; 48: const lower = trimmed.toLocaleLowerCase('vi'); 49: const candidates = pool.filter( 50: (p) => p.toLocaleLowerCase('vi').includes(lower) || lower.includes(p.toLocaleLowerCase('vi')) 51: ); 52: if (candidates.length === 1) return { kind: 'ok' }; 53: if (candidates.length > 1) return { kind: 'ambiguous', candidates }; 54: return { kind: 'unknown' }; 55: } 56: 57: function validateSpecEntities( 58: spec: ConstraintSpec, 59: entities: ReturnType<typeof collectEntityLabels> 60: ): ConstraintParseIssue[] { 61: const issues: ConstraintParseIssue[] = []; 62: const checks: Array<{ field: string; value: unknown; pool: string[]; label: string }> = [ 63: { field: 'teacher', value: spec.params.teacher, pool: entities.teachers, label: 'giáo viên' }, 64: { field: 'subject', value: spec.params.subject, pool: entities.subjects, label: 'môn' }, 65: { field: 'class', value: spec.params.class, pool: entities.classes, label: 'lớp' }, 66: ]; 67: for (const { field, value, pool, label } of checks) { 68: if (typeof value !== 'string' || !value.trim()) continue; 69: const result = matchEntity(value, pool); 70: if (result.kind === 'unknown') { 71: issues.push({ 72: code: 'unknown_entity', 73: field, 74: message: `Không tìm thấy ${label} “${value}” trong phân công.`, 75: }); 76: } else if (result.kind === 'ambiguous') { 77: issues.push({ 78: code: 'multiple_entity_matches', 79: field, 80: message: `“${value}” trùng nhiều ${label}.`, 81: candidates: result.candidates, 82: }); 83: } 84: } 85: return issues; 86: } 87: 88: /** Detect when an if_then spec drops teacher entities that the user mentioned in the 89: * original THEN clause. ''; 100: if (!thenClauseRaw) return null; 101: const entities = collectEntityLabels(input); 102: 103: // Collect teachers actually captured in spec.then (across all entries). 104: const capturedThenTeachers = new Set<string>(); 105: const thenList = Array.isArray(spec.params.then) ? (spec.params.then as ConstraintSpec[]) : []; 106: for (const entry of thenList) { 107: const t = entry?.params?.teacher; 108: if (typeof t === 'string' && t.trim()) capturedThenTeachers.add(t.trim()); 109: } 110: 111: // Use union of THEN sub-clause teachers so we never miss a comma-separated mention. 112: const mentionedThenTeachers = new Set<string>(); 113: for (const sub of splitThenClause(thenClauseRaw)) { 114: for (const t of matchTeacherLabels(sub, entities.teachers)) mentionedThenTeachers.add(t); 115: } 116: if (mentionedThenTeachers.size === 0) return null; 117: 118: const lost = [...mentionedThenTeachers].filter((t) => !capturedThenTeachers.has(t)); 119: if (lost.length === 0) return null; 120: 121: return { 122: code: 'possible_entity_loss', 123: field: 'then', 124: message: `Có thể thiếu giáo viên trong phần THEN — câu gốc nhắc đến ${lost.length} giáo viên (${lost.join(', ')}) mà hệ thống chưa ghi nhận.`, 125: candidates: lost, 126: }; 127: } 128: 129: function validateSpecShape(spec: ConstraintSpec): ConstraintParseIssue[] { 130: const issues: ConstraintParseIssue[] = []; 131: const meta = getConstraintMeta(spec.kind); 132: if (!meta) { 133: issues.push({ code: 'unsupported_kind', message: `Loại ràng buộc không hỗ trợ: ${spec.kind}.` }); 134: return issues; 135: } 136: for (const key of meta.requiredParams) { 137: const val = spec.params[key]; 138: if (val === undefined || val === null || val === '') { 139: issues.push({ 140: code: 'missing_required_param', 141: field: key, 142: message: `Thiếu tham số bắt buộc “${key}”.`, 143: }); 144: } 145: } 146: // hard_unchecked: kinds that solver can't encode are flagged as unsupported. 147: // EXCEPTION: custom_dsl with `expr` (IR form) IS solver-encodable. 148: const isCustomDslWithExpr = spec.kind === 'custom_dsl' && !!spec.params.expr; 149: if (spec.severity === 'hard' && !SOLVER_ENCODABLE_KINDS.has(spec.kind) && !isCustomDslWithExpr) { 150: issues.push({ 151: code: 'hard_unchecked', 152: message: `Ràng buộc bắt buộc “${spec.kind}” chưa mã hoá được vào solver.`, 153: }); 154: } 155: return issues; 156: } 157: 158: export function validateConstraintSpecs( 159: input: AgentInputPayload, 160: specs: ConstraintSpec[], 161: options?: { 162: rawText?: string; 163: source?: 'rule' | 'translator' | 'manual' | 'template' | 'ai_reparse'; 164: confidence?: 'high' | 'medium' | 'low'; 165: } 166: ): { 167: issues: ConstraintParseIssue[]; 168: status: ConstraintParseStatus; 169: confidence?: 'high' | 'medium' | 'low'; 170: clarificationQuestions?: Array<{ id: string; prompt: string; options: string[] }>; 171: } { 172: const rawText = options?.rawText ?? specs[0]?.original ?? ''; 173: if (isRoomConstraintText(rawText)) { 174: return { 175: status: 'ignored', 176: issues: [ 177: { 178: code: 'room_constraint_ignored', 179: message: 'Ràng buộc phòng học được bỏ qua theo phạm vi sản phẩm.', 180: }, 181: ], 182: }; 183: } 184: 185: const issues: ConstraintParseIssue[] = []; 186: const entities = collectEntityLabels(input); 187: 188: if (!specs.length) { 189: return { 190: status: 'unparsed', 191: issues: [{ code: 'low_confidence', message: 'Không tạo được ràng buộc có cấu trúc.' }], 192: confidence: 'low', 193: }; 194: } 195: 196: let recomputedConfidence = options?.confidence; 197: for (const spec of specs) { 198: issues.push(...validateSpecShape(spec)); 199: issues.push(...validateSpecEntities(spec, entities)); 200: const loss = detectIfThenEntityLoss(spec, rawText, input); 201: if (loss) { 202: issues.push(loss); 203: recomputedConfidence = 'low'; 204: } 205: } 206: 207: const hasHardUnchecked = issues.some((i) => i.code === 'hard_unchecked'); 208: // custom_dsl with `expr` (IR form) IS solver-encodable — don't treat as unsupported 209: const hasCustomDslHard = specs.some( 210: (s) => s.kind === 'custom_dsl' && s.severity === 'hard' && !s.params.expr 211: ); 212: const hasEntityProblem = issues.some( 213: (i) => i.code === 'unknown_entity' || i.code === 'multiple_entity_matches' 214: ); 215: const hasMissing = issues.some((i) => i.code === 'missing_required_param'); 216: const hasEntityLoss = issues.some((i) => i.code === 'possible_entity_loss'); 217: 218: if (hasCustomDslHard || hasHardUnchecked) { 219: return { status: 'unsupported', issues, confidence: recomputedConfidence }; 220: } 221: if (hasEntityProblem) { 222: return { status: 'ambiguous', issues, confidence: recomputedConfidence }; 223: } 224: if (hasMissing) { 225: return { status: 'needs_review', issues, confidence: recomputedConfidence }; 226: } 227: if (recomputedConfidence === 'low' || hasEntityLoss) { 228: if (!hasEntityLoss) { 229: issues.push({ code: 'low_confidence', message: 'Độ tin cậy phân tích thấp.' }); 230: } 231: return { status: 'needs_review', issues, confidence: 'low' }; 232: } 233: if (options?.source === 'translator') { 234: // In LLM-first architecture, translator is the primary parser. 235: // Only flag as needs_review if specs contain custom_dsl (unclear semantics). 236: // Valid, well-formed specs from translator are trusted → status 'parsed'. 237: const hasCustomDsl = specs.some((s) => s.kind === 'custom_dsl'); 238: if (hasCustomDsl) { 239: issues.push({ code: 'llm_fallback_used', message: 'Đã dùng mô hình dịch ràng buộc.' }); 240: return { status: 'needs_review', issues, confidence: recomputedConfidence }; 241: } 242: return { status: 'parsed', issues, confidence: recomputedConfidence ?? 'medium' }; 243: } 244: if (options?.source === 'ai_reparse') { 245: return { status: 'parsed', issues, confidence: recomputedConfidence ?? 'high' }; 246: } 247: 248: const needsClarification = 249: hasCustomDslHard || 250: // custom_dsl WITHOUT `expr` (IR form) needs clarification — pure natural language fallback 251: specs.some((s) => s.kind === 'custom_dsl' && !s.params.expr); 252: 253: if (needsClarification) { 254: issues.push({ 255: code: 'needs_user_clarification', 256: message: 'Hệ thống cần bạn làm rõ ý nghĩa trước khi duyệt.', 257: }); 258: return { 259: status: 'needs_review', 260: issues, 261: confidence: recomputedConfidence, 262: clarificationQuestions: buildClarificationQuestions(rawText), 263: }; 264: } 265: 266: return { status: 'parsed', issues, confidence: recomputedConfidence }; 267: } 268: 269: export function buildDraftFromSpecs( 270: draftId: string, 271: raw: { id: string; text: string; type: 'required' | 'preferred' }, 272: specs: ConstraintSpec[], 273: input: AgentInputPayload, 274: meta: { 275: source: ParsedConstraintDraft['source']; 276: confidence: ParsedConstraintDraft['confidence']; 277: explanation?: string; 278: } 279: ): ParsedConstraintDraft { 280: const { issues, status, confidence: recomputedConfidence } = validateConstraintSpecs(input, specs, { 281: rawText: raw.text, 282: source: meta.source, 283: confidence: meta.confidence, 284: }); 285: const clarificationQuestions = 286: status === 'needs_review' ? buildClarificationQuestions(raw.text) : undefined; 287: 288: const draftSummary = humanizeDraft({ 289: id: draftId, 290: rawConstraintId: raw.id, 291: original: raw.text, 292: proposedSpecs: specs, 293: status, 294: confidence: recomputedConfidence ?? meta.confidence, 295: explanation: meta.explanation ?? '', 296: issues, 297: source: meta.source, 298: }); 299: 300: return { 301: id: draftId, 302: rawConstraintId: raw.id, 303: original: raw.text, 304: proposedSpecs: specs, 305: status, 306: confidence: recomputedConfidence ?? meta.confidence, 307: explanation: meta.explanation ?? 317: } 1: /** 2: * Stage 1 Resolver — Shared function (Section 4) 3: * 4: * Extracts deterministic hints from a Vietnamese constraint text: 5: * - resolved entities (teacher / subject / class / assignment) 6: * - extracted numbers (max, periods, etc.) 7: * - inferred scope (teacher / subject / class / assignment / global) 8: * - keyword flags (block, max, min, consecutive, only, preferred, if-then) 9: * 10: * This function is shared by: 11: * - built-in-suggestion.ts (rule parser) 12: * - constraint-retriever.ts (Stage 2 input) 13: * - analyze-constraint-service.ts (small-prompt LLM context) 14: * 15: * Pure code; 25: import type { BuiltInConstraintScope } from './constraint-registry'; 26: import type { NormalizedAssignment } from './types'; 27: 28: export type ResolverHints = { 29: /** Normalized user text. */ 30: normalizedText: string; 31: /** Resolved teacher label (first match). */ 32: resolvedTeacher: string | null; 33: /** All resolved teacher labels. */ 34: resolvedTeachers: string[]; 35: /** Resolved subject label (first match). */ 36: resolvedSubject: string | null; 37: /** All resolved subject labels. */ 38: resolvedSubjects: string[]; 39: /** Resolved class label (first match). */ 40: resolvedClass: string | null; 41: /** All resolved class labels. */ 42: resolvedClasses: string[]; 43: /** Numeric hints extracted by code. */ 44: extractedNumber: number | null; 45: extractedPeriods: number[]; 46: extractedDays: string[]; 47: /** Illustration phrases stripped from trusted numeric/entity hints. */ 48: droppedIllustrations: string[]; 49: illustrationSpans: Array<{ start: number; end: number; text: string }>; 50: /** Scope inferred from entity match. */ 51: inferredScope: BuiltInConstraintScope | null; 52: /** Whether the text mentions specific constraint keywords. */ 53: mentionsBlock: boolean; 54: mentionsMax: boolean; 55: mentionsMin: boolean; 56: mentionsConsecutive: boolean; 57: mentionsOnly: boolean; 58: mentionsPreferred: boolean; 59: mentionsIfThen: boolean; 60: /** Any entity that was ambiguous (e.g., "Lan" matches "Lan Anh" and "Lan An"). */ 61: ambiguousEntity: { kind: 'teacher' | 'subject' | 'class'; candidates: string[] } | null; 62: }; 63: 64: export type ResolverInput = { 65: userText: string; 66: teachers: string[]; 67: subjects: string[]; 68: classes: string[]; 69: assignments: NormalizedAssignment[]; 70: days?: Array<{ id: string; label: string }>; 71: }; 72: 73: function stripIllustrations(text: string): { 74: trustedText: string; 75: droppedIllustrations: string[]; 76: illustrationSpans: Array<{ start: number; end: number; text: string }>; 77: } { 78: const match = text.match(/\s*(?:,?\s*)(ví dụ|chẳng hạn|kiểu như|như là)\s+(.+)$/iu); 79: if (!match || match.index == null) { 80: return { trustedText: text, droppedIllustrations: [], illustrationSpans: [] }; 81: } 82: const dropped = match[0].replace(/^[,\s]+/u, '').trim(); 83: return { 84: trustedText: text.slice(0, match.index).trim(), 85: droppedIllustrations: [dropped], 86: illustrationSpans: [{ start: match.index, end: text.length, text: dropped }], 87: }; 88: } 89: 90: /** Run Stage 1: deterministic extraction of all hints. */ 91: export function resolveConstraintHints(input: ResolverInput): ResolverHints { 92: const { trustedText, droppedIllustrations, illustrationSpans } = stripIllustrations(input.userText); 93: const normalized = normalizeConstraintText(trustedText); 94: const teacherMatch = matchEntity(normalized, input.teachers, 'giáo viên'); 95: const subjectMatch = matchEntity(normalized, input.subjects, 'môn học'); 96: const classMatch = matchEntity(normalized, input.classes, 'lớp'); 97: const subjectMatches = matchKnownEntities(trustedText, input.subjects); 98: const teacherMatches = matchKnownEntities(trustedText, input.teachers); 99: const classMatches = matchKnownEntities(trustedText, input.classes); 100: 101: const extractedNumber = extractFirstNumber(trustedText); 102: const periods: number[] = []; 103: const day = input.days ? extractDayId(trustedText, input.days) : null; 104: const period = extractPeriodNumber(trustedText); 105: if (period !== null) periods.push(period); 106: if (day) { 107: // Day is already in extractedDays 108: } 109: 110: const mentionsBlock = / (khong|cam|nghi|ko) /iu.test(normalized) && / (day|hoc) /iu.test(normalized); 111: const mentionsMax = / (toi\s*da|khong\s*qua|khong\s*hon|gioi\s*han|qua\s*\d|khong\s*day\s*qua|day\s*qua) /iu.test(normalized); 112: const mentionsMin = / (it\s*nhat|toi\s*thieu) /iu.test(normalized); 113: const mentionsConsecutive = / (lien\s*tiep|lien\s*tuc) /iu.test(normalized); 114: const mentionsOnly = / (chi) /iu.test(normalized) && / (day|hoc) /iu.test(normalized); 115: const mentionsPreferred = / (uu\s*tien|thich| nen) /iu.test(normalized); 116: const mentionsIfThen = / (neu) /iu.test(normalized) && / (thi) /iu.test(normalized); 117: 118: // Infer scope from entity match 119: let inferredScope: BuiltInConstraintScope | null = null; 120: if (teacherMatches.length > 0) inferredScope = 'teacher'; 121: else if (subjectMatches.length > 0) inferredScope = 'subject'; 122: else if (classMatches.length > 0) inferredScope = 'class'; 123: 124: // If-then → global 125: if (mentionsIfThen) inferredScope = 'global'; 126: 127: // Detect ambiguous entity 128: let ambiguousEntity: ResolverHints['ambiguousEntity'] = null; 129: const mentionsTeacherPair = / (va|cung|dong\s+thoi) /iu.test(normalized) && teacherMatches.length >= 2; 130: if (teacherMatch.status === 'ambiguous' && !mentionsIfThen && !mentionsTeacherPair) { 131: ambiguousEntity = { kind: 'teacher', candidates: teacherMatches }; 132: } else if (subjectMatch.status === 'ambiguous' && !mentionsIfThen) { 133: ambiguousEntity = { kind: 'subject', candidates: subjectMatches }; 134: } else if (classMatch.status === 'ambiguous' && !mentionsIfThen) { 135: ambiguousEntity = { kind: 'class', candidates: classMatches }; 136: } 137: 138: return { 139: normalizedText: normalized, 140: resolvedTeacher: teacherMatch.status === 'matched' ? teacherMatch.label : null, 141: resolvedTeachers: teacherMatches, 142: resolvedSubject: subjectMatch.status === 'matched' ? subjectMatch.label : null, 143: resolvedSubjects: subjectMatches, 144: resolvedClass: classMatch.status === 'matched' ? classMatch.label : null, 145: resolvedClasses: classMatches, 146: extractedNumber, 147: extractedPeriods: periods, 148: extractedDays: day ? [day] : [], 149: droppedIllustrations, 150: illustrationSpans, 151: inferredScope, 152: mentionsBlock, 153: mentionsMax, 154: mentionsMin, 155: mentionsConsecutive, 156: mentionsOnly, 157: mentionsPreferred, 158: mentionsIfThen, 159: ambiguousEntity, 160: }; 161: } 1: import { z } from 'zod'; 2: 3: import { preferCanonicalNormalizedText } from './constraint-canonical-text'; 4: import { invokeChat, type ChatPayload } from './chat-client'; 5: import { parseModelJson } from './parse-model-json'; 10: export type CustomNormalizationStatus = 'normalized' | 'needs_clarification' | 'unsupported'; 11: 12: export type CustomConstraintNormalizationInput = { 13: severity: CustomConstraintSeverity; 14: originalText: string; 33: confidence: number; 34: needsClarification: boolean; 35: clarificationQuestions: string[]; 36: rawResponse?: string; 37: usageTokens?: number; 38: }; 39: 40: type CustomNormalizationChatInvoke = ( 41: payload: ChatPayload 42: ) => Promise<{ content?: string; usage?: ChatUsage }>; 43: 44: const modelResponseSchema = z.object({ 45: status: z.enum(['normalized', 'needs_clarification', 'unsupported']).optional(), 46: normalizedText: z.string(), 47: detectedEntities: z.object({ 48: teachers: z.array(z.string()).default([]), 49: subjects: z.array(z.string()).default([]), 50: classes: z.array(z.string()).default([]), 51: assignments: z.array(z.string()).default([]), 52: days: z.array(z.string()).default([]), 53: periods: z.array(z.number()).default([]), 54: }), 55: confidence: z.number(), 56: needsClarification: z.boolean(), 57: clarificationQuestions: z.array(z.string()).default([]), 58: }); 59: 60: function unique(values: string[]): string[] { 61: return Array.from(new Set(values.map((value) => value.trim()).filter(Boolean))); 62: } 63: 64: function sentenceCase(text: string): string { 65: const compact = text.replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim(); 66: if (!compact) return ''; 67: const first = compact.charAt(0).toLocaleUpperCase('vi'); 68: const rest = compact.slice(1); 69: return /[.!?]$/u.test(compact) ? `${first}${rest}` : `${first}${rest}.`; 70: } 71: 72: function includesNormalized(text: string, value: string): boolean { 73: const normalizedValue = normalizeConstraintText(value); 74: if (!normalizedValue) return false; 75: return new RegExp(`(?:^|\s)${normalizedValue.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\]/g, '\$&')}(?:\s|$)`, 'u').test(text); 76: } 77: 78: function detectPeriods(text: string): number[] { 79: const found: number[] = []; 80: const pattern = /(?:tiết|tiet|period)\s*(\d+)/giu; 81: for (const match of text.matchAll(pattern)) { 82: const period = Number(match[1]); 83: if (Number.isFinite(period)) found.push(period); 84: } 85: return Array.from(new Set(found)); 86: } 87: 88: function detectKnownEntities(input: CustomConstraintNormalizationInput): CustomConstraintNormalizationResult['detectedEntities'] { 89: const normalized = normalizeConstraintText(input.originalText); 90: const teachers = input.teachers.filter((label) => includesNormalized(normalized, label)); 91: const subjects = input.subjects.filter((label) => includesNormalized(normalized, label)); 92: const classes = input.classes.filter((label) => includesNormalized(normalized, label)); 93: const days = input.days 94: .filter((day) => includesNormalized(normalized, day.id) || includesNormalized(normalized, day.label)) 95: .map((day) => day.id); 96: const assignments = input.assignments 97: .filter((assignment) => ( 98: teachers.includes(assignment.teacher.label) && 99: subjects.includes(assignment.subject.label) && 100: classes.includes(assignment.class.label) 101: )) 102: .map((assignment) => assignment.id); 103: 104: return { 105: teachers, 106: subjects, 107: classes, 108: assignments, 109: days: unique(days), 110: periods: detectPeriods(input.originalText), 111: }; 112: } 113: 114: function clampConfidence(value: number): number { 115: if (!Number.isFinite(value)) return 0; 116: return Math.min(1, Math.max(0, value)); 117: } 118: 119: function buildAgentInputFromNormalization(input: CustomConstraintNormalizationInput): AgentInputPayload { 120: return { 121: days: input.days, 122: sessions: [], 123: periodCounts: {}, 124: deletedPeriods: {}, 125: assignments: input.assignments, 126: constraints: [], 127: }; 128: } 129: 130: function deterministicNormalize(input: CustomConstraintNormalizationInput): CustomConstraintNormalizationResult { 131: const agentInput = buildAgentInputFromNormalization(input); 132: const baseText = sentenceCase(input.originalText); 133: const normalizedText = preferCanonicalNormalizedText(agentInput, input.originalText, baseText); 134: const detectedEntities = detectKnownEntities(input); 135: const normalized = normalizeConstraintText(input.originalText); 136: const hasConcreteEntity = 137: detectedEntities.teachers.length > 0 || 138: detectedEntities.subjects.length > 0 || 139: detectedEntities.classes.length > 0 || 140: detectedEntities.assignments.length > 0; 141: 142: if (!normalizedText) { 143: return { 144: status: 'needs_clarification', 145: normalizedText: '', 146: detectedEntities, 147: confidence: 0, 148: needsClarification: true, 149: clarificationQuestions: ['Vui lòng nhập nội dung ràng buộc custom.'], 150: }; 151: } 152: 153: if (/ (phong|phòng|san|sân|thiet bi|thiết bị|may chieu|máy chiếu) /u.test(normalized)) { 154: return { 155: status: 'unsupported', 156: normalizedText, 157: detectedEntities, 158: confidence: 0.4, 159: needsClarification: true, 160: clarificationQuestions: ['Ràng buộc này liên quan tới tài nguyên chưa có trong dữ liệu thời khóa biểu.'], 161: }; 162: } 163: 164: if (!hasConcreteEntity) { 165: return { 166: status: 'needs_clarification', 167: normalizedText, 168: detectedEntities, 169: confidence: 0.35, 170: needsClarification: true, 171: clarificationQuestions: ['Ràng buộc này áp dụng cho giáo viên, lớp, môn hoặc phân công nào?'], 172: }; 173: } 174: 175: if (/ (hop ly|hợp lý|can doi|cân đối|dep|đẹp|it|ít|nhieu|nhiều) /u.test(normalized)) { 176: return { 177: status: 'needs_clarification', 178: normalizedText, 179: detectedEntities, 180: confidence: 0.5, 181: needsClarification: true, 182: clarificationQuestions: ['Vui lòng đổi yêu cầu định tính thành giới hạn cụ thể để hệ thống kiểm tra được.'], 183: }; 184: } 185: 186: return { 187: status: 'normalized', 188: normalizedText, 189: detectedEntities, 190: confidence: 0.68, 191: needsClarification: false, 192: clarificationQuestions: [], 193: }; 194: } 195: 196: function buildPrompt(input: CustomConstraintNormalizationInput): string { 197: const assignments = input.assignments.map((assignment) => ({ 198: id: assignment.id, 199: teacher: assignment.teacher.label, 200: subject: assignment.subject.label, 201: class: assignment.class.label, 202: weeklyPeriods: assignment.weeklyPeriods, 203: })); 204: 205: return JSON.stringify({ 206: task: 'Normalize a Vietnamese custom timetable constraint without converting it into built-in specs.', 207: rules: [ 208: 'Preserve meaning and original intent.', 209: 'Do not output built-in kind names or solver specs.', 210: 'Use only provided teachers, subjects, classes, assignment ids, day ids, and period numbers mentioned by the user.', 211: 'Ask clarification when an entity, day, period, or quantity is ambiguous.', 212: 'Return unsupported when the statement cannot be made precise with the timetable data.', 213: ], 214: input: { 215: severity: input.severity, 216: originalText: input.originalText, 217: }, 218: context: { 219: teachers: input.teachers, 220: subjects: input.subjects, 221: classes: input.classes, 222: days: input.days, 223: assignments, 224: }, 225: responseShape: { 226: status: 'normalized | needs_clarification | unsupported', 227: normalizedText: 'Clear Vietnamese sentence for user review', 228: detectedEntities: { 229: teachers: ['known teacher labels'], 230: subjects: ['known subject labels'], 231: classes: ['known class labels'], 232: assignments: ['known assignment ids'], 233: days: ['known day ids'], 234: periods: ['numbers'], 235: }, 236: confidence: 'number from 0 to 1', 237: needsClarification: 'boolean', 238: clarificationQuestions: ['Vietnamese questions'], 239: }, 240: }); 254: const allowedAssignmentIds = input.assignments.map((assignment) => assignment.id); 255: const detectedEntities: CustomConstraintNormalizationResult['detectedEntities'] = { 256: teachers: filterKnown(parsed.detectedEntities.teachers, input.teachers), 257: subjects: filterKnown(parsed.detectedEntities.subjects, input.subjects), 258: classes: filterKnown(parsed.detectedEntities.classes, input.classes), 259: assignments: filterKnown(parsed.detectedEntities.assignments, allowedAssignmentIds), 260: days: filterKnown(parsed.detectedEntities.days, allowedDayIds), 261: periods: Array.from(new Set(parsed.detectedEntities.periods.filter((period) => Number.isFinite(period)))), 262: }; 263: const clarificationQuestions = unique(parsed.clarificationQuestions); 264: const needsClarification = parsed.needsClarification || clarificationQuestions.length > 0; 265: const status = parsed.status ?? (needsClarification ? 'needs_clarification' : 'normalized'); 266: 267: const agentInput = buildAgentInputFromNormalization(input); 268: const modelText = sentenceCase(parsed.normalizedText) || fallback.normalizedText; 269: const normalizedText = preferCanonicalNormalizedText(agentInput, input.originalText, modelText); 270: 271: return { 272: status, 273: normalizedText, 274: detectedEntities, 275: confidence: clampConfidence(parsed.confidence), 276: needsClarification, 277: clarificationQuestions, 278: }; 279: } 280: 281: export function buildCustomNormalizationInput( 282: severity: CustomConstraintSeverity, 283: originalText: string, 284: agentInput: AgentInputPayload 285: ): CustomConstraintNormalizationInput { 286: return { 287: severity, 288: originalText, 289: teachers: unique(agentInput.assignments.map((assignment) => assignment.teacher.label)), 290: subjects: unique(agentInput.assignments.map((assignment) => assignment.subject.label)), 291: classes: unique(agentInput.assignments.map((assignment) => assignment.class.label)), 292: assignments: agentInput.assignments, 293: days: agentInput.days, 294: }; 295: } 296: 297: export async function normalizeCustomConstraint( 298: input: CustomConstraintNormalizationInput, 299: config: AIProviderConfig, 300: chatInvoke: CustomNormalizationChatInvoke = invokeChat 301: ): Promise<CustomConstraintNormalizationResult> { 302: const fallback = deterministicNormalize(input); 303: if (fallback.status === 'unsupported' || fallback.normalizedText === '') { 304: return fallback; 305: } 306: 307: const payload: ChatPayload = { 308: baseURL: config.baseURL || 'https://openrouter.ai/api/v1', 309: apiKey: config.apiKey, 310: model: config.model, 311: messages: [ 312: { 313: role: 'user', 314: content: buildPrompt(input), 315: }, 316: ], 317: temperature: 0, 318: max_tokens: 1200, 319: response_format: { type: 'json_object' }, 320: };
