Each function in this file 6: * is the SINGLE source of truth for "what does this kind mean in IR 7: * form". The Python side (ir_compiler.py + macros.py) and the TS 8: * interpreter (ir-interpreter.ts) MUST agree with these conversions; 9: * the property-based parity test in kind-to-ir.test.ts verifies that. 10: * 11: * Why this matters: previously, the IR was a side product of macros.py 12: * and a TS interpreter that nobody directly authored for new kinds. 13: * Adding a new kind required hand-editing Python, hand-editing TS, and 14: * hoping the two agreed. This file is the contract: the adapter is 15: * canonical; the compilers must consume it. 16: * 17: * For each kind we produce a ConstraintIR where: 18: * - `id` and `original` come from the spec 19: * - `severity` and `weight` come from the spec 20: * - `expr` is the canonical semantic expression 21: * - `explain` is the humanizer output (used for display) 22: * 23: * The new require-family kinds (Phase 0.2) are first-class citizens 24: * here. Their IR is `atLeast` with a `teaches` body. 25: */ 26: 27: import type { ConstraintSpec } from './constraint-spec'; 28: import type { BoolExpr, ConstraintIR, Domain } from './constraint-ir'; 29: import { humanizeConstraintSpec } from './constraint-humanizer'; 30: 31: function makeDaysDomain(): Domain { 32: return 'days'; 33: } 34: 35: function makePeriodsDomain(): Domain { 36: return 'periods'; 37: } 38: 39: function teachesBody(teacher: string): BoolExpr { 40: return { 41: teaches: { teacher, day: '$$DAY$$', period: '$$PERIOD$$' }, 42: }; 43: } 44: 45: /** 46: * Convert a single ConstraintSpec to ConstraintIR. Returns null if the kind 47: * is not in SOLVER_ENCODABLE_KINDS or is custom_dsl (which already carries 48: * an expr in spec.params.expr). 49: */ 50: export function specToIR(spec: ConstraintSpec): ConstraintIR | null { 51: const p = spec.params as Record<string, unknown>; 52: const id = spec.id; 53: const severity = spec.severity; 54: const weight = spec.weight; 55: const original = spec.original; 56: const explain = humanizeConstraintSpec(spec); 57: const base: Pick<ConstraintIR, 'id' | 'severity' | 'weight' | 'original' | 'explain'> = { 58: id, 59: severity, 60: original, 61: explain, 62: ...(weight !== undefined ? { weight } : {}), 63: }; 64: 65: switch (spec.kind) { 66: // ─── Block kinds (negative, set-restricting) ───────────────────────── 67: case 'teacher_block_day': { 68: const teacher = String(p.teacher ?? ''); 69: const day = String(p.day ?? ''); 70: return { 71: ...base, 72: expr: { 73: not: { 74: forall: { 75: var: 'p', 76: in: makePeriodsDomain(), 77: body: { teaches: { teacher, day, period: '$$P$$' } }, 78: }, 79: }, 80: }, 81: }; 82: } 83: case 'teacher_block_period': { 84: const teacher = String(p.teacher ?? ''); 85: const period = Number(p.period); 86: return { 87: ...base, 88: expr: { 89: not: { 90: forall: { 91: var: 'd', 92: in: makeDaysDomain(), 93: body: { teaches: { teacher, day: '$$D$$', period } }, 94: }, 95: }, 96: }, 97: }; 98: } 99: case 'class_block_day': { 100: const klass = String(p.class ?? ''); 101: const day = String(p.day ?? ''); 102: return { 103: ...base, 104: expr: { 105: not: { 106: forall: { 107: var: 'p', 108: in: makePeriodsDomain(), 109: body: { 110: classBusy: { class: klass, day, period: '$$P$$' }, 111: }, 112: }, 113: }, 114: }, 115: }; 116: } 117: case 'class_block_period': { 118: const klass = String(p.class ?? ''); 119: const period = Number(p.period); 120: return { 121: ...base, 122: expr: { 123: not: { 124: forall: { 125: var: 'd', 126: in: makeDaysDomain(), 127: body: { classBusy: { class: klass, day: '$$D$$', period } }, 128: }, 129: }, 130: }, 131: }; 132: } 133: case 'subject_block_period': { 134: const subject = String(p.subject ?? ''); 135: const periods = (p.periods as number[]) ?? []; 136: return { 137: ...base, 138: expr: { 139: forall: { 140: var: 'pd', 141: in: { list: periods }, 142: body: { 143: not: { 144: forall: { 145: var: 'c', 146: in: 'classes', 147: body: { classSubjectAt: { class: '$$C$$', subject, day: '$$D$$', period: '$$PD$$' } }, 148: }, 149: }, 150: }, 151: }, 152: }, 153: }; 154: } 155: case 'subject_block_days': { 156: const subject = String(p.subject ?? ''); 157: const days = (p.days as string[]) ?? []; 158: return { 159: ...base, 160: expr: { 161: forall: { 162: var: 'dy', 163: in: { list: days }, 164: body: { 165: not: { 166: forall: { 167: var: 'c', 168: in: 'classes', 169: body: { classSubjectAt: { class: '$$C$$', subject, day: '$$DY$$', period: '$$P$$' } }, 170: }, 171: }, 172: }, 173: }, 174: }, 175: }; 176: } 177: // ─── Require kinds (positive, atLeast) — Phase 0.2 ──────────────────── 178: case 'teacher_required_period': { 179: const teacher = String(p.teacher ?? ''); 180: const period = Number(p.period); 181: const minCount = Number(p.minCount ?? p.count ?? 1); 182: return { 183: ...base, 184: expr: { 185: atLeast: { 186: k: minCount, 187: var: 'd', 188: in: makeDaysDomain(), 189: body: { teaches: { teacher, day: '$$D$$', period } }, 190: }, 191: }, 192: }; 193: } 194: case 'class_required_period': { 195: const klass = String(p.class ?? ''); 196: const period = Number(p.period); 197: const minCount = Number(p.minCount ?? p.count ?? 1); 198: return { 199: ...base, 200: expr: { 201: atLeast: { 202: k: minCount, 203: var: 'd', 204: in: makeDaysDomain(), 205: body: { classBusy: { class: klass, day: '$$D$$', period } }, 206: }, 207: }, 208: }; 209: } 210: case 'subject_required_period': { 211: const subject = String(p.subject ?? ''); 212: const period = Number(p.period); 213: const minCount = Number(p.minCount ?? p.count ?? 1); 214: return { 215: ...base, 216: expr: { 217: atLeast: { 218: k: minCount, 219: var: 'd', 220: in: makeDaysDomain(), 221: body: { 222: forall: { 223: var: 'c', 224: in: 'classes', 225: body: { classSubjectAt: { class: '$$C$$', subject, day: '$$D$$', period } }, 226: }, 227: }, 228: }, 229: }, 230: }; 231: } 232: // ─── Allowed kinds (positive, set-restricting) ──────────────────────── 233: case 'teacher_allowed_periods': { 234: const teacher = String(p.teacher ?? ''); 235: const periods = (p.periods as number[]) ?? []; 236: return { 237: ...base, 238: expr: { 239: forall: { 240: var: 'd', 241: in: makeDaysDomain(), 242: body: { 243: implies: [ 244: { teaches: { teacher, day: '$$D$$', period: '$$P$$' } }, 245: { const: false }, // placeholder; allowed kinds are checked externally 246: ] as [BoolExpr, BoolExpr], 247: }, 248: }, 249: }, 250: }; 251: } 252: // ─── max kinds (atMost) ────────────────────────────────────────────── 253: case 'teacher_max_per_day': { 254: const teacher = String(p.teacher ?? ''); 255: const maxPerDay = Number(p.maxPerDay); 256: return { 257: ...base, 258: expr: { 259: forall: { 260: var: 'd', 261: in: makeDaysDomain(), 262: body: { 263: compare: { 264: op: '<=', 265: lhs: { 266: count: { 267: var: 'p', 268: in: makePeriodsDomain(), 269: body: { teaches: { teacher, day: '$$D$$', period: '$$P$$' } }, 270: }, 271: }, 272: rhs: maxPerDay, 273: }, 274: }, 275: }, 276: }, 277: }; 278: } 279: case 'teacher_min_per_day': { 280: const teacher = String(p.teacher ?? ''); 281: const minPerDay = Number(p.minPerDay); 282: return { 283: ...base, 284: expr: { 285: forall: { 286: var: 'd', 287: in: makeDaysDomain(), 288: body: { 289: compare: { 290: op: '>=', 291: lhs: { 292: count: { 293: var: 'p', 294: in: makePeriodsDomain(), 295: body: { teaches: { teacher, day: '$$D$$', period: '$$P$$' } }, 296: }, 297: }, 298: rhs: minPerDay, 299: }, 300: }, 301: }, 302: }, 303: }; 304: } 305: // ─── Fallback: kind not yet IR-encodable ───────────────────────────── 306: default: 307: return null; 308: } 309: } 310: 311: /** 312: * Convert a list of specs to IR. Specs that don't have a known kind 313: * adapter are returned in `unconvertible` so the caller can mark them 314: * for user re-confirmation rather than auto-mapping. 315: */ 316: export function specsToIR(specs: ConstraintSpec[]): { 317: irs: ConstraintIR[]; 318: unconvertible: ConstraintSpec[]; 319: } { 320: const irs: ConstraintIR[] = []; 321: const unconvertible: ConstraintSpec[] = []; 322: for (const spec of specs) { 323: const ir = specToIR(spec); 324: if (ir) { 325: irs.push(ir); 326: } else { 327: unconvertible.push(spec); 328: } 329: } 330: return { irs, unconvertible }; 331: } 1: import { runDeterministicSolver } from './deterministic-solver'; 2: import { getDeterministicEligibility } from './deterministic-solver-eligibility'; 3: import type { ConstraintSpec } from './constraint-spec'; 4: import type { AgentInputPayload, LocalAgentConfig, LocalAgentFinalResult } from './types'; 5: import { 6: dedupeConstraintSpecs, 7: emit, 8: resolveSolverRuntime, 9: stableHash, 10: } from './local-agent-utils'; 11: 12: export interface RunLocalAgentOptions { 13: /** Specs đã xác nhận (bỏ qua translator LLM). */ 14: preTranslatedConstraintSpecs?: ConstraintSpec[]; 15: } 16: 17: export interface RunLocalAgentResult { 18: success: boolean; 19: finalResult?: LocalAgentFinalResult; 20: error?: string; 21: } 22: 23: /** 24: * Default solve path: chỉ chạy deterministic solver trên specs đã xác nhận. 25: * 26: * Pipeline AI codegen (planner/coder/repair) đã bị gỡ — batch specs không 27: * eligible cho deterministic solver sẽ fail-closed thay vì âm thầm rơi 28: * vào nhánh cũ. 29: */ 30: export async function runLocalAgent( 31: input: AgentInputPayload, 32: config: LocalAgentConfig, 33: options?: RunLocalAgentOptions 34: ): Promise<RunLocalAgentResult> { 35: // Cross-tier (VAL-CROSS-012): missing code_executor binary must surface a Vietnamese 36: // error and prevent the solver from running silently. 37: if (typeof process !== 'undefined' && process.versions?.node) { 38: const candidates = [ 39: 'linux/code_executor', 40: 'macos/code_executor', 41: 'win32/code_executor.exe', 42: 'code_executor', 43: 'code_executor.exe', 44: ]; 45: const fs = await import('node:fs'); 46: const path = await import('node:path'); 47: const found = candidates.some((rel) => { 48: try { 49: return fs.existsSync(path.join(process.cwd(), 'python-dist', rel)); 50: } catch { 51: return false; 52: } 53: }); 54: if (!found) { 55: const msg = 'đã có lỗi: code_executor binary missing. Chạy `npm run build:executor` để tạo lại.'; 56: emit(config, { type: 'status', message: msg, iteration: 0, maxIterations: 1 }); 57: return { success: false, error: msg }; 58: } 59: } 60: 61: try { 62: emit(config, { type: 'status', message: 'Khởi tạo pipeline v2...', iteration: 0, maxIterations: 1 }); 63: emit(config, { type: 'phase', phase: 'translator', message: 'Dùng confirmed specs, bỏ qua translator LLM', iteration: 0 }); 64: 65: const preTranslated = options?.preTranslatedConstraintSpecs; 66: if (!preTranslated?.length) { 67: const msg = 'Cần xác nhận (confirm) constraints trước khi chạy solver.'; 68: emit(config, { type: 'error', message: msg, fatal: true }); 69: return { success: false, error: msg }; 70: } 71: const deduped = dedupeConstraintSpecs(preTranslated); 72: const eligibility = getDeterministicEligibility(deduped); 73: if (!eligibility.ok) { 74: emit(config, { type: 'error', message: eligibility.reason, fatal: true }); 75: return { success: false, error: eligibility.reason }; 76: } 77: return runDeterministicSolver(input, config, { 78: constraintSpecs: deduped, 79: }); 80: } catch (error) { 81: const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown local-agent error'; 82: emit(config, { type: 'error', message, fatal: true }); 83: return { success: false, error: message }; 84: } 85: } 86: 87: /** 88: * Re-export helpers used by workspace storage, run cache, và test nội bộ. 89: * Một số helper còn sót từ pipeline AI codegen cũ (buildViolationSignature, 90: * shouldRepairExecutableFailure, …) đã được dọn. 91: */ 92: export const __localAgentInternal = { 93: dedupeConstraintSpecs, 94: resolveSolverRuntime, 95: stableHash, 96: }; 1: /** 2: * negative-guard.ts — Phase 0.3 3: * 4: * Pure-code safety net that prevents silent misparse. Runs after every 5: * parser (LLM, rule, reparse) and flags/demotes specs that would invert 6: * the user's stated meaning. 7: * 8: * Two guard families: 9: * 10: * 1) REQUIRE markers (`phải có`, `cần có`, `ít nhất`, `có ít nhất`, 11: * `bắt buộc có`, `phải được`) are positive intent. They MUST NOT 12: * be mapped to a *block* or *allowed* (set-restricting) kind. If a 13: * parser emits such a mismatch, the guard demotes confidence to 14: * `medium` and forces `requiresConfirmation = true`. Callers should 15: * surface the demotion so the user can re-confirm — the spec is 16: * NEVER auto-confirmed. 17: * 18: * 2) BLOCK markers (`không`, `cấm`, `nghỉ`, `đừng`, `tránh`) are 19: * negative intent. They MUST NOT be mapped to a *required* / 20: * *allowed* (positive-set / atLeast) kind. Same demotion policy. 21: * 22: * The guard is intentionally conservative: false positives only force 23: * confirmation, never auto-confirm. False negatives (a misparse that 24: * slipped through) are caught by the back-translation gate. 25: * 26: * The guard also returns a list of `hardReasons` so callers can short- 27: * circuit to `needs_clarification` when ambiguity is unresolvable. 28: * 29: * NOTE: This module now uses semantic-direction.ts for unified marker 30: * detection across all parser paths. 31: */ 32: 33: import type { ConstraintSpec } from './constraint-spec'; 34: import { analyzeSemanticDirection } from './semantic-direction'; 35: 36: // Re-export for backward compatibility 37: export { REQUIRE_PATTERNS as REQUIRE_MARKERS, BLOCK_PATTERNS as BLOCK_MARKERS } from './semantic-direction'; 38: 39: /** Kinds that express positive, set-restricting (only) or atLeast semantics. */ 40: const POSITIVE_SET_KINDS = new Set([ 41: 'teacher_allowed_days', 42: 'teacher_allowed_periods', 43: 'teacher_preferred_periods', 44: 'class_allowed_days', 45: 'class_allowed_periods', 46: 'subject_allowed_days', 47: 'subject_preferred_periods', 48: 'subject_pin_period', 49: 'class_fixed_period', 50: 'teacher_required_period', 51: 'class_required_period', 52: 'subject_required_period', 53: 'teacher_required_day', 54: 'teacher_required_slot', 55: ]); 56: 57: /** Kinds that express negative, set-restricting (block) semantics. */ 58: const NEGATIVE_SET_KINDS = new Set([ 59: 'teacher_block_day', 60: 'teacher_block_period', 61: 'teacher_block_slot', 62: 'class_block_day', 63: 'class_block_period', 64: 'class_block_slot', 65: 'subject_block_period', 66: 'subject_block_days', 67: ]); 68: 69: export type GuardDecision = 70: | { kind: 'ok' } 71: | { 72: kind: 'demote_to_medium_with_confirmation'; 73: reason: string; 74: marker: 'require' | 'block'; 75: violatedKind: string; 76: } 77: | { 78: kind: 'force_clarification'; 79: reason: string; 80: hardReasons: string[]; 81: }; 82: 83: /** 84: * Run the negative guard against a single spec. Pure, side-effect-free. 85: * 86: * @param spec the candidate spec emitted by a parser 87: * @param originalText the user's original Vietnamese input 88: * @returns a decision the caller must honor 89: */ 90: export function evaluateNegativeGuard( 91: spec: ConstraintSpec, 92: originalText: string 93: ): GuardDecision { 94: // Use semantic-direction analyzer for unified marker detection 95: const semanticAnalysis = analyzeSemanticDirection(originalText); 96: 97: // We check the raw marker lists, not just the resolved direction. 98: // Reason: contradictory text (e.g. "phải có X nhưng không dạy Y") still 99: // contains both require and block markers, and any positive-set spec 100: // produced for it must be demoted — even though the analyzer's final 101: // direction is 'contradictory'. This keeps the demote policy 102: // conservative and independent of how the contradiction is resolved. 103: const hasRequireMarker = semanticAnalysis.matched.require.length > 0; 104: const hasBlockMarker = semanticAnalysis.matched.block.length > 0; 105: 106: // REQUIRE marker + negative-set kind = silent misparse (e.g. "phải có tiết 4" → block_period). 107: if (hasRequireMarker && NEGATIVE_SET_KINDS.has(spec.kind as string)) { 108: return { 109: kind: 'demote_to_medium_with_confirmation', 110: reason: `Câu chứa mỏ neo yêu cầu (${semanticAnalysis.matched.require.join(', ')}) nhưng parser ánh xạ sang kind chặn (${spec.kind}). Cần xác nhận lại.`, 111: marker: 'require', 112: violatedKind: spec.kind, 113: }; 114: } 115: 116: // BLOCK marker + positive-set kind = silent misparse (e.g. "không dạy tiết 4" → required_period). 117: if (hasBlockMarker && POSITIVE_SET_KINDS.has(spec.kind as string)) { 118: return { 119: kind: 'demote_to_medium_with_confirmation', 120: reason: `Câu chứa mỏ neo phủ định (${semanticAnalysis.matched.block.join(', ')}) nhưng parser ánh xạ sang kind dương (${spec.kind}). Cần xác nhận lại.`, 121: marker: 'block', 122: violatedKind: spec.kind, 123: }; 124: } 125: 126: // Same conflict can appear in custom_dsl (custom_dsl without `expr` is opaque to the guard; 127: // with `expr` we trust the IR humanizer). Skip custom_dsl to avoid false positives. 128: 129: return { kind: 'ok' }; 130: } 131: 132: /** 133: * Apply the guard to a list of specs. Returns a list of decisions (one per spec) 134: * and a list of hard reasons that, if non-empty, force `needs_clarification`. 135: * 136: * Hard reason rule: if the same spec violates the guard with BOTH a require 137: * marker AND a block marker present in the same text, the sentence is 138: * self-contradicting and the parser cannot resolve it. Force clarification. 139: */ 140: export function evaluateNegativeGuardForSpecs( 141: specs: ConstraintSpec[], 142: originalText: string 143: ): { decisions: GuardDecision[]; hardReasons: string[]; anyDemote: boolean } { 144: const decisions: GuardDecision[] = []; 145: const hardReasons: string[] = []; 146: let anyDemote = false; 147: 148: for (const spec of specs) { 149: const decision = evaluateNegativeGuard(spec, originalText); 150: decisions.push(decision); 151: if (decision.kind === 'demote_to_medium_with_confirmation') { 152: anyDemote = true; 153: } 154: } 155: 156: // Use semantic-direction analyzer to detect contradictions 157: const semanticAnalysis = analyzeSemanticDirection(originalText); 158: if (semanticAnalysis.direction === 'contradictory') { 159: hardReasons.push(semanticAnalysis.explanation); 160: } 161: 162: return { decisions, hardReasons, anyDemote }; 163: } 1: /** 2: * semantic-direction.ts — Unified Semantic Direction Analyzer 3: * 4: * Extracts semantic direction from Vietnamese constraint text. This module 5: * is used by ALL parser paths (built-in-suggestion, constraint-retriever, 6: * ir-first-parser, negative-guard, shadow-mode) to ensure consistent 7: * semantic interpretation across the entire pipeline. 8: * 9: * Design: 10: * - Pure function (no side effects) 11: * - Pattern-based (no LLM) 12: * - Deterministic 13: * - Handles Vietnamese canonical phrases (accented + unaccented) 14: * 15: * Semantic direction taxonomy: 16: * - 'require': positive intent (phải có, cần có, ít nhất, bắt buộc) 17: * - 'block': negative intent (không, cấm, nghỉ, tránh) 18: * - 'only': set-restricting positive (chỉ, chỉ dạy, chỉ học, chỉ được) 19: * - 'prefer': soft preference (nên, ưu tiên, thích, muốn) 20: * - 'unknown': no clear direction markers detected 21: * - 'contradictory': both positive and negative markers present 22: */ 23: 24: export type SemanticDirection = 'require' | 'block' | 'only' | 'prefer' | 'unknown' | 'contradictory'; 25: 26: export type SemanticAnalysis = { 27: /** Primary semantic direction detected. */ 28: direction: SemanticDirection; 29: /** Confidence score (0-1) based on marker strength and context. */ 30: confidence: number; 31: /** Matched markers (for debugging/logging). */ 32: matched: { 33: require: string[]; 34: block: string[]; 35: only: string[]; 36: prefer: string[]; 37: }; 38: /** Whether multiple conflicting directions were detected. */ 39: hasConflict: boolean; 40: /** Human-readable explanation of the decision. */ 41: explanation: string; 42: }; 43: 44: // ─── Marker Patterns ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── 45: 46: /** 47: * Unicode-aware word boundary lookarounds. 48: * ` ` in JS regex is ASCII-only and breaks for Vietnamese diacritics 49: * (e.g., "nghỉ" — the   between "ú" and "n" fails). We use 50: * lookarounds on Unicode letters/marks so boundaries work for VI text. 51: */ 52: const WORD_BOUNDARY = { 53: start: '(?<![\p{L}\p{M}\d_])', 54: end: '(?![\p{L}\p{M}\d_])', 55: }; 56: const BOUNDED = (inner: string): string => 57: `${WORD_BOUNDARY.start}${inner}${WORD_BOUNDARY.end}`; 58: 59: /** REQUIRE markers: positive intent, at-least semantics. */ 60: export const REQUIRE_PATTERNS = [ 61: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('ph[ảa]i\s+c[óo]'), 'iu'), marker: 'phải có', weight: 1.0 }, 62: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('phai\s+co'), 'iu'), marker: 'phai co', weight: 1.0 }, 63: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('c[ầa]n\s+c[óo]'), 'iu'), marker: 'cần có', weight: 1.0 }, 64: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('can\s+co'), 'iu'), marker: 'can co', weight: 1.0 }, 65: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('[íi]t\s+nh[ấa]t'), 'iu'), marker: 'ít nhất', weight: 0.95 }, 66: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('it\s+nhat'), 'iu'), marker: 'it nhat', weight: 0.95 }, 67: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('c[óo]\s+[íi]t\s+nh[ấa]t'), 'iu'), marker: 'có ít nhất', weight: 1.0 }, 68: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('co\s+it\s+nhat'), 'iu'), marker: 'co it nhat', weight: 1.0 }, 69: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('b[ắa]t\s+bu[ộo]c\s+(c[óo]|ph[ảa]i)'), 'iu'), marker: 'bắt buộc', weight: 1.0 }, 70: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('bat\s+buoc\s+(co|phai)'), 'iu'), marker: 'bat buoc', weight: 1.0 }, 71: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('b[ắa]t\s+bu[ộo]c'), 'iu'), marker: 'bắt buộc', weight: 1.0 }, 72: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('ph[ảa]i\s+đ[ượu][ợo]c'), 'iu'), marker: 'phải được', weight: 1.0 }, 73: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('phai\s+duoc'), 'iu'), marker: 'phai duoc', weight: 1.0 }, 74: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('nh[ấa]t\s+đ[ịi]nh\s+ph[ảa]i'), 'iu'), marker: 'nhất định phải', weight: 1.0 }, 75: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('nhat\s+dinh\s+phai'), 'iu'), marker: 'nhat dinh phai', weight: 1.0 }, 76: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('t[ốo]i\s+thi[ểe]u'), 'iu'), marker: 'tối thiểu', weight: 0.9 }, 77: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('toi\s+thieu'), 'iu'), marker: 'toi thieu', weight: 0.9 }, 78: ] as const; 79: 80: /** BLOCK markers: negative intent, forbid semantics. */ 81: export const BLOCK_PATTERNS = [ 82: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('kh[ôo]ng\s+(d[ạa]y|h[ọo]c|đ[ượu][ợo]c|x[ếe]p)'), 'iu'), marker: 'không', weight: 1.0 }, 83: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('khong\s+(day|hoc|duoc|xep)'), 'iu'), marker: 'khong', weight: 1.0 }, 84: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('ko\s+(d[ạa]y|h[ọo]c|đ[ượu][ợo]c)'), 'iu'), marker: 'ko', weight: 1.0 }, 85: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('c[ấa]m'), 'iu'), marker: 'cấm', weight: 1.0 }, 86: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('cam'), 'iu'), marker: 'cam', weight: 1.0 }, 87: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('ngh[ỉi]'), 'iu'), marker: 'nghỉ', weight: 0.95 }, 88: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('nghi'), 'iu'), marker: 'nghi', weight: 0.95 }, 89: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('đ[ừu]ng'), 'iu'), marker: 'đừng', weight: 1.0 }, 90: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('dung'), 'iu'), marker: 'dung', weight: 0.8 }, 91: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('tr[áa]nh'), 'iu'), marker: 'tránh', weight: 0.9 }, 92: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('tranh'), 'iu'), marker: 'tranh', weight: 0.9 }, 93: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('n[ée]'), 'iu'), marker: 'né', weight: 0.85 }, 94: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('ne'), 'iu'), marker: 'ne', weight: 0.85 }, 95: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('d[ừu]ng'), 'iu'), marker: 'dừng', weight: 0.9 }, 96: ] as const; 97: 98: /** ONLY markers: positive set-restricting (whitelist) semantics. */ 99: const ONLY_PATTERNS = [ 100: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('ch[ỉi]\s+(d[ạa]y|h[ọo]c|đ[ượu][ợo]c|x[ếe]p|r[ảa]nh)'), 'iu'), marker: 'chỉ', weight: 1.0 }, 101: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('chi\s+(day|hoc|duoc|xep|ranh)'), 'iu'), marker: 'chi', weight: 1.0 }, 102: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('ch[ỉi]\s+đ[ượu][ợo]c\s+(d[ạa]y|h[ọo]c|x[ếe]p)'), 'iu'), marker: 'chỉ được', weight: 1.0 }, 103: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('chi\s+duoc\s+(day|hoc|xep)'), 'iu'), marker: 'chi duoc', weight: 1.0 }, 104: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('c[ốo]\s+đ[ịi]nh'), 'iu'), marker: 'cố định', weight: 0.95 }, 105: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('co\s+dinh'), 'iu'), marker: 'co dinh', weight: 0.95 }, 106: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('whitelist'), 'iu'), marker: 'whitelist', weight: 1.0 }, 107: ] as const; 108: 109: /** PREFER markers: soft preference, not hard constraint. */ 110: const PREFER_PATTERNS = [ 111: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('n[êe]n'), 'iu'), marker: 'nên', weight: 0.9 }, 112: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('nen'), 'iu'), marker: 'nen', weight: 0.9 }, 113: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('[ưu]u\s+ti[êe]n'), 'iu'), marker: 'ưu tiên', weight: 0.95 }, 114: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('uu\s+tien'), 'iu'), marker: 'uu tien', weight: 0.95 }, 115: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('th[íi]ch'), 'iu'), marker: 'thích', weight: 0.9 }, 116: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('thich'), 'iu'), marker: 'thich', weight: 0.9 }, 117: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('mu[ốo]n'), 'iu'), marker: 'muốn', weight: 0.85 }, 118: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('muon'), 'iu'), marker: 'muon', weight: 0.85 }, 119: { pattern: new RegExp(BOUNDED('prefer'), 'iu'), marker: 'prefer', weight: 0.95 }, 120: ] as const; 121: 122: // ─── Analyzer ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 123: 124: function matchPatterns(text: string, patterns: readonly { pattern: RegExp; marker: string; weight: number }[]): { markers: string[]; score: number } { 125: const markers: string[] = []; 126: let score = 0; 127: for (const p of patterns) { 128: if (p.pattern.test(text)) { 129: markers.push(p.marker); 130: score = Math.max(score, p.weight); 131: } 132: } 133: return { markers, score }; 134: } 135: 136: /** 137: * Analyze the semantic direction of a Vietnamese constraint text. 138: * 139: * @param text raw user input (Vietnamese) 140: * @returns semantic analysis with direction + confidence 141: */ 142: export function analyzeSemanticDirection(text: string): SemanticAnalysis { 143: const normalized = text.normalize('NFC').replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim(); 144: 145: const requireMatch = matchPatterns(normalized, REQUIRE_PATTERNS); 146: const blockMatch = matchPatterns(normalized, BLOCK_PATTERNS); 147: const onlyMatch = matchPatterns(normalized, ONLY_PATTERNS); 148: const preferMatch = matchPatterns(normalized, PREFER_PATTERNS); 149: 150: const matched = { 151: require: requireMatch.markers, 152: block: blockMatch.markers, 153: only: onlyMatch.markers, 154: prefer: preferMatch.markers, 155: }; 156: 157: const scores = { 158: require: requireMatch.score, 159: block: blockMatch.score, 160: only: onlyMatch.score, 161: prefer: preferMatch.score, 162: }; 163: 164: // Conflict detection: if both require and block are present, mark contradictory 165: const hasRequire = requireMatch.markers.length > 0; 166: const hasBlock = blockMatch.markers.length > 0; 167: const hasOnly = onlyMatch.markers.length > 0; 168: const hasPrefer = preferMatch.markers.length > 0; 169: 170: if (hasRequire && hasBlock) { 171: return { 172: direction: 'contradictory', 173: confidence: 0, 174: matched, 175: hasConflict: true, 176: explanation: `Câu vừa chứa mỏ neo yêu cầu (${requireMatch.markers.join(', ')}) vừa chứa mỏ neo phủ định (${blockMatch.markers.join(', ')}) — không thể tự quyết định nghĩa.`, 177: }; 178: } 179: 180: // Prefer "require" over "only" when both are present (e.g., "phải có chỉ tiết 4") 181: // This is a rare edge case; in practice the user will clarify. 182: if (hasRequire && hasOnly) { 183: // If require score is higher, pick require. Otherwise pick only. 184: if (scores.require >= scores.only) { 185: return { 186: direction: 'require', 187: confidence: scores.require * 0.9, // penalize ambiguity 188: matched, 189: hasConflict: true, 190: explanation: `Câu có cả "phải có" và "chỉ" — chọn require vì điểm cao hơn (${scores.require} vs ${scores.only}).`, 191: }; 192: } else { 193: return { 194: direction: 'only', 195: confidence: scores.only * 0.9, 196: matched, 197: hasConflict: true, 198: explanation: `Câu có cả "phải có" và "chỉ" — chọn only vì điểm cao hơn (${scores.only} vs ${scores.require}).`, 199: }; 200: } 201: } 202: 203: // Prefer "only" over "block" when both are present (e.g., "chỉ dạy không dạy" is rare) 204: if (hasOnly && hasBlock) { 205: if (scores.only >= scores.block) { 206: return { 207: direction: 'only', 208: confidence: scores.only * 0.9, 209: matched, 210: hasConflict: true, 211: explanation: `Câu có cả "chỉ" và "không" — chọn only vì điểm cao hơn (${scores.only} vs ${scores.block}).`, 212: }; 213: } else { 214: return { 215: direction: 'block', 216: confidence: scores.block * 0.9, 217: matched, 218: hasConflict: true, 219: explanation: `Câu có cả "chỉ" và "không" — chọn block vì điểm cao hơn (${scores.block} vs ${scores.only}).`, 220: }; 221: } 222: } 223: 224: // Single direction detected 225: if (hasRequire) { 226: return { 227: direction: 'require', 228: confidence: scores.require, 229: matched, 230: hasConflict: false, 231: explanation: `Câu chứa mỏ neo yêu cầu: ${requireMatch.markers.join(', ')}`, 232: }; 233: } 234: 235: if (hasBlock) { 236: return { 237: direction: 'block', 238: confidence: scores.block, 239: matched, 240: hasConflict: false, 241: explanation: `Câu chứa mỏ neo phủ định: ${blockMatch.markers.join(', ')}`, 242: }; 243: } 244: 245: if (hasOnly) { 246: return { 247: direction: 'only', 248: confidence: scores.only, 249: matched, 250: hasConflict: false, 251: explanation: `Câu chứa mỏ neo giới hạn: ${onlyMatch.markers.join(', ')}`, 252: }; 253: } 254: 255: if (hasPrefer) { 256: return { 257: direction: 'prefer', 258: confidence: scores.prefer, 259: matched, 260: hasConflict: false, 261: explanation: `Câu chứa mỏ neo ưu tiên: ${preferMatch.markers.join(', ')}`, 262: }; 263: } 264: 265: // No direction detected 266: return { 267: direction: 'unknown', 268: confidence: 0, 269: matched, 270: hasConflict: false, 271: explanation: 'Không phát hiện mỏ neo ngữ nghĩa rõ ràng', 272: }; 273: } 274: 275: /** 276: * Check if a text contains a require marker (convenience wrapper). 277: */ 278: export function hasRequireMarker(text: string): boolean { 279: return analyzeSemanticDirection(text).matched.require.length > 0; 280: } 281: 282: /** 283: * Check if a text contains a block marker (convenience wrapper). 284: */ 285: export function hasBlockMarker(text: string): boolean { 286: return analyzeSemanticDirection(text).matched.block.length > 0; 287: } 288: 289: /** 290: * Check if a text contains an only marker (convenience wrapper). 291: */ 292: export function hasOnlyMarker(text: string): boolean { 293: return analyzeSemanticDirection(text).matched.only.length > 0; 294: } 295: 296: /** 297: * Check if a text contains a prefer marker (convenience wrapper). 298: */ 299: export function hasPreferMarker(text: string): boolean { 300: return analyzeSemanticDirection(text).matched.prefer.length > 0; 301: } 302: 303: /** 304: * Check if a text has contradictory markers (convenience wrapper). 305: */ 306: export function hasContradiction(text: string): boolean { 307: return analyzeSemanticDirection(text).direction === 'contradictory'; 308: } 1: /** 2: * shadow-mode.ts — Phase 2.3 3: * 4: * The new IR-first pipeline runs in PARALLEL with the legacy built-in 5: * pipeline. The legacy pipeline's output is the AUTHORITATIVE one for 6: * the user; the IR-first pipeline's output is LOGGED for divergence 7: * analysis. This file manages that logging. 8: * 9: * Divergence taxonomy: 10: * - 'kind_mismatch': legacy emitted a built-in kind, new emitted a 11: * different kind for the same sentence. 12: * - 'param_mismatch': same kind, but different params (e.g. legacy 13: * mapped "ít nhất" to count=0; new mapped to count=1). 14: * - 'silent_flip': legacy mapped a require-marker sentence to a 15: * *_block_* kind. CRITICAL — must be zero before flipping. 16: * - 'clarification_diff': one asked for clarification, the other did not. 17: * 18: * Each log entry includes: 19: * - raw text 20: * - legacy kind + params 21: * - new kind + params (or new expr kind) 22: * - divergence category 23: * - timestamp 24: * 25: * The log is consumed by the divergence analyzer (CLI / CI) to compute: 26: * - silent-flip rate (must be 0 to flip the parser) 27: * - clarification rate 28: * - kind-mismatch rate 29: * - param-mismatch rate 30: * 31: * NOTE: This module now uses semantic-direction.ts for unified marker 32: * detection across all parser paths. 33: */ 34: 35: import type { ConstraintSpec } from './constraint-spec'; 36: import { analyzeSemanticDirection } from './semantic-direction'; 37: 38: export type DivergenceCategory = 39: | 'kind_mismatch' 40: | 'param_mismatch' 41: | 'silent_flip' 42: | 'clarification_diff' 43: | 'no_legacy_spec' 44: | 'no_new_spec' 45: | 'match'; 46: 47: export type ShadowLogEntry = { 48: /** Frozen ID. */ 49: id: string; 50: /** Raw user input. */ 51: rawText: string; 52: /** Legacy pipeline output (the one the user sees). */ 53: legacy?: { 54: specs: ConstraintSpec[]; 55: status: 'mapped_builtin' | 'semantic_only' | 'needs_clarification' | 'unsupported'; 56: }; 57: /** New IR-first pipeline output. */ 58: new?: { 59: specs: ConstraintSpec[]; 60: status: 'mapped_builtin' | 'semantic_only' | 'needs_clarification' | 'unsupported'; 61: }; 62: /** Computed divergence category. */ 63: divergence: DivergenceCategory; 64: /** Human-readable explanation. */ 65: explanation: string; 66: /** When the divergence was detected. */ 67: timestamp: string; 68: }; 69: 70: export type ShadowLogOptions = { 71: /** Max number of entries to keep in memory. Older entries are evicted. */ 72: maxEntries?: number; 73: /** Whether to log the same divergence multiple times. */ 74: deduplicate?: boolean; 75: }; 76: 77: export class ShadowLogger { 78: private entries: ShadowLogEntry[] = []; 79: private seenKeys = new Set<string>(); 80: private maxEntries: number; 81: private deduplicate: boolean; 82: 83: constructor(options: ShadowLogOptions = {}) { 84: this.maxEntries = options.maxEntries ?? 5000; 85: this.deduplicate = options.deduplicate ?? true; 86: } 87: 88: log(entry: Omit<ShadowLogEntry, 'id' | 'timestamp'>): ShadowLogEntry { 89: const full: ShadowLogEntry = { 90: ...entry, 91: id: `slog_${Date.now()}_${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8)}`, 92: timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), 93: }; 94: if (this.deduplicate) { 95: const key = `${entry.rawText}::${entry.divergence}`; 96: if (this.seenKeys.has(key)) { 97: return full; 98: } 99: this.seenKeys.add(key); 100: } 101: this.entries.push(full); 102: if (this.entries.length > this.maxEntries) { 103: this.entries.shift(); 104: } 105: return full; 106: } 107: 108: getEntries(): ReadonlyArray<ShadowLogEntry> { 109: return this.entries; 110: } 111: 112: summarize(): { 113: total: number; 114: silentFlipCount: number; 115: kindMismatchCount: number; 116: paramMismatchCount: number; 117: clarificationDiffCount: number; 118: matchCount: number; 119: silentFlipRate: number; 120: } { 121: const total = this.entries.length; 122: let silentFlipCount = 0; 123: let kindMismatchCount = 0; 124: let paramMismatchCount = 0; 125: let clarificationDiffCount = 0; 126: let matchCount = 0; 127: for (const e of this.entries) { 128: if (e.divergence === 'silent_flip') silentFlipCount += 1; 129: else if (e.divergence === 'kind_mismatch') kindMismatchCount += 1; 130: else if (e.divergence === 'param_mismatch') paramMismatchCount += 1; 131: else if (e.divergence === 'clarification_diff') clarificationDiffCount += 1; 132: else if (e.divergence === 'match') matchCount += 1; 133: } 134: return { 135: total, 136: silentFlipCount, 137: kindMismatchCount, 138: paramMismatchCount, 139: clarificationDiffCount, 140: matchCount, 141: silentFlipRate: total > 0 ? _soft_weight(spec) 564: for a in assignments: 565: if a["subject"] != subj or a["class"] not in target_classes: 566: continue 567: # Rule A: require only floor(weeklyPeriods / length) consecutive blocks. 568: # Any remainder (weeklyPeriods % length) may be scheduled as loose periods; 569: # do not require every subject period to belong to a consecutive block. 570: required_runs = int(a.get("weeklyPeriods", 0)) // length 571: if required_runs <= 0: 572: continue 573: run_vars = [] 574: run_positions = [] # (day, start_period_index) for non-overlap enforcement 575: for d in days: 576: day_periods = _periods_for(d) 577: day_runs = [] 578: for i in range(0, max(0, len(day_periods) - length + 1)): 579: window = day_periods[i:i + length] 580: if len(window) != length or any(window[k + 1] != window[k] + 1 for k in range(len(window) - 1)): 581: continue 582: run = model.NewBoolVar(f"run_{a['id']}_{d}_{window[0]}_{length}") 583: window_vars = _slot_vars(_slot_var(a, d, within `length` steps of each other 589: # cannot both be active (aligns with validator's floor(streak/length)). 590: for j in range(len(day_runs)): 591: for k in range(j + 1, len(day_runs)): 592: if string; day: string; period: number } 92: | { op: 'teacher_pair_teaches_same_slot'; teachers: [string, string]; day: string; period: number } 93: | { op: 'teacher_pair_teaches_same_day'; teachers: [string, string]; day: string } 94: | { op: 'class_teacher_at_slot'; class: string; subject: string; day: string; period: number } 95: | { op: 'and'; args: ConditionExpr[] } 96: | { op: 'or'; args: ConditionExpr[] } 97: | { op: 'not'; arg: ConditionExpr }; 98: 99: export type ConstraintSeverity = 'hard' | 'soft' | 'info'; 100: export type ConstraintTag = 'auto_base' | 'user_required' | 'user_preferred'; 101: 102: export type ConstraintSpec = { 103: id: string; 104: original: string; 105: severity: ConstraintSeverity; 106: kind: ConstraintKind; 107: params: Record<string, unknown>; 108: /** Trọng số cho soft constraint. Mặc định 1 nếu bỏ trống. */ 109: weight?: number; 110: tags?: ConstraintTag[]; 111: notes?: string; 112: /** Chỉ dùng cho kind='custom_dsl': mã Python nhận schedule và trả true/false hoặc list violation */ 113: pythonPredicate?: string; 114: }; 115: 116: export type Plan = { 117: decisionVars: string; 118: domainSize: { 119: classes: number; 120: days: number; 121: periods: number; 122: estimated?: number; 123: estimatedVars?: number; 124: }; 125: constraintOrder: string[]; 126: reifiedNeeded: string[]; 127: objective: 'none' | 'maximize_soft' | 'minimize_gaps'; 128: templatesUsed: string[]; 129: objectiveFunction?: string; 130: provenPatterns?: string[]; 131: risks: string[]; 132: }; 133: 134: export type ScheduleEntry = { 135: assignmentId?: string; 136: class: string; 137: day: string; 138: period: number | string; 139: subject: string; 140: teacher: string; 141: }; 142: 143: export type Violation = { 144: constraintId: string; 145: kind: ConstraintKind | 'base_constraint'; 146: message: string; 147: offendingEntries: ScheduleEntry[]; 148: }; 149: 150: export type DeterministicValidationContext = { 151: assignments?: Array<{ 152: id: string; 153: class: string; 154: subject: string; 155: teacher: string; 156: weeklyPeriods: number; 157: }>; 158: }; 159: 160: export type DeterministicValidationReport = { 161: ok: boolean; 162: baseConstraintPass: boolean; 163: hardConstraintPass: boolean; 164: softConstraintPass: boolean; 165: /** True khi MỌI hard constraint đều có checker thực sự kiểm (fail-closed). */ 166: hardCoverageComplete: boolean; 167: violations: Violation[]; 168: hardViolations: Violation[]; 169: softViolations: Violation[]; 170: uncheckedConstraintIds: string[]; 171: /** Các hard constraint không được deterministic check (custom_dsl/không có checker). */ 172: hardUncheckedConstraintIds: string[]; 173: }; 1: /** 2: * ir-first-parser.ts — Phase 2.1 3: * 4: * Tier-1 deterministic pattern-based parser that outputs the IR DIRECTLY 5: * (not a built-in kind). This is the new fast-path that the IR-first 6: * pipeline uses. 7: * 8: * Architecture (B2): 9: * [1] Disambiguation table -> direction (require / only / block / soft_prefer) 10: * [2] Resolver hints (entity + number) from Stage 1 11: * [3] Pattern matching for the common require/only/block families 12: * [4] Output: ConstraintIR (or `null` to indicate "I don't know, escalate 13: * to Tier-2 LLM") 14: * 15: * This module is SHADOW-MODE only in Phase 2. The legacy built-in 16: * parser (built-in-suggestion.ts + analyze-constraint-service.ts) is 17: * still the AUTHORITATIVE parser. The new parser runs in parallel and 18: * logs divergence via shadow-mode.ts. Flipping the parser to be 19: * authoritative is Phase 4 work, gated on: 20: * - silentFlipRate === 0 on the shadow log 21: * - clarification rate < 25% on golden V2 22: * - all frozen regression cases still pass 23: * 24: * Why the parser is a function and not a stateful class: 25: * - Deterministic / pure / no LLM 26: * - Easy to test 27: * - Easy to wrap in shadow mode 28: */ 29: 30: import type { BoolExpr, ConstraintIR } from './constraint-ir'; 31: import { validateIR } from './constraint-ir'; 32: import { normalizeConstraintText } from './translator-text'; 33: import { findDisambiguationMatch } from './disambiguation-table'; 34: import { evaluateNegativeGuard } from './negative-guard'; 35: import type { ConstraintSpec } from './constraint-spec'; 36: import type { ConstraintResolverHints } from './constraint-retriever'; 37: import { analyzeSemanticDirection } from './semantic-direction'; 38: 39: export type IRFirstParseResult = 40: | { kind: 'ir'; ir: ConstraintIR; spec: ConstraintSpec } 41: | { kind: 'needs_clarification'; reason: string; candidates?: Array<{ kind: string; params: Record<string, unknown> }> } 42: | { kind: 'escalate_to_tier2'; reason: string }; 43: 44: /** 45: * Build a ConstraintSpec from a direction + extracted params. The spec 46: * is the LEGACY form (so we can compare divergence against the legacy 47: * parser). The IR is the new canonical form. 48: */ 49: function specFromIR(ir: ConstraintIR, kind: string, params: Record<string, unknown>): ConstraintSpec { 50: return { 51: id: ir.id, 52: original: ir.original, 53: severity: ir.severity as 'hard' | 'soft' | 'info', 54: kind: kind as ConstraintSpec['kind'], 55: params, 56: ...(ir.weight !== undefined ? { weight: ir.weight } : {}), 57: }; 58: } 59: 60: /** 61: * Extract the period number from a sentence. Returns the first positive 62: * integer that follows "tiết". Returns null if no period is found. 63: */ 64: function extractPeriod(rawText: string, normalized: string): number | null { 65: // Look for "tiết N" or "tiết thứ N" patterns. 66: const m = normalized.match(/tiet\s+(\d+)/u); 67: if (m) return Number(m[1]); 68: // Also try "tiết đầu" = 1. 69: if (/tiet\s+dau|tiet\s+1/u.test(normalized)) return 1; 70: // Look for "tiết cuối" — caller will need to resolve to actual count later. 71: return null; 72: } 73: 74: function extractPeriods(normalized: string): number[] { 75: const periods: number[] = []; 76: const periodMatches = normalized.matchAll(/tiet\s+(\d+)/gu); 77: for (const m of periodMatches) { 78: periods.push(Number(m[1])); 79: } 80: return periods; 81: } 82: 83: function buildIR(args: { 84: id: string; 85: original: string; 86: severity: 'hard' | 'soft' | 'info'; 87: weight?: number; 88: expr: BoolExpr; 89: explain?: string; 90: }): ConstraintIR { 91: const result: ConstraintIR = { 92: id: args.id, 93: severity: args.severity, 94: original: args.original, 95: expr: args.expr, 96: }; 97: if (args.weight !== undefined) result.weight = args.weight; 98: if (args.explain !== undefined) result.explain = args.explain; 99: return result; 100: } 101: 102: /** 103: * Try to parse the sentence as a require-family constraint 104: * (phải có | cần có | ít nhất + period/day) for the given teacher. 105: * 106: * Returns the IR or null if the sentence doesn't match. 107: */ 108: function tryParseRequireTeacher( 109: rawText: string, 110: normalized: string, 111: teacher: string, 112: hints: ConstraintResolverHints 113: ): ConstraintIR | null { 114: // Use semantic direction analyzer instead of disambiguation table 115: const semanticAnalysis = analyzeSemanticDirection(rawText); 116: if (semanticAnalysis.direction !== 'require') return null; 117: 118: // Must be a teacher-scope sentence. 119: if (hints.inferredScope && hints.inferredScope !== 'teacher') return null; 120: 121: const period = extractPeriod(rawText, normalized); 122: if (period === null) return null; 123: 124: const minCount = hints.extractedNumber ?? 1; 125: 126: const ir = buildIR({ 127: id: 'ir_first_teacher_required_period', 128: original: rawText, 129: severity: 'hard', 130: expr: { 131: atLeast: { 132: k: minCount, 133: var: 'd', 134: in: 'days', 135: body: { teaches: { teacher, day: '$$D$$', period } }, 136: }, 137: }, 138: explain: `Giáo viên ${teacher} phải có ít nhất ${minCount} tiết ${period} trong tuần`, 139: }); 140: return ir; 141: } 142: 143: function tryParseRequireClass( 144: rawText: string, 145: normalized: string, 146: klass: string, 147: hints: ConstraintResolverHints 148: ): ConstraintIR | null { 149: const semanticAnalysis = analyzeSemanticDirection(rawText); 150: if (semanticAnalysis.direction !== 'require') return null; 151: if (hints.inferredScope && hints.inferredScope !== 'class') return null; 152: const period = extractPeriod(rawText, normalized); 153: if (period === null) return null; 154: const minCount = hints.extractedNumber ?? 1; 155: return buildIR({ 156: id: 'ir_first_class_required_period', 157: original: rawText, 158: severity: 'hard', 159: expr: { 160: atLeast: { 161: k: minCount, 162: var: 'd', 163: in: 'days', 164: body: { classBusy: { class: klass, day: '$$D$$', period } }, 165: }, 166: }, 167: explain: `Lớp ${klass} phải có ít nhất ${minCount} tiết ${period} trong tuần`, 168: }); 169: } 170: 171: function tryParseRequireSubject( 172: rawText: string, 173: normalized: string, 174: subject: string, 175: hints: ConstraintResolverHints 176: ): ConstraintIR | null { 177: const semanticAnalysis = analyzeSemanticDirection(rawText); 178: if (semanticAnalysis.direction !== 'require') return null; 179: if (hints.inferredScope && hints.inferredScope !== 'subject') return null; 180: const period = extractPeriod(rawText, normalized); 181: if (period === null) return null; 182: const minCount = hints.extractedNumber ?? 1; 183: return buildIR({ 184: id: 'ir_first_subject_required_period', 185: original: rawText, 186: severity: 'hard', 187: expr: { 188: atLeast: { 189: k: minCount, 190: var: 'd', 191: in: 'days', 192: body: { 193: forall: { 194: var: 'c', 195: in: 'classes', 196: body: { classSubjectAt: { class: '$$C$$', subject, day: '$$D$$', period } }, 197: }, 198: }, 199: }, 200: }, 201: explain: `Mỗi lớp, mỗi ngày: số tiết môn ${subject} ở tiết ${period} ≥ ${minCount}`, 202: }); 203: } 204: 205: function tryParseBlockTeacher( 206: rawText: string, 207: normalized: string, 208: teacher: string, 209: hints: ConstraintResolverHints 210: ): ConstraintIR | null { 211: const semanticAnalysis = analyzeSemanticDirection(rawText); 212: if (semanticAnalysis.direction !== 'block') return null; 213: if (hints.inferredScope && hints.inferredScope !== 'teacher') return null; 214: const period = extractPeriod(rawText, normalized); 215: if (period === null) return null; 216: return buildIR({ 217: id: 'ir_first_teacher_block_period', 218: original: rawText, 219: severity: 'hard', 220: expr: { 221: not: { 222: forall: { 223: var: 'd', 224: in: 'days', 225: body: { teaches: { teacher, day: '$$D$$', period } }, 226: }, 227: }, 228: }, 229: explain: `Giáo viên ${teacher} không dạy tiết ${period}`, 230: }); 231: } 232: 233: function tryParseOnlyTeacher( 234: rawText: string, 235: normalized: string, 236: teacher: string, 237: hints: ConstraintResolverHints 238: ): ConstraintIR | null { 239: const semanticAnalysis = analyzeSemanticDirection(rawText); 240: if (semanticAnalysis.direction !== 'only') return null; 241: if (hints.inferredScope && hints.inferredScope !== 'teacher') return null; 242: // Allowed: "chỉ dạy tiết 4" or "chỉ dạy các tiết 2, 3, 4" 243: const periods = extractPeriods(normalized); 244: if (periods.length === 0) return null; 245: return buildIR({ 246: id: 'ir_first_teacher_allowed_periods', 247: original: rawText, 248: severity: 'hard', 249: expr: { 250: forall: { 251: var: 'd', 252: in: 'days', 253: body: { 254: implies: [ 255: { teaches: { teacher, day: '$$D$$', period: '$$P$$' } }, 256: { const: false }, // sentinel; allowed_periods is a positive-set kind 257: ] as [BoolExpr, BoolExpr], 258: }, 259: }, 260: }, 261: explain: `Giáo viên ${teacher} chỉ dạy các tiết ${periods.join(', ')}`, 262: }); 263: } 264: 265: function tryParseMaxPerDayTeacher( 266: rawText: string, 267: normalized: string, 268: teacher: string, 269: hints: ConstraintResolverHints 270: ): ConstraintIR | null { 271: // "tối đa N tiết mỗi ngày" with extractedNumber=N 272: if (!/toi\s*da|tối\s*đa/iu.test(normalized)) return null; 273: if (!/ngay|mot\s*ngay/iu.test(normalized)) return null; 274: if (!/tiet/iu.test(normalized)) return null; 275: if (hints.inferredScope && hints.inferredScope !== 'teacher') return null; 276: const n = hints.extractedNumber; 277: if (n === null) return null; 278: return buildIR({ 279: id: 'ir_first_teacher_max_per_day', 280: original: rawText, 281: severity: 'hard', 282: expr: { 283: forall: { 284: var: 'd', 285: in: 'days', 286: body: { 287: compare: { 288: op: '<=', 289: lhs: { 290: count: { 291: var: 'p', 292: in: 'periods', 293: body: { teaches: { teacher, day: '$$D$$', period: '$$P$$' } }, 294: }, 295: }, 296: rhs: n, 297: }, 298: }, 299: }, 300: }, 301: explain: `Với mỗi ngày: số tiết của giáo viên ${teacher} tối đa ${n}`, 302: }); 303: } 304: 305: /** 306: * Run the IR-first parser on a sentence with the given resolver hints. 307: * 308: * This is the public entry point. It tries each pattern in order and 309: * returns the first match. If no pattern matches, it returns 310: * `escalate_to_tier2` so the caller knows to invoke the LLM. 311: */ 312: export function parseIRFirst( 313: rawText: string, 314: hints: ConstraintResolverHints 315: ): IRFirstParseResult { 316: const normalized = normalizeConstraintText(rawText); 317: 318: // Try each require/block/only family. 319: const teacher = hints.resolvedTeacher; 320: const klass = hints.resolvedClass; 321: const subject = hints.resolvedSubject; 322: 323: if (teacher) { 324: const requireIr = tryParseRequireTeacher(rawText, normalized, teacher, hints); 325: if (requireIr) return { kind: 'ir', ir: requireIr, spec: specFromIR(requireIr, 'teacher_required_period', { teacher, period: extractPeriod(rawText, normalized) ?? 0, minCount: hints.extractedNumber ?? 1 }) }; 326: 327: const blockIr = tryParseBlockTeacher(rawText, normalized, teacher, hints); 328: if (blockIr) return { kind: 'ir', ir: blockIr, spec: specFromIR(blockIr, 'teacher_block_period', { teacher, period: extractPeriod(rawText, normalized) ?? 0 }) }; 329: 330: const onlyIr = tryParseOnlyTeacher(rawText, normalized, teacher, hints); 331: if (onlyIr) return { kind: 'ir', ir: onlyIr, spec: specFromIR(onlyIr, 'teacher_allowed_periods', { teacher, periods: extractPeriods(normalized) }) }; 332: 333: const maxIr = tryParseMaxPerDayTeacher(rawText, normalized, teacher, hints); 334: if (maxIr) return { kind: 'ir', ir: maxIr, spec: specFromIR(maxIr, 'teacher_max_per_day', { teacher, maxPerDay: hints.extractedNumber ?? 0 }) }; 335: } 336: 337: if (klass) { 338: const requireIr = tryParseRequireClass(rawText, normalized, klass, hints); 339: if (requireIr) return { kind: 'ir', ir: requireIr, spec: specFromIR(requireIr, 'class_required_period', { class: klass, period: extractPeriod(rawText, normalized) ?? 0, minCount: hints.extractedNumber ?? 1 }) }; 340: } 341: 342: if (subject) { 343: const requireIr = tryParseRequireSubject(rawText, normalized, subject, hints); 344: if (requireIr) return { kind: 'ir', ir: requireIr, spec: specFromIR(requireIr, 'subject_required_period', { subject, period: extractPeriod(rawText, normalized) ?? 0, minCount: hints.extractedNumber ?? 1 }) }; 345: } 346: 347: // Disambiguation table detected a row but no scope/period matched; 348: // surface as a clarification rather than escalate. 349: const disambig = findDisambiguationMatch(rawText); 350: if (disambig.length > 0 && !disambig[0].contradictory) { 351: const m = disambig[0]; 352: return { 353: kind: 'needs_clarification', 354: reason: `Câu có thể hiểu theo hướng ${m.direction}; cần thêm thông tin (giáo viên/lớp/môn, tiết cụ thể).`, 355: candidates: [ 356: { kind: m.row.positiveKinds[0], params: {} }, 357: { kind: m.row.negativeKinds[0], params: {} }, 358: ], 359: }; 360: } 361: 362: return { kind: 'escalate_to_tier2', reason: 'No Tier-1 pattern matched; LLM semantic parser should handle this.' }; 363: } 364: 365: /** 366: * Run the IR-first parser and run the negative-guard over the resulting 367: * spec (if any). This is the integration point that prevents silent 368: * flips. If the guard flags a flip, the result is demoted to 369: * `needs_clarification` and the user is asked to re-confirm. 370: */ 371: export function parseIRFirstWithGuard( 372: rawText: string, 373: hints: ConstraintResolverHints 374: ): IRFirstParseResult & { guardReason?: string } { 375: const result = parseIRFirst(rawText, hints); 376: if (result.kind !== 'ir') return result; 377: const decision = evaluateNegativeGuard(result.spec, rawText); 378: if (decision.kind === 'force_clarification') { 379: return { 380: kind: 'needs_clarification', 381: reason: decision.hardReasons.join('; '), 382: guardReason: decision.reason, 383: }; 384: } 385: if (decision.kind === 'demote_to_medium_with_confirmation') { 386: // Still return the IR, but flag for the caller to require confirmation. 387: return { ...result, guardReason: decision.reason }; 388: } 389: return result; 390: } 391: 392: /** 393: * Validate that a parseIRFirst result is semantically valid. The caller 394: * should run this before consuming the IR. 395: */ 396: export function validateIRFirstResult(result: IRFirstParseResult): string[] { 397: if (result.kind !== 'ir') return []; 398: return validateIR(result.ir).map((i) => `${i.path}: ${i.message}`); 399: } 1: /** 2: * ir-humanizer-v2.ts — Phase 1.3 3: * 4: * Deterministic IR-to-Vietnamese renderer. The output is stable for a 5: * given IR (the same IR always renders to the same Vietnamese string), 6: * and uses canonical patterns so the humanizer can be re-parsed back 7: * to the same IR (the "metamorphic" property in B5). 8: * 9: * The previous humanizer (constraint-humanizer.ts) was spec-kind-keyed 10: * (one branch per kind). This V2 humanizer is IR-shape-keyed: it 11: * walks the IR and emits Vietnamese. This is necessary because the IR 12: * may have been hand-authored or produced by the toIR() adapter and 13: * we want a single, consistent rendering path. 14: * 15: * Recognized canonical patterns (rendered in Vietnamese): 16: * - atLeast(1, days, teaches(T, d, p)) -> "Giáo viên T phải có ít nhất 1 tiết p trong tuần" 17: * - atLeast(k, days, teaches(T, d, p)) -> "Giáo viên T phải có ít nhất k tiết p trong tuần" 18: * - atMost(k, days, count(teaches)) -> "Giáo viên T dạy tối đa k tiết/ngày" 19: * - forall(days, compare(count, <=, k, ...)) -> ditto 20: * - not(forall(...)) -> "không ... trong ..." 21: * - and / or / not / implies -> rendered with "và" / "hoặc" / "không" / "nếu ... thì" 22: * - gap / before / after -> "cách nhau ít nhất N", "trước", "sau" 23: * - session { teacher, session } -> "Giáo viên T có tiết buổi S" 24: * 25: * Patterns that don't match a canonical template are rendered as a 26: * structural description ("Với mỗi d: ...") plus a flag for the caller 27: * to add a new template. The humanizer NEVER calls the LLM. 28: */ 29: 30: import type { BoolExpr, IntExpr, ConstraintIR, Domain } from './constraint-ir'; 31: 32: function domainToString(d: Domain): string { 33: if (typeof d === 'string') return d; 34: if ('list' in d) return `[${d.list.join(', ')}]`; 35: if ('range' in d) return `${d.range[0]}..${d.range[1]}`; 36: if ('in' in d) return `in(${domainToString(d.in)})`; 37: return '?'; 38: } 39: 40: function sessionLabel(sessionId: string): string { 41: const normalized = sessionId.toLowerCase(); 42: if (normalized === 'morning' || normalized === 'sang' || normalized === 'sáng') return 'sáng'; 43: if (normalized === 'afternoon' || normalized === 'chieu' || normalized === 'chiều') return 'chiều'; 44: return sessionId; 45: } 46: 47: function varSubstitute(expr: BoolExpr, varName: string, value: string | number): BoolExpr { 48: // Substitute a quantifier binding in an inner expression. Used by the 49: // canonical patterns to inline the var. 50: const sub = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(expr)) as BoolExpr; 51: function walk(e: BoolExpr): void { 52: if ('teaches' in e) { 53: // Substitute day or period if the value matches the binding. 54: const t = e.teaches; 55: if (t.day === `$$${varName}$$`) t.day = String(value); 56: if (t.period === `$$${varName}$$`) t.period = value; 57: } else if ('teachesOnDay' in e) { 58: if (e.teachesOnDay.day === `$$${varName}$$`) e.teachesOnDay.day = String(value); 59: } else if ('classBusy' in e) { 60: if (e.classBusy.day === `$$${varName}$$`) e.classBusy.day = String(value); 61: } else if ('classSubjectAt' in e) { 62: const c = e.classSubjectAt; 63: if (c.day === `$$${varName}$$`) c.day = String(value); 64: if (c.period === `$$${varName}$$`) c.period = value; 65: } else if ('and' in e) e.and.forEach(walk); 66: else if ('or' in e) e.or.forEach(walk); 67: else if ('not' in e) walk(e.not); 68: else if ('implies' in e) { 69: walk(e.implies[0]); 70: walk(e.implies[1]); 71: } else if ('iff' in e) { 72: walk(e.iff[0]); 73: walk(e.iff[1]); 74: } else if ('exists' in e) walk(e.exists.body); 75: else if ('forall' in e) walk(e.forall.body); 76: else if ('atLeast' in e) walk(e.atLeast.body); 77: else if ('atMost' in e) walk(e.atMost.body); 78: else if ('exactly' in e) walk(e.exactly.body); 79: else if ('compare' in e) { 80: // IntExpr — substitute scale/etc if it contains a var (not currently used). 81: } else if ('consecutive' in e) walk(e.consecutive.body); 82: else if ('gap' in e) walk(e.gap.body); 83: else if ('before' in e) { 84: walk(e.before.first); 85: walk(e.before.second); 86: } else if ('after' in e) { 87: walk(e.after.first); 88: walk(e.after.second); 89: } 90: } 91: walk(sub); 92: return sub; 93: } 94: 95: function isTeachesAtom(e: BoolExpr): e is { teaches: { teacher: string; day: string; period: string | number } } { 96: return 'teaches' in e; 97: } 98: 99: function isClassBusyAtom(e: BoolExpr): e is { classBusy: { class: string; day: string; period: string | number } } { 100: return 'classBusy' in e; 101: } 102: 103: function isClassSubjectAtAtom( 104: e: BoolExpr 105: ): e is { classSubjectAt: { class: string; subject: string; day: string; period: string | number } } { 106: return 'classSubjectAt' in e; 107: } 108: 109: function isForallDaysCompareCount( 110: e: BoolExpr 111: ): e is { 112: forall: { 113: var: string; 114: in: Domain; 115: body: { compare: { op: '<=' | '<' | '==' | '!=' | '>=' | '>'; lhs: { count: { var: string; in: Domain; body: BoolExpr } }; rhs: number } }; 116: }; 117: } { 118: if (!('forall' in e)) return false; 119: const f = e.forall; 120: if (typeof f.in !== 'string' || f.in !== 'days') return false; 121: if (!('compare' in f.body)) return false; 122: const c = f.body.compare; 123: if (typeof c.rhs !== 'number') return false; 124: if (typeof c.lhs !== 'object' || !('count' in c.lhs)) return false; 125: return true; 126: } 127: 128: function isAtLeast( 129: e: BoolExpr 130: ): e is { atLeast: { k: number; var: string; in: Domain; body: BoolExpr } } { 131: return 'atLeast' in e; 132: } 133: 134: function isNotForall( 135: e: BoolExpr 136: ): e is { not: { forall: { var: string; in: Domain; body: BoolExpr } } } { 137: if (!('not' in e)) return false; 138: return 'forall' in e.not; 139: } 140: 141: function isImplies(e: BoolExpr): e is { implies: [BoolExpr, BoolExpr] } { 142: return 'implies' in e; 143: } 144: 145: function isSessionAtom(e: BoolExpr): e is { session: { teacher?: string; class?: string; subject?: string; session: string } } { 146: return 'session' in e; 147: } 148: 149: function isGap(e: BoolExpr): e is { gap: { var: string; in: Domain; min: number; body: BoolExpr } } { 150: return 'gap' in e; 151: } 152: 153: function isBefore(e: BoolExpr): e is { before: { var: string; in: Domain; first: BoolExpr; second: BoolExpr } } { 154: return 'before' in e; 155: } 156: 157: function isAfter(e: BoolExpr): e is { after: { var: string; in: Domain; first: BoolExpr; second: BoolExpr } } { 158: return 'after' in e; 159: } 160: 161: /** 162: * Humanize a single BoolExpr to a Vietnamese string. The shape-keyed 163: * rendering is deterministic and stable. 164: * 165: * When a shape does not match any canonical pattern, the humanizer 166: * returns a structural description like "Với mỗi d ∈ days: ..." and 167: * the caller can use the `unmatched` flag to add a new template. 168: */ 169: export function humanizeIRExpr(expr: BoolExpr): { text: string; unmatched: boolean } { 170: // Pattern: atLeast(1, days, teaches(T, d, p)) -> require at least 1 171: if (isAtLeast(expr)) { 172: const al = expr.atLeast; 173: if (typeof al.in === 'string' && al.in === 'days' && isTeachesAtom(al.body)) { 174: const t = al.body.teaches; 175: // Match if the period is the bound var (either case) OR a literal. 176: const isVarDay = t.day === `$$${al.var}$$` || t.day === `$$${al.var.toUpperCase()}$$`; 177: const isVarPeriod = 178: t.period === `$$${al.var}$$` || t.period === `$$${al.var.toUpperCase()}$$`; 179: if (isVarDay && (isVarPeriod || typeof t.period === 'number')) { 180: return { 181: text: `Giáo viên ${t.teacher} phải có ít nhất ${al.k} tiết ${t.period} trong tuần`, 182: unmatched: false, 183: }; 184: } 185: } 186: if (typeof al.in === 'string' && al.in === 'days' && isClassBusyAtom(al.body)) { 187: const c = al.body.classBusy; 188: const isVarDay = c.day === `$$${al.var}$$` || c.day === `$$${al.var.toUpperCase()}$$`; 189: const isVarPeriod = 190: c.period === `$$${al.var}$$` || c.period === `$$${al.var.toUpperCase()}$$`; 191: if (isVarDay && (isVarPeriod || typeof c.period === 'number')) { 192: return { 193: text: `Lớp ${c.class} phải có ít nhất ${al.k} tiết ${c.period} trong tuần`, 194: unmatched: false, 195: }; 196: } 197: } 198: if (typeof al.in === 'string' && al.in === 'days' && isClassSubjectAtAtom(al.body)) { 199: const c = al.body.classSubjectAt; 200: return { 201: text: `Mỗi lớp, mỗi ngày: số tiết môn ${c.subject} ở tiết ${c.period} ≥ ${al.k}`, 202: unmatched: false, 203: }; 204: } 205: } 206: 207: // Pattern: not(forall(...)) -> "không (Với mỗi: ...)" 208: if (isNotForall(expr)) { 209: const f = expr.not.forall; 210: const inner = humanizeIRExpr(f.body); 211: if (typeof f.in === 'string') { 212: return { 213: text: `Không (với mỗi ${f.var} ∈ ${f.in}: ${inner.text})`, 214: unmatched: inner.unmatched, 215: }; 216: } 217: } 218: 219: // Pattern: forall(days, compare(count(...), <=, k)) -> "Giáo viên T dạy tối đa k tiết/ngày" 220: if (isForallDaysCompareCount(expr)) { 221: const f = expr.forall; 222: const c = f.body.compare; 223: const cnt = c.lhs.count; 224: if (typeof cnt.in === 'string' && cnt.in === 'periods' && isTeachesAtom(cnt.body)) { 225: const t = cnt.body.teaches; 226: const opWord = c.op === '<=' ? 'tối đa' : c.op === '>=' ? 'ít nhất' : c.op === '<' ? 'ít hơn' : c.op === '>' ? 'nhiều hơn' : c.op; 227: return { 228: text: `Với mỗi ngày: số tiết của giáo viên ${t.teacher} ${opWord} ${c.rhs}`, 229: unmatched: false, 230: }; 231: } 232: } 233: 234: // Pattern: implies 235: if (isImplies(expr)) { 236: const cond = humanizeIRExpr(expr.implies[0]); 237: const cons = humanizeIRExpr(expr.implies[1]); 238: return { 239: text: `Nếu ${cond.text} thì ${cons.text}`, 240: unmatched: cond.unmatched || cons.unmatched, 241: }; 242: } 243: 244: // Pattern: and / or / not 245: if ('and' in expr) { 246: const parts = expr.and.map(humanizeIRExpr); 247: return { text: parts.map((p) => p.text).join(' và '), unmatched: parts.some((p) => p.unmatched) }; 248: } 249: if ('or' in expr) { 250: const parts = expr.or.map(humanizeIRExpr); 251: return { text: parts.map((p) => p.text).join(' hoặc '), unmatched: parts.some((p) => p.unmatched) }; 252: } 253: if ('not' in expr) { 254: const inner = humanizeIRExpr(expr.not); 255: return { text: `không (${inner.text})`, unmatched: inner.unmatched }; 256: } 257: 258: // Pattern: gap 259: if (isGap(expr)) { 260: return { 261: text: `Với mỗi ${expr.gap.var} ∈ ${domainToString(expr.gap.in)}: khoảng cách ≥ ${expr.gap.min}`, 262: unmatched: false, 263: }; 264: } 265: if (isBefore(expr)) { 266: const first = humanizeIRExpr(expr.before.first); 267: const second = humanizeIRExpr(expr.before.second); 268: return { 269: text: `Với mỗi ${expr.before.var} ∈ ${domainToString(expr.before.in)}: ${first.text} trước ${second.text}`, 270: unmatched: first.unmatched || second.unmatched, 271: }; 272: } 273: if (isAfter(expr)) { 274: const first = humanizeIRExpr(expr.after.first); 275: const second = humanizeIRExpr(expr.after.second); 276: return { 277: text: `Với mỗi ${expr.after.var} ∈ ${domainToString(expr.after.in)}: ${first.text} sau ${second.text}`, 278: unmatched: first.unmatched || second.unmatched, 279: }; 280: } 281: 282: // Pattern: session atom 283: if (isSessionAtom(expr)) { 284: const s = expr.session; 285: const parts: string[] = []; 286: if (s.teacher) parts.push(`giáo viên ${s.teacher}`); 287: if (s.class) parts.push(`lớp ${s.class}`); 288: if (s.subject) parts.push(`môn ${s.subject}`); 289: return { 290: text: `${parts.join(', ') || 'mọi đối tượng'} có tiết trong buổi ${sessionLabel(s.session)}`, 291: unmatched: false, 292: }; 293: } 294: 295: // Pattern: teaches/class atoms 296: if (isTeachesAtom(expr)) { 297: return { 298: text: `giáo viên ${expr.teaches.teacher} dạy ngày ${expr.teaches.day} tiết ${expr.teaches.period}`, 299: unmatched: false, 300: }; 301: } 302: if (isClassBusyAtom(expr)) { 303: return { 304: text: `lớp ${expr.classBusy.class} có tiết ngày ${expr.classBusy.day} tiết ${expr.classBusy.period}`, 305: unmatched: false, 306: }; 307: } 308: if (isClassSubjectAtAtom(expr)) { 309: return { 310: text: `lớp ${expr.classSubjectAt.class} học môn ${expr.classSubjectAt.subject} ngày ${expr.classSubjectAt.day} tiết ${expr.classSubjectAt.period}`, 311: unmatched: false, 312: }; 313: } 314: if ('const' in expr) { 315: return { text: expr.const ? 'luôn đúng' : 'luôn sai', unmatched: false }; 316: } 317: return { text: '(không nhận dạng được)', unmatched: true }; 318: } 319: 320: /** 321: * Humanize a full ConstraintIR. Returns a stable Vietnamese string. 322: * If `ir.explain` is present, it is preferred (it was authored in Vietnamese 323: * by the specToIR adapter for known kinds). Otherwise the renderer walks 324: * `ir.expr`. 325: */ 326: export function humanizeIR(ir: ConstraintIR): { text: string; unmatched: boolean } { 327: if (ir.explain) { 328: return { text: ir.explain, unmatched: false }; 329: } 330: return humanizeIRExpr(ir.expr); 331: } 1: /** 2: * ir-type-checker.ts — Phase 1.2 3: * 4: * TS-side type checker for the Constraint IR. The zod schema in 5: * constraint-ir.ts validates the SHAPE; this module validates that 6: * the IR is SEMANTICALLY valid for a given AgentInput: 7: * 8: * - atom entities (teacher, class, subject) must exist in agentInput 9: * - period values must be in range for the day/session they appear in 10: * - day ids must exist in agentInput.days 11: * - session ids must exist in agentInput.sessions 12: * - binding variables in quantifiers (var, in, body) must be consistent 13: * - atLeast / atMost / exactly / count / gap / consecutive / forall / exists: 14: * k / length / min must be positive integers 15: * - before / after: first and second must reference the same day 16: * 17: * The checker is fail-closed. Every issue is a TYPE_CHECK_ERROR with 18: * a specific Vietnamese question (or technical message) that the UI 19: * can show to the user. The caller decides whether the constraint 20: * is rejected outright (hard type errors) or forced to re-enter 21: * clarification (soft type errors). 22: * 23: * NOTE: this module is intentionally separate from `validateIR` in 24: * constraint-ir.ts. `validateIR` is the SHAPE check (zod); this is 25: * the SEMANTIC check (entity existence, period range, etc.). 26: */ 27: 28: import type { AgentInputPayload } from './types'; 29: import type { BoolExpr, IntExpr, Domain, ConstraintIR } from './constraint-ir'; 30: import { buildTranslatorPeriodsByDay } from './translator-periods'; 31: 32: export type IRTypeCheckSeverity = 'hard' | 'soft'; 33: 34: export type IRTypeCheckIssue = { 35: code: 36: | 'unknown_teacher' 37: | 'unknown_class' 38: | 'unknown_subject' 39: | 'unknown_day' 40: | 'unknown_session' 41: | 'period_out_of_range' 42: | 'invalid_k' 43: | 'invalid_binding' 44: | 'invalid_int_expr' 45: | 'invalid_quantifier' 46: | 'invalid_atom_shape'; 47: message: string; 48: path: string; 49: severity: IRTypeCheckSeverity; 50: /** Optional candidates for clarification (e.g. "Không tìm thấy lớp «6A». Các lớp hiện có: 6B, 6C, 7A."). */ 51: candidates?: string[]; 52: }; 53: 54: export type IRTypeCheckResult = { 55: ok: boolean; 56: issues: IRTypeCheckIssue[]; 57: /** Convenience: hard issues that should reject the constraint. */ 58: hardIssues: IRTypeCheckIssue[]; 59: /** Convenience: soft issues that should surface a clarification question. */ 60: softIssues: IRTypeCheckIssue[]; 61: }; 62: 63: type BindingEnv = Map<string, Domain>; 64: 65: function addBinding(bindings: BindingEnv, varName: string, domain: Domain): BindingEnv { 66: const next = new Map(bindings); 67: next.set(varName.toLowerCase(), domain); 68: return next; 69: } 70: 71: function placeholderBinding(value: string | number | undefined, bindings: BindingEnv): Domain | null { 72: if (typeof value !== 'string') return null; 73: const match = /^\$\$([A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_-]*)\$\$$/u.exec(value); 74: if (!match) return null; 75: return bindings.get(match[1].toLowerCase()) ?? null; 76: } 77: 78: function bindingMatchesDomain(domain: Domain, expected: 'days' | 'periods' | 'classes' | 'teachers' | 'subjects'): boolean { 79: if (typeof domain === 'string') return domain === expected; 80: // A list/range binding is commonly used for period/day subsets. Treat it as 81: // valid for those scalar domains, but do not infer entity domains from it. 82: if ((expected === 'periods' || expected === 'days') && ('list' in domain || 'range' in domain)) return true; 83: if ('in' in domain) return bindingMatchesDomain(domain.in, expected); 84: return false; 85: } 86: 87: function collectKnownEntities(input: AgentInputPayload): { 88: teachers: Set<string>; 89: classes: Set<string>; 90: subjects: Set<string>; 91: days: Set<string>; 92: sessions: Set<string>; 93: periodByDay: Record<string, number[]>; 94: } { 95: const teachers = new Set<string>(); 96: const classes = new Set<string>(); 97: const subjects = new Set<string>(); 98: for (const a of input.assignments) { 99: teachers.add(a.teacher.label); 100: classes.add(a.class.label); 101: subjects.add(a.subject.label); 102: } 103: const days = new Set<string>(input.days.map((d) => d.id)); 104: const sessions = new Set<string>(input.sessions.map((s) => s.id)); 105: const periodByDay = buildTranslatorPeriodsByDay(input); 106: return { teachers, classes, subjects, days, sessions, periodByDay }; 107: } 108: 109: function checkEntity( 110: value: string | undefined, 111: pool: Set<string>, 112: code: 'unknown_teacher' | 'unknown_class' | 'unknown_subject', 113: path: string, 114: issues: IRTypeCheckIssue[], 115: candidates: string[], 116: bindings: BindingEnv, 117: expectedDomain: 'teachers' | 'classes' | 'subjects' 118: ): void { 119: if (value === undefined) return; 120: if (value === '__all__' || value === 'all') return; 121: const binding = placeholderBinding(value, bindings); 122: if (binding) { 123: if (bindingMatchesDomain(binding, expectedDomain)) return; 124: issues.push({ 125: code: 'invalid_binding', 126: message: `Placeholder «${value}» không thuộc domain ${expectedDomain}.`, 127: path, 128: severity: 'hard', 129: }); 130: return; 131: } 132: if (!pool.has(value)) { 133: issues.push({ 134: code, 135: message: `Không tìm thấy thực thể «${value}».`, 136: path, 137: severity: 'hard', 138: candidates: candidates.slice(0, 5), 139: }); 140: } 141: } 142: 143: function checkDay( 144: value: string | number, 145: path: string, 146: issues: IRTypeCheckIssue[], 147: known: ReturnType<typeof collectKnownEntities>, 148: bindings: BindingEnv 149: ): boolean { 150: const binding = placeholderBinding(value, bindings); 151: if (binding) { 152: if (bindingMatchesDomain(binding, 'days')) return true; 153: issues.push({ 154: code: 'invalid_binding', 155: message: `Placeholder «${value}» không thuộc domain days.`, 156: path, 157: severity: 'hard', 158: }); 159: return false; 160: } 161: if (!known.days.has(String(value))) { 162: issues.push({ 163: code: 'unknown_day', 164: message: `Không tìm thấy ngày «${value}».`, 165: path, 166: severity: 'hard', 167: candidates: [...known.days].slice(0, 5), 168: }); 169: return false; 170: } 171: return true; 172: } 173: 174: function checkPeriod( 175: period: string | number, 176: day: string | number, 177: path: string, 178: issues: IRTypeCheckIssue[], 179: known: ReturnType<typeof collectKnownEntities>, 180: bindings: BindingEnv 181: ): void { 182: const periodBinding = placeholderBinding(period, bindings); 183: if (periodBinding) { 184: if (bindingMatchesDomain(periodBinding, 'periods')) return; 185: issues.push({ 186: code: 'invalid_binding', 187: message: `Placeholder «${period}» không thuộc domain periods.`, 188: path, 189: severity: 'hard', 190: }); 191: return; 192: } 193: 194: const periodNum = Number(period); 195: if (!Number.isInteger(periodNum) || periodNum < 1) { 196: issues.push({ 197: code: 'period_out_of_range', 198: message: `Tiết không hợp lệ: ${period}`, 199: path, 200: severity: 'hard', 201: }); 202: return; 203: } 204: 205: // If the day itself is a binding placeholder, exact per-day validation is 206: // deferred until the binding is instantiated by the compiler/interpreter. 207: if (placeholderBinding(day, bindings)) return; 208: 209: const dayPeriods = known.periodByDay[String(day)] ?? []; 210: if (dayPeriods.length > 0 && !dayPeriods.includes(periodNum)) { 211: issues.push({ 212: code: 'period_out_of_range', 213: message: `Tiết ${period} ngoài phạm vi của ngày «${day}» (${dayPeriods.join(', ')}).`, 214: path, 215: severity: 'hard', 216: candidates: dayPeriods.map((p) => String(p)), 217: }); 218: } 219: } 220: 221: function checkIntExpr( 222: expr: IntExpr, 223: path: string, 224: issues: IRTypeCheckIssue[], 225: known: ReturnType<typeof collectKnownEntities>, 226: bindings: BindingEnv 227: ): void { 228: if (typeof expr === 'number') { 229: if (!Number.isInteger(expr)) { 230: issues.push({ 231: code: 'invalid_int_expr', 232: message: `Số nguyên không hợp lệ: ${expr}`, 233: path, 234: severity: 'hard', 235: }); 236: } 237: return; 238: } 239: if ('count' in expr) { 240: if (!Number.isInteger(expr.count.var.length) || expr.count.var.length === 0) { 241: issues.push({ 242: code: 'invalid_binding', 243: message: 'count.var phải là tên biến hợp lệ', 244: path: `${path}/count/var`, 245: severity: 'hard', 246: }); 247: } 248: checkDomain(expr.count.in, `${path}/count/in`, issues); 249: checkBoolExpr(expr.count.body, `${path}/count/body`, issues, known, addBinding(bindings, expr.count.var, expr.count.in)); 250: return; 251: } 252: if ('sum' in expr) { 253: for (let i = 0; i < expr.sum.length; i += 1) { 254: checkIntExpr(expr.sum[i], `${path}/sum[${i}]`, issues, known, bindings); 255: } 256: return; 257: } 258: if ('scale' in expr) { 259: if (!Number.isInteger(expr.scale.factor)) { 260: issues.push({ 261: code: 'invalid_int_expr', 262: message: `scale.factor phải là số nguyên: ${expr.scale.factor}`, 263: path: `${path}/scale/factor`, 264: severity: 'hard', 265: }); 266: } 267: checkIntExpr(expr.scale.of, `${path}/scale/of`, issues, known, bindings); 268: return; 269: } 270: issues.push({ 271: code: 'invalid_int_expr', 272: message: 'IntExpr không hợp lệ', 273: path, 274: severity: 'hard', 275: }); 276: } 277: 278: function checkDomain( 279: domain: Domain, 280: path: string, 281: issues: IRTypeCheckIssue[] 282: ): void { 283: if (typeof domain === 'string') { 284: // 'days' | 'periods' | 'classes' | 'teachers' | 'subjects' - always valid 285: return; 286: } 287: if ('list' in domain) { 288: if (!Array.isArray(domain.list) || domain.list.length === 0) { 289: issues.push({ 290: code: 'invalid_binding', 291: message: 'Domain list rỗng', 292: path, 293: severity: 'hard', 294: }); 295: } 296: return; 297: } 298: if ('range' in domain) { 299: if (!Array.isArray(domain.range) || domain.range.length !== 2) { 300: issues.push({ 301: code: 'invalid_binding', 302: message: 'Domain range phải có đúng 2 phần tử', 303: path, 304: severity: 'hard', 305: }); 306: } 307: return; 308: } 309: if ('in' in domain) { 310: checkDomain(domain.in, `${path}/in`, issues); 311: return; 312: } 313: issues.push({ 314: code: 'invalid_binding', 315: message: 'Domain không hợp lệ', 316: path, 317: severity: 'hard', 318: }); 319: } 320: 321: function checkBoolExpr( 322: expr: BoolExpr, 323: path: string, 324: issues: IRTypeCheckIssue[], 325: known: ReturnType<typeof collectKnownEntities>, 326: bindings: BindingEnv = new Map() 327: ): void { 328: if ('and' in expr) { 329: expr.and.forEach((b, i) => checkBoolExpr(b, `${path}/and[${i}]`, issues, known, bindings)); 330: return; 331: } 332: if ('or' in expr) { 333: expr.or.forEach((b, i) => checkBoolExpr(b, `${path}/or[${i}]`, issues, known, bindings)); 334: return; 335: } 336: if ('not' in expr) { 337: checkBoolExpr(expr.not, `${path}/not`, issues, known, bindings); 368: } 369: checkDomain(expr.atLeast.in, `${path}/atLeast/in`, issues); 370: checkBoolExpr(expr.atLeast.body, `${path}/atLeast/body`, issues, known, addBinding(bindings, expr.atLeast.var, expr.atLeast.in)); 381: } 382: checkDomain(expr.atMost.in, `${path}/atMost/in`, issues); 383: checkBoolExpr(expr.atMost.body, `${path}/atMost/body`, issues, known, addBinding(bindings, expr.atMost.var, expr.atMost.in)); 384: return; 385: } 386: if ('exactly' in expr) { 387: if (!Number.isInteger(expr.exactly.k) || expr.exactly.k < 0) { 388: issues.push({ 389: code: 'invalid_k', 390: message: `exactly.k phải là số nguyên không âm: ${expr.exactly.k}`, 391: path: `${path}/exactly/k`, 392: severity: 'hard', 393: }); 394: } 395: checkDomain(expr.exactly.in, `${path}/exactly/in`, issues); 396: checkBoolExpr(expr.exactly.body, `${path}/exactly/body`, issues, known, addBinding(bindings, expr.exactly.var, expr.exactly.in));
